content:
'''Actaeon''' is one of [[Lidia Cervos]]'s twin sons.

## Biography
### Early Life
Lidia Cervos was selected from the females in her family to participate in a [[Fertility Rite|fertility rite]] in the [[Aldosian Forest]]. She mated with a [[Unnamed Stag Shifter|male]] of another family, their identities remained a secret to each other.

A few weeks after the rite, Lidia discovered that she was pregnant. Although a baby from a sacred ritual was celebrated, she decided not to tell her father about it. At that point, Lidia already knew what kind of monsters she was serving, so to avoid getting her children close to [[Pollux Antonius]], [[Sandriel]], or others who served the [[Asteri]], she faked her own kidnapping and disappearance. She surrendered the twins to the Ophion rebels who took her to [[the Ocean Queen]]'s ship, ''[[the Depth Charger]]''. The Ocean Queen offered her a place to stay for a while and to bear the babies but for her children's continued protection, she had to go back to the Asteri to spy for Ophion. Lidia had to leave her sons when they were 18 months old and shortly after, they were adopted by a couple named [[Davit]] and [[Renki]].

###  
TBA

## Personality
Acteaon is described to be a bit of a genius. When he was still a child, he devised helmets and fins for his twin and himself to use so they could keep up with the [[Mer]] children, even in the deep ocean. He's been taking things apart and putting them back together into something smarter and cooler since he was a baby. When in the presence of his mother, Acteaon acts brash and cold towards her, as he thinks she left them voluntarily and seems to be hurt about it.

## Physical Description
Actaeon is said to have dark hair and golden eyes, like his mother.

## Powers & Abilities
* '''Shifter Physiology''': As Actaeon is part-[[Shifter]], he presumably has enhanced speed, strength, and regenerative abilities because of his [[Vanir]] status. He has more acute senses and can heal from injuries much faster than a [[human]].

## Relationships
TBA

## Trivia
* In Greek mythology, Actaeon is a Theban hero who is killed by Artemis when he is transformed into a stag and his own hounds are infected by Lyssa, the spirit of mad rage, frenzy, and rabies in animals.

'''Aidas''', the '''Prince of the Chasm''', is a powerful [[demon]] and one of the seven Princes of [[Hel]]. He occupies the fifth level of Hel.

## Biography
### Early Life
Not much is currently known about the demon race, particularly about the Princes, execpt that they are feared by even many of the most powerful [[Malakh|Archangels]]. At some point in the distant past, the Princes successfully kept the [[Asteri]] from conquering Hel. Afterwards, Aidas' spies kept him informed about the actions of the Asteri. Aidas hunted for the Asteri so that he and his brothers could defeat them completely. They wanted to ensure that evil wouldn't triumph on any other world again.

Aidas eventually made contact with the [[Fae]] Queen [[Theia]] on [[Midgard]]. Together they plotted against the Asteri. Aidas was madly in love with Theia and was struck hard with grief when she was murdered by [[Pelias]]. Adias' brother and fellow Prince of Hel, [[Apollion]], killed and ate Pelias in retaliation.

#### Meeting Bryce
In 15.023 [[V.E.]] Aidas made an illicit trip to Midgard through a crack in the [[Northern Rift|Rift]]. During his short visit, he came across a young [[Bryce Quinlan]] in his cat form. Bryce, then thirteen years old and having just returned from a disastrous visit to the [[The Oracle|Oracle]], was crying on a park bench outside the temple when the Prince found her. Aidas, having seemingly heard the screaming from inside the temple, was intrigued by Bryce and appeared next to her. Aidas told the young girl to stop crying, saying it would only satisfy those who wronged her. He then sat with her until she stopped crying and felt brave enough to return to her father, though he disappeared before she could thank him.

###  
Twelve years after their encounter outside the temple, Bryce used obsidian salt to illegally summon Aidas in her [[Bryce's Apartment|apartment]] in the hopes that he would have some information about [[Danika Fendyr|Danika]]'s death and who was using the [[kristallos]] demon to commit murders in [[Lunathion]].

Aidas remembered Bryce from their first meeting but was unable to provide much assistance, beyond advising her and [[Hunt Athalar|Hunt]] to run tests on the first murders again. Noticing that his power was slowly freezing the room-and Bryce-Aidas left, telling Bryce to make the [[Drop]] and find him afterward.

Later, Aidas and [[Jesiba Roga|Jesiba]] are shown discussing Bryce's powers and how she holds Theia's light.

###  
Bryce and Ruhn return to her apartment to find [[Aidas|Prince Aidas]] waiting there in cat form. Aidas asks Bryce why she hasn’t mastered her powers yet and continues to be cryptic about his intentions with Bryce. He also tells Bryce that her star power comes from the ancient Fae queen [[Theia]] and that Hel’s armies are waiting for Bryce. Bryce and the gang are confused because Hel has always been their enemy. Aidas tells them that isn’t true and to think about how The Asteri have been the ones to write, and often re-write, history. Aidas explains that Bryce’s starlight comes from Theia, and Ruhn’s comes from [[Pelias|Prince Pelias]]. Aidas explains that Pelias forcibly wed Theia’s daughter, [[Helena]], and commandeered the throne for himself after Theia died. The Starsword is supposed to belong to Theia’s female heir. Aidas says that this brewing war is the same war they fought 15,000 years ago and the same war Hunt fought 200+ years ago. A war against the Asteri. He urges Bryce to get ready. She responds with something along the lines of “How? By aligning with Ophion rebels?” Aidas essentially states that if that is how she must be ready for the war, then yes. She says that she doesn’t even know any rebels. Aidas begins to disappear and says, “There’s one right behind you…Hello, Agent Silverbow.”

Later on Apollion visits Bryce in her dreams. He tells her that Aidas loved Queen Theia and that the fae and forces of Hel united against the Asteri in the war. He also says that it is easier to communicate with Hunt because he was “bred” to interact with those from Hel.

###  
TBA

## Personality
TBA

## Physical Description


Aidas is implied to be able to change his appearance. When he first appears to Bryce following her traumatic encounter with the Oracle he takes the shape of a white cat with unnaturally blue eyes; due to being bound by the laws that trap demons in animal form if they enter Midgard through cracks in the Rift he was stuck in this form during their meeting, but was able to talk.

When Bryce summons him twelve years later, Aidas appears as a beautiful young man with blond hair that falls to his shoulders in soft waves, a fine boned face, and eyes like blue opals. His mouth is described as full and sensuous, and his teeth as unnaturally white. In this form, he wears a closely tailored black suit.
## Powers and Abilities
Despite a general, unnamed, dark power Aidas also displays instances of the following:

* '''Cryokinesis''': When Bryce summons Aidas to question him about the murders she's investigating, his presence severely lowers the temperature in the room. The effect worsens the longer he remains summoned, to the point where Hunt began to worry that the cold will actually kill him and Bryce.
*'''Shapeshifting''': Like the other Princes of Hel, Aidas has the ability to shapeshift between forms when crossing through rifts from Hel to Midgard, favoring the form of a white cat. In this form, he is capable of verbal communication.

'''Amelie Ravenscroft''' is a Wolf [[Shifter]] and an underling of [[Sabine Fendyr]]. She is also the Alpha of the [[Black Rose Wolf Pack]].

## Biography
## ==
Sometime after [[Danika Fendyr]]'s death, Amelie became the Alpha of the Black Rose Wolf Pack, helping provide security to [[the Wolves Den]] in [[Moonwood]].

When [[the White Raven]] is destroyed from a bomb, Sabine sends Amelie to head the [[Auxiliary|Aux]] packs hauling away debris and helping the wounded. She notices [[Ruhn Danaan]] at the scene.

When [[Bryce Quinlan]] and [[Hunt Athalar]] pay a visit to Moonwood to talk to the [[Fendyr Prime|Prime]] about Danika's movements before her death, they encounter Amelie and her Pack stationed at the front gate. The Alpha takes pleasure in refusing them entry until Hunt mentions they're working for the Governor, at which point she reluctantly radios through for [[Ithan Holstrom]] to escort them inside. Amelie and Bryce trade barbed insults all throughout their encounter, with Amelie mostly commenting on Bryce's party girl lifestyle and her sexual promiscuity. She grows angry when Bryce tells her that she isn't half as good as Danika was, and continues to spew vitriol at Bryce until Ithan arrives.

Amelie later accompanies Sabine to act as security when Sabine pays a visit to Bryce at her [[Bryce's Apartment|apartment]], staying out of sight with her phone ready to call in help if Bryce and Hunt get out of hand.

When Bryce buys a box of chocolate croissants a few days later to commemorate Danika's birthday, Amelie arranges to have the word "trash" spelled out in icing on one of them. Unbeknownst to her, Hunt happens to be with Bryce when she opens the box and notices her reaction. He is enraged on her behalf and immediately flies off to confront Amelie, whom he finds drinking with her pack in a bar in Moonwood. Hunt easily overpowers her and shoves her against a wall, demanding that she leave Bryce alone. Outmatched and in discomfort from Hunt's lightning [[magic]], Amelie reluctantly agrees and he leaves.

Amelie reports the incident to Sabine, who takes the opportunity to get back at Hunt and Bryce for accusing her of murdering her daughter. She takes Amelie with her to [[the Comitium]] to complain to [[Micah Domitus|Micah]] about Hunt's behavior; Amelie stands by while Hunt and Bryce are summoned, and watches quietly while Sabine feigns outrage at her treatment by one of Micah's [[Malakh|angels]] and demands he be punished. As a slave who has attacked a citizen of [[Lunathion]] Hunt's life is forfeit.

Upon realizing the implications of her complaint against Hunt, Amelie attempts to get Sabine to let the issue go, but is duly ignored and watches in horror as his wings are cut off by Micah as punishment. Sabine leaves, smug with her small victory while Bryce kneels by Hunt's side, the angel having lost consciousness from the pain. On her way out a sombre Amelie attempts to apologize to Bryce for what has happened, but Bryce spits at her and Amelie leaves visibly upset.

## ==
TBA

## Physical Description
Amelie has long dark hair and gold eyes, and a sharp-angled face. As a member of the Black Rose Pack she also has her pack's symbol tattooed on to her neck: an onyx rose with three claw marks slashed through it.

## Powers & Abilities
### Powers
* '''Shifter Physiology''': Amelie has enhanced speed, strength, and regenerative abilities because of her [[Vanir]] status. She has more acute senses and can heal from injuries much faster than a [[human]]. 
* '''Shifting''': As a shifter Amelie possesses the ability to transform into a magically enhanced animal form. Her animal form is that of a larger than average wolf. In this form she is stronger, faster, and has keener senses than in human form. She can effortlessly change between her human and animal forms and can communicate well while in her wolf form.
* '''Immortality''': Having made the [[Drop]], Amelie is immortal which not only grants her a slowed and expanded lifespan but allows her to survive injuries that not even her accelerated healing would be able to regenerate from.

### Abilities
* '''Combat Skills''': Amelie has been trained in various forms of combat and self-defense to an exceedingly high level.

## Relationships
### [[Connor Holstrom]]
Amelie has had a crush on Connor since they were children, though he never seemed to return her affections. When he asked Bryce Quinlan out Amelie finally realized he never would. After Connor's death, and the leaking of Bryce's texting history, Amelie developed a hatred for Bryce for both hooking up with a stranger on the night of Connor's death and believing she stole him from her.

'''Apollion''', known as the '''Prince of the Pit''' or the '''Star-Eater''', is a powerful [[demon]] and one of the seven Princes of [[Hel]]. He occupies the seventh and deepest level of Hel.

## Biography
The Star Eater's name derives from killing the Asteri [[Sirius]] and eating her body afterward. Since that act, his true name hasn't been uttered in [[Midgard]].

During the final battle of the First Wars, Prince [[Pelias]] and the Prince of the Pit faced each other. The two of them fought for around three days until the Star-Eater struck the fatal blow. But not before Pelias was able to summon all the [[Luna's Horn|Horn]]'s strength, and banished the Prince of the Pit, his brethren, and their armies back to Hel. He sealed the Northern Rift forever—so only small cracks in it or summonings with salt can bring them over now. Pelias wielded the Horn until he died.

## Personality
Apollion is described to be micro-managing by [[Bryce Quinlan]] after he visits her in a dream. He also has love for his brother, [[Aidas]], and likely by extension, the rest of his brothers as well. 

## Physical Description
Like the other Princes of Hel, Apollion has the ability to change his appearance, not only physically but also in his mental projections. He first appears in [[Hunt Athalar]]'s dreams, and while Hunt doesn't see him, Hunt describes Apollion possessing leathery wings that "whispered" with his "soft, deep" voice. However, when he appears to Bryce in her dreams, he appears as a golden-haired, golden-skinned male with black eyes Bryce likened to an unending darkness, and a handsome appearance similar to that of statues. Bryce described his movements as being more "animalistic" than humanoid. 

## Powers and Abilities
Apollion has the power of a void, which he used to devour the heart of Sirius. He is also able to transform into a beast of "darkness and decay" with leathery wings and rows of sharp teeth. Apollion has a black hole in his mouth, and like Hunt, has the power of Hellfire which is strong enough to kill an Asteri. 

## Trivia
*  Apollion is also the first being to kill an [[Asteri]].
*  He is regarded as the mightiest of the seven demon princes.

TBA

Ariadne is a short, curvy female with her waist-length hair, the color of darkest iron, falling in waves around her delicately featured face. Her tan body shimmers with ripples of what seems like red-and-gold scales flowing beneath her skin. She has a luscious form and eyes a bloodred, burning crimson, glowing like embers.

## Powers & Abilities
* '''Firstlight and Secondlight Manipulation''': Like all the Asteri, Austrus could manipulate the power of [[firstlight]] and [[secondlight]] to achieve God-like feats. This resulted in the people of Midgard believing that the Asteri housed the power of a star within themselves.
* '''Energy Absorption''': Like all the Asteri, Austrus consumed firstlight and secondlight to increase his power to that of a god. However, it was alluded to that this power was not infinite and without regular consumption, his powers would have weakened over time.

'''Azriel''' is from the [[w:c:acourtofthornsandroses:Prythian|Fae Homeworld]], whom [[Bryce Quinlan]] meets when she escapes [[Midgard]].

## Biography
:''This character is part of the [[w:c:acourtofthornsandroses:A Court of Thorns and Roses series|A Court of Thorns and Roses series]]. To view this character's full biography, please proceed to [[w:c:acourtofthornsandroses:Azriel|his page]] in the [[w:c:acourtofthornsandroses:A Court of Thorns and Roses Wiki|A Court of Thorns and Roses Wiki]].''

## ==
After Bryce Quinlan manages to escape the [[Asteri]] by opening a Gate to travel through the worlds to reach [[Hel]] in order to request aid from the Princes, she accidentally lands somewhere in Velaris near the Sidra River instead, carrying with her the [[Gwydion|Starsword]]. Azriel finds her, surprising her from behind presses a knife to her throat, and speaks to her but she doesn't recognize the language, as her powers are spent she cannot attack him with them. As he twists her to face him she mistakes him for a demon due to his wings and the fact that she believes herself to be in Hel. She repeatedly asks him to take her to Prince [[Aidas]] but he cannot understand her and vice versa. Azriel picks up her sword and pulls a scrap of black cloth, a blindfold, that he carries in a hidden pocket of his armor. He blindfolds her, carries her and flies to a residence.

When they go in, she trips, so he carries her over his shoulders all the way to the living room and sits her in a comfortable chair. Then, Bryce panics and tears the blindfold. A moment later, the doors to the foyer open and Amren, Cassian and [[Nesta Archeron|Nesta]] enter the room. Bryce sees Amren first, then has the idea to try to communicate with them in the only other language she knew, the ancient language of the [[Fae]], of the [[Starborn]]. Amren stagers back, surprised to hear her speak the language, as no one has spoken it in their world for fifteen thousand years, and the others gape. Amren notices the Starsword and nods to Azriel's knife, [[Truth-Teller]], at his side. The knife is the twin to the sword as it glimmers with dark light in answer to the sword's white light. Amren whispers that the Starsword is actually Gwydion.

Bryce tells Amren she needs to find Prince Aidas or [[Apollion]]. She replies saying she doesn't know them and that their world is not Hel. At this moment, the front door opens again, Feyre enters the room, with [[Rhysand]] entering after her. Bryce pleads her case to Amren and she translates to the others. Feyre then asks Bryce for her name, and Amren translates the question. Bryce addresses Rhysand and Feyre, noticing they possess an air of authority, and tells them her name. Rhysand steps forward and introduces himself in the Old Language.

'''Baxian Argos''', better known as '''The Helhound''', was part of [[Sandriel's Triarii]] before starting to work for [[Celestina]].

## Biography
### Early Life
Baxian grew up in one of the poorer parts of [[Ravilis]]. With his mother a Helhound [[shifter]] and his father an [[Malakh|angel]], it made life hard. They were neither well-accepted by the [[House of Earth and Blood]] nor the [[House of Sky and Breath]]. It even made it hard for his parents to keep their jobs.

###  
Baxian is seen to have helped Bryce on more than one occasion much to Hunt's distrust.

TBA

###  
TBA

## Personality
TBA

## Physical Description
Baxian has dark closely buzzed hair, obsidian eyes, and brown skin. He is tall and muscular with jet-black wings. Baxian has a scar snaking down his neck caused by [[Hunt Athalar]]. Across his heart he wears a tattoo reading ''Through love, all is possible'', inked in [[Danika Fendyr|Danika]]'s handwriting. It gets shredded during his torture in the dungeons of the [[Crystal Palace]].

## Powers & Abilities
### Powers
*'''Malakim Physiology''': As an angel, Baxian has enhanced physical abilities and senses that make him far stronger than both [[humans]] and [[Vanir]]. He also possesses regenerative abilities that are far superior to humans and Vanir. 
* '''Flight''': Through his Malakim wings, Baxian can fly at high speeds. He is also capable of carrying people in his arms while flying. His wings also act like a shield to protect him from some attacks.
*'''Immortality''': Having made the [[Drop]], Baxian is immortal, which not only grants him a slowed and expanded lifespan, but allows him to survive injuries that not even his accelerated healing would be able to regenerate from.
* '''Shifting''': As part-shifter, Baxian possesses the ability to transform into a magically enhanced animal form. His animal form is that of a helhound. In this form he is stronger, faster, and has keener senses than a regular human.

### Abilites
* '''Combat Skills''': Baxian has been trained in various forms of combat and self-defense to an exceedingly high level. When it comes to weapons, it seems he favors a sword.

## Relationships
### [[Danika Fendyr]]
Unknown to most, Baxian and Danika were [[Mate|mates]]. Baxian tells Bryce and Hunt in the catacombs that Danika was his mate by showing them his tattoo in Danikas handwriting '''Through love, all is possible'.''

=== [[Hunt Athalar]] ===
Hunt and Baxian were enemies for a very long-time, having both served in Sandriel's Triari, and Hunt being responsible for the scar he gave to the Hellhound. Although Baxian hadn't done anything personal to Hunt, Hunt's animosity towards him was still prevalent when he joined the 33rd Legion. When Hunt was made to chaperone the Hellhound, Hunt still made his distaste towards Baxian known at any chance he got, sighting him as a nuisance more than anything. And while there were moments where he tolerated his presence (such as playing video games with each other), Hunt didn't trust Baxian and believed for a while he would sell  him and his friends out during their search for Sofie. Upon both their capture in the Asteri dungeons, Hunt  and Baxian, for their own survival, were forced to work together, and eventually considered each other as allies.

== Trivia ==
* In Chinese mythology, Baxian is a group of holy Daoists who earn the right to immortality. They are frequently referred to as the Eight Immortals.
* Argos is, inter alia, a city in Peloponnese, Greece.In Homer's ''Odyssey'', '''Argos''' (/ˈɑːrɡɒs, -ɡəs/; Ancient Greek: Ἄργος), sometimes referred to as '''Argus''', is the legendary faithful dog of Odysseus. Bred to be a hunting dog before Odysseus leaves for the Trojan War. https://en.m.wikipedia.org/wiki/Argos_(dog)

'''Brannon''', better known as '''Brann''', is one of [[Lidia Cervos]]'s twin sons.

## Biography
### Early Life
Lidia Cervos was selected from the females in her family to participate in a [[Fertility Rite|fertility rite]] in the [[Aldosian Forest]]. She mated with a [[Unnamed Stag Shifter|male]] of another family, their identities remained a secret to each other.

A few weeks after the rite, Lidia discovered that she was pregnant. Although a baby from a sacred ritual was celebrated, she decided not to tell her father about it. At that point, Lidia already knew what kind of monsters she was serving, so to avoid getting her children close to [[Pollux Antonius]], [[Sandriel]], or others who served the [[Asteri]], she faked her own kidnapping and disappearance. She surrendered to the [[Ophion]] rebels who took her to the [[The Ocean Queen|Ocean Queen]]'s ship, ''[[the Depth Charger]]''. The Ocean Queen offered her a place to stay for a while and to bear the babies but for her children's continued protection, she had to go back to the Asteri to spy for Ophion. Lidia had to leave her sons when they were 18 months old and shortly after, they were adopted by a couple named [[Davit]] and [[Renki]].

###  
TBA

## Personality
Brann is described to be a "what-you-see-is-what-you-get" kind of guy. He is a natural athlete, fearless, quick to anger, and quick to laugh. He is usually doing okay in school, but at the same time, he's more interested in hanging out with his buddies. He is the stereotypical jock.

## Physical Description
Brann is said to have golden blonde hair like his mother, and blue eyes.

## Powers & Abilities
* '''Shifter Physiology''': As Brann is part-[[Shifter]], he presumably has enhanced speed, strength, and regenerative abilities because of his [[Vanir]] status. He has more acute senses and can heal from injuries much faster than a [[human]].

'''Bronson''' was a Wolf [[shifter]] and part of [[the Pack of Devils]].

## Biography
###  
Bronson, along with the rest of the Pack of Devils, is at [[Danika and Bryce's Apartment|Danika and Bryce's apartment]] after going to [[Ithan Holstrom]]'s [[sunball]] game. The pack is watching a legal procedural, much to Bronson's annoyance.

[[Micah Domitus]] hatched a plan to get revenge on [[Danika Fendyr|Danika]] and to try and find the [[Luna's Horn|Horn]]. He waited until [[Philip Briggs]], a [[human]] rebel, was released from prison so he could frame him for the pack's deaths. He went to Danika and Bryce's apartment where the Pack of Devils, including Bronson, were at the time and used his [[magic]] to disable them. He then injected Danika with [[Synth]], which caused Danika to rip her pack apart before turning on herself. Micah then summoned a [[Kristallos]] [[demon]] to cover his tracks. Bronson and [[Zelda]]'s bodies were found by the apartment door.

Philip Briggs was then accused of their murders due to him being imprisoned by Danika previously, leading the authorities to believe he did it for revenge. However, Micah admitted to [[Bryce Quinlan|Bryce]] that he was the one to kill Bronson and the pack.

## Personality
His movie tastes skewed more toward art house flicks and documentaries. Unsurprisingly, he was never allowed to select the entertainment for Pack Night.

## Physical Description
Bronson had brown eyes and was a massive male.

## Powers & Abilities
### Powers
* '''Shifter Physiology''': Bronson had enhanced speed, strength, and regenerative abilities because of his [[Vanir]] status. He had more acute senses and could heal from injuries much faster than a human. He could effortlessly change between his human and animal forms.
* '''Shifting''': Bronson possessed the ability to transform into a magically enhanced animal form. His animal form was that of a larger than average wolf. In this form he was stronger, faster, and had keener senses than in his human form.
* '''Immortality''': Having made the [[Drop]], Bronson was immortal which not only granted him a slowed and expanded lifespan but allows him to survive injuries that not even his accelerated healing would be able to regenerate from.

### Abilities
* '''Combat Skills''': Bronson had been trained in various forms of combat and self-defense to an exceedingly high level. 

## Trivia
* Bronson's drink of choice was mead, and he liked cured meats and stinky cheeses on his pizza.
* Bronson liked to smoke [[mirthroot]].

'''Bryce Adelaide Quinlan''', is a half-[[Fae]], half-[[human]] who is the Heir to the [[Starborn]] Fae. She is the bastard daughter of [[Ember Quinlan]] and [[Einar Danaan]].

## Biography
### Early Life
Bryce was born in the year [[Timeline|15,010]] [[V.E.]] to Ember Quinlan, a 19-year-old human woman who had been seduced by Einar Danaan. Bryce was raised by Ember and her stepfather [[Randall Silago]], who taught her self-defense and how to handle weapons, especially guns. Bryce's mother was very protective of her and did not want Bryce to move from her hometown, [[Nidaros]], to [[Lunathion]].

Growing up, Bryce was somewhat of an outcast amongst her peers. This was largely due to her Fae heritage, considering Nidaros is mostly populated by humans. At age five, she managed to collect [[Jelly Jubilee]], a highly coveted toy from ''The Starlight Fancy'' franchise which were the number one toys on every girl's Winter Solstice wish list. Ember left Nidaros for the first time in years to buy Jelly Jubilee from one of the big towns two hours away. This served as a means for Bryce to finally connect with other kids, she showed them the toy at school and was instantly accepted. The toy is of significant emotional value to Bryce; Jelly Jubilee's half-[[pegasus]], half-unicorn heritage served as a self-esteem boost to Bryce. Although the toy differed greatly from the other horses in the franchise, it was still the most valuable.

Twelve years ago, when Bryce was thirteen, Ember had a sudden change of heart about her meeting her father and got in contact with him. Documents showed up a few weeks later granting Bryce [[civitas]] status in exchange for her declaring allegiance to the [[House of Sky and Breath]]. Bryce was hesitant, but Ember encouraged her to do it.

Bryce soon visited her father for the first time at his [[The Autumn King's Villa|villa]] in [[Five Roses]]. Einar insisted the visit be discreet. This was also the first time she met her half-brother [[Ruhn Danaan]], and they quickly became close. Her visit to her father, however, was less successful and her hopes of having a relationship with him were dashed by his vindictive personality and behavior towards her. The Autumn King refused to acknowledge her until her traditional visit to [[the Oracle]]. When looking into Bryce's future, the Oracle began screaming and clawing at its eyes. The Oracle is said to have been blind for a week after. This unmitigated disaster left her in tears and ended with her father kicking her out of his villa to make her own way home.

Having just returned from a disastrous visit to the Oracle, Bryce was crying on a park bench outside the temple when a cat found her, who turned out to be [[Aidas]], the Prince of the [[Chasm]] of [[Hel]], having seemingly heard the screaming from inside the temple, was intrigued by Bryce and appeared next to her. Aidas told the young girl to stop crying, saying it would only satisfy those who wronged her. He then sat with her until she stopped crying and felt brave enough to return to her father, though he disappeared before she could thank him.

Ten years ago, Bryce had the blood test done that confirmed she'll have an immortal lifespan upon making the [[Drop]], and her power rating was near-negligible.

The Autumn King later offered Bryce a monstrous amount of gold marks in exchange for Bryce to keep her eternal silence about him being her father. Bryce rejected the money, but still kept quiet about her parentage. Despite the falling out with the Autumn King, Bryce maintained a relationship with Ruhn until she was sixteen, at which point they had a fight after Ruhn insulted her. Bryce stopped speaking to Ruhn afterward, ignoring his various attempts to apologize.

When she was older Bryce attended [[Crescent City University]], where she met [[Danika Fendyr]]. She was a classical literature major. The two quickly became close friends. They remained friends throughout college and after they graduated. During their senior year, Danika uncovered an illegal animal smuggling ring run by asp [[Shifter|shifters]]; plagued by guilt at the thought of the animals in cages Danika and Bryce snuck into their hideout to free the animals and were caught, resulting in them being chased and injured by the shifters. Cornered and about to be killed, Bryce told Danika to close her eyes and killed some of the shifters, while the others were killed by police.

After college Bryce and Danika moved in together. Bryce got a job working at [[Griffin Antiquities]] for the feared [[Jesiba Roga]] while Danika went on to become Alpha of [[the Pack of Devils]]. On her fist day of work, Jesiba gifted Bryce an [[Archesian Amulet]] for her protection.

###  
Bryce Quinlan works as an assistant at Griffin Antiquities, and lives in an [[Danika and Bryce's Apartment|apartment]] together with her best friend Danika, granddaughter to the Werewolf [[Fendyr Prime|Prime]] and alpha to the Pack of Devils. They recently got the news that [[Philip Briggs]], who was caught by Danika’s pack planning to blow up a nightclub to spread the war between humans and [[Vanir]], has been released from jail. Danika is going to meet up with [[Micah Domitus]], the Governer of Lunathion and wider [[Valbara]] to complain about Briggs's release. Meanwhile, Jesiba has tasked Bryce with locating [[Luna's Horn]], an ancient Fae relic that was stolen during a recent power outage from its [[Luna's Temple|temple]]. 

Because of Briggs's release, Danika and the pack spent the evening at Danika and Bryce's apartment. Bryce leaves for a date with [[Reid Redner]]. On her way, [[Connor Holstrom]], a member of the pack, interrupts her and begs her to let him take her on a date. However, Bryce refuses and makes her way to the date. Because Reid is late, and barely pays attention, always being on his phone, Bryce dumps him and texts Connor that she'd go out with him. After leaving the place where she was with Reid, Bryce meets [[Fury Axtar]] and [[Juniper Andromeda]] so that they can go clubbing, taking drugs, and drinking heavily as she texts Danika.

However, once she comes home, Bryce finds her door smashed and Danika and the pack dead, cut into pieces. Seeing a [[demon]] escaping, she chases after it to understand what has happened and finds out that it was feasting on an [[Malakh|angel]]. Bryce hits the demon, however, it gouges her leg and runs away. Bryce later stays to keep the angel alive. Bryce is taken to a holding cell, watched by over two angels, [[Isaiah Tiberian]] and [[Hunt Athalar]]. Both of them took part in a rebellion, that later resulted in them being proclaimed [[The Fallen|Fallen]]. Ruhn, Bryce's half-brother, arrives and demands that they let Bryce go and Isaiah has no choice but to release her.

## Personality
Bryce is a very headstrong, sarcastic girl. She does a lot of things with different intentions, and can secretly do a lot while putting on the facade of a "stupid party girl." She doesn't care about rules and is willing to break the law to get things done. However, she is hated by many people, leading to a hidden depressive side. She is also deadlier than she lets on, being raised by a legendary sharpshooter. Bryce doesn't like to be coddled. [[Rhysand]] describes her as "softhearted yet ruthless, scheming yet impulsive, determined and stubborn, and with a dangerous tendency toward recklessness." Ember confirms she has been like that since she was a baby. Bryce is resourceful, wild, and willful. She has always prided herself on resenting any and all Fae. The outside world sees Bryce as reckless and unchecked, but she’s been the master of her fate for most of her life. Once she’s set her sights on what she wants, she doesn’t let anything get in her way. According to Ruhn, Bryce gets a thrill not from the sheer force of her power, but in the control over herself and her impulses. She lets everyone think the worst of her after Danika’s murder. She refused to go to therapy or do anything else to help herself heal from the trauma of Danika’s murder. She knew it would help, but she doesn’t feel she deserves to be helped or to feel better. She is plagued with survivor’s guilt.

## Physical Description
Bryce has golden-dusted and freckled skin, long wine-red hair, amber-colored eyes, full lips, high cheekbones, and slightly arched ears, thanks to her Fae heritage. She has a curvy figure and is said to be busty and attractive. She has a tattoo down her back, written in an [[w:c:acourtofthornsandroses:Leshon Hakodesh|unknown alphabet]], saying ''Through love, all is possible'', which she got together with Danika. She furthermore has a white scar in the shape of an eight-pointed star between her breasts, which she got when she used her Starborn gifts to close the [[Gates]] in Lunathion. This scar illuminates with her Starlight.

## Powers & Abilities
### Powers
While initially having negligible power levels, Bryce performs the Drop with Danika's spirit as an [[Anchor]] while they are connected to [[the Old Square]] Gate and [[the Dead Gate]] respectfully. Bryce then receives power from the seven Gates of Lunathion which held droplets of power from every creature who ever used them and is revealed to have more power than her father, the King of the Valbaran Fae. She is also the strongest Starborn Fae anyone has seen in centuries. 

It is later revealed that Bryce can absorb "certain kinds of [[magic]], forms of pure energy." So far she has only done this with Hunt's lightning and [[Hypaxia Enador]]'s healing magic. [[Declan Emmet]] states that Bryce is "literally a Gate," she can take in power and offer it. However unlike the Gates which can store power indefinitely hers "clearly peters out after a while."

* '''Fae Physiology''': As a Half-Fae, Bryce has enhanced senses and physical abilities, including a sense of smell much stronger than that of humans which she frequently uses to identify the types of Vanir she encounters in Lunathion. Her Fae attributes are not as strong as that of a full-blooded Fae, however.
* '''Starlight''': Bryce is revealed to be a Starborn and to have the ability to wield starlight. A light of a pure star - it has no other power other than the light itself and to blind people. Bryce used it to shield Old Square Gate and stop demons from entering the city. She removed a star wholly from within and blasted its light into the Gate's clear stones and with the Horn repaired she channeled her light and sealed [[the Heart Gate]] portal. According to Prince Aidas Bryce has the same starlight as the Starborn Queen [[Theia]].
* '''Starfire:''' Laser wielding
* '''Teleportation''': Bryce is able to teleport from place to place when charged up with some kind of energy - usually Hunt's lightning. Since she is still learning to work with that power, her teleportation is limited and not as strong as that of [[Cormac Donnall]]. 
* '''Immortality''': Having made the Drop, Bryce is immortal which not only grants her a slowed and expanded lifespan but allows her to survive injuries that not even her accelerated healing would be able to regenerate from.

### Abilities
* '''Combat''': Bryce has been trained in various forms of combat and self-defense by her stepfather Randall, to keep her safe in Lunathion.
* '''Sharpshooting''': Bryce is an expert marksman, having been trained by her step-father Randall.
* '''Dancing''': Before Danika's death, Bryce was an avid dancer, despite discouragement from local studios, saying she had the "wrong body type." After her saving of Lunathion, Bryce picked up dancing classes again.

## Relationships
### [[Connor Holstrom]]
Bryce and Connor met at university, through Danika. Connor had feelings for Bryce for years, but Danika told him he wasn’t allowed to make a move in until Bryce was ready. After five years, Connor let her know how serious he was for her. Bryce broke up with her boyfriend, Reid Redner, and decided to give him a chance. However, he was murdered before they got the chance to go on the pizza date they had planned.

### [[Hunt Athalar]]
During Bryce's investigation into Danika's death on behalf of Micah, Hunt grew close to Bryce and began a relationship with her. They have a classic enemies-to-lovers relationship: at first, they only tolerated each other due to direct orders from the Archangel Micah, but as the investigation matures, they start to form a tight friendship. They are engaged in a battle of wills. At first, Bryce finds Hunts to be "full of demeaning, condescending swagger." She repeatedly refers to him as an "alphahole." She thinks he "has a stick up his ass." Hunt, for his part, wants to chuck her into the [[Istros River]] and thinks she's a brat and shallow party girl. Bryce stated that she knew Hunt was her friend ever since he pulled out Jelly Jubilee. Their romantic relationship develops, and when Hunt was on his deathbed, Bryce whispered "I love you" to him before making the Drop. Hunt feels an unconditional and unending love for Bryce. Bryce feels that Hunt's strength "compliments her own, bolstering it and helping it thrive, not overpowering hers."

### [[Ruhn Danaan]]
When Ruhn learned of Bryce's existence, he was intrigued at the idea of having a half-sister after more than sixty years of being an only child. Ruhn is fifty years older than Bryce. Their father's treatment toward thirteen-year-old Bryce opened Ruhn's eyes to the Autumn King's true nature. Over the next three years Bryce and Ruhn had been best friends, united in their hatred of their father. Ruhn would frequently visit Bryce at her parents' home. She'd been the brightest spot in his life. But that ended when Bryce was sixteen. They had a big argument where Ruhn insulted her. Bryce stopped speaking to Ruhn afterward, ignoring his various attempts to apologize. Their relationship became a bitter one, with Bryce and Ruhn blaming him for their falling out. Ruhn thought she always did whatever she wanted without considering others, while Bryce found him to be a "domineering dick" whenever she ran into him. She avoided him whenever possible. However, Ruhn still watched over Bryce and made sure she was safe. They later fixed their relationship, with Bryce forgiving him. Bryce tells Ruhn at his wedding that he "deserves to be happy more than anyone she knows."

### [[Tristan Flynn]]
Though Bryce never hooked up with Tristan, she had a crush on him when she was younger. When she used to visit Ruhn as a teenager, she was barely able to function in both Flynn’s and Declan’s presence. For a while, Bryce had a fanatic conviction that Flynn would be her husband one day. She wrote ''Lady Bryce Flynn'' on all her school notebooks for two years straight. Years later, when she was already in college, she ran into Flynn again—this time, Danika went home with him because Bryce hadn’t wanted to mess up anything between him and Ruhn.

'''Celestina''' is an [[Malakh|Archangel]] and the current Governor of [[Lunathion]].

## Biography
### Early Life
Celestina has stayed out of the spotlight, unlike most other Archangels. Before being appointed to the role of governor of Lunathion, she was governor of [[Nena]], a territory with a population of around fifty. She only has one legion under her command, and no [[triarii]]. Her legion acts as watchdogs for the [[Northern Rift]] and is controlled by the [[Asteri]]. Celestina served as merely a figurehead.

###  
With the Archangel [[Micah Domitus|Micah]] gone, Crescent City needs a new Archangel to lead. Hunt and his angel friends anticipate the arrival of the Archangel, [[Celestina]], and are unsure what to expect. Unfortunately, with [[Sandriel's Triarii|Sandriel’s]] death, The Asteri decided it would be best if her [[triarii]] split between Celestina and another Archangel, [[Ephraim]]. Because of this, The Hind (Lidia), [[Pollux Antonius|The Hammer (Pollux)]], [[Baxian Argos|The Hellhound (Baxian)]], and [[the Harpy]] form Celestina’s new triarii. Hunt and some of the other angels have a horrible history with this group. Hunt abhors The Hind and The Hammer especially, and suffered much torture at Sandriel’s command. While tensions are high between Hunt and the new triarii, Celestina seems kind and like she has good intentions. Hunt still does not trust her, though.

Later, the angels are called to assemble by Celestina. Pollux and Baxian got into a public altercation. Baxian attacked Pollux because Pollux was sexually harassing a woman. The brawl caused a scene and made it look like Celestina did not have a good grip on her triarii. Because of this, the Asteri want a happy distraction (and also to keep a tight leash on Celestina). They force Celestina to mate with Ephraim, which she accepts as her duty.

Celestina and Hunt talk privately. She tells him that she wants to look out for him because Shahar was her close friend. She confesses that she tried to buy him out of slavery numerous times but was denied every time. Hunt says that he wants to look out for her as well.

In Part II, Celestina and Ephraim host a celebration party to toast to their relationship. Celestina specifically asks Hunt to be there for support. Meanwhile, Bryce is breaking down what happened with the Under King to Ithan and the rest of the gang. Ithan is distraught because he doesn’t want his brother’s soul to be fodder. Their conversation is interrupted when Sabine enters the apartment and attacks Bryce because she wants her to stay out of wolf business, and hosting Ithan is staying involved. Bryce manages to shoot a text to Hunt, and he has to leave the celebration, which disappoints Celestina. Baxian follows Hunt. They manage to diffuse the tension and kick Sabine out. As punishment for leaving her celebration party early, Celestina orders Hunt to stay in the barracks for two weeks.

Bryce uses her title as princess to get a meeting with Celestina. She asks Celestina to please allow Hunt to leave the barracks so that they can go to the island. She tells Celestina that she needs Hunt to have a night away so that Hunt can meet her parents. Celestina understands. Because Bryce has been using her princess title around town, Fae law dictates that she officially accepts her title. This means the Autumn King has more control over her now.

Later, Bryce and Hunt break into a coat closet to fool around. Someone had the same idea. Turns out, Hypaxia and Celestina are in a secret relationship.

## Personality
Celestina is described to be polite, elegant, and friendly. She is unusually nice for an Archangel.

## Physical Description
Celestina has skin as dark as onyx, light brown eyes, tightly curling black hair, and gleaming white wings. She is described as voluptuous and full-bodied. She often wears silver jewelry at her wrists and neck. 

## Powers & Abilities
* '''Malakim Physiology''': As an angel, Celestina has enhanced physical abilities and senses that make her far stronger than both [[humans]] and [[Vanir]]. She also possesses regenerative abilities that are far superior to humans and Vanir. As an Archangel, Celestina has stronger powers and abilities than other Malakim.
* '''Flight''': Through her Malakim wings, Celestine can fly at high speeds. She is also capable of carrying people in her arms while she flies. Her wings also act like a shield to protect her from some attacks.
* '''Immortality:''' Having made the [[Drop]], Celestina is immortal which not only grants her a slowed and expanded lifespan but allows her to survive injuries that not even her accelerated healing would be able to regenerate from.
* '''Raw Power''': Like all Archangels, Celestina possesses a form of raw power.

## Relationships
### [[Ephraim]]
The Asteri have declared that she is to mate with Ephraim. She is not romantically attracted to him, but does consider him a friend.

### [[Hypaxia Enador]]
Hypaxia and Celestina have a secret love affair. Because of their social status and their respective engagements they are keeping their relationship quiet.

### [[Shahar]]
Celestina had a close relationship with Shahar, leader of the Angel Rebellion which Hunt fought for. She claims they had a close friendship and because of this, after [[Hunt Athalar|Hunt]] was enslaved after the rebellion failed, she attempted to gather the funds necessary to buy him, but was denied to do so by the Asteri.

'''Colin McCarthy''' is a [[Fae]] guard and assassin for [[the Viper Queen]].

## Biography
### Early Life
Colin and [[Sathia Flynn]] were childhood friends. He later became a guard in the Flynn household. At some point, he and Sathia started a romantic relationship. Unbeknownst to her, her father [[Padraig Flynn]] would not let Colin court Sathia. Padraig introduced Colin to the Viper Queen and somehow got him to become one of her drugged-out guards and assassins. Padraig lied to Sathia and told her Colin had left because he had found a new position in [[Korinth]]. It was a difficult time for Sathia, so much so, that Colin leaving was her [[Ordeal]].

## ==
Sometime after, while Sathia is with her husband [[Tharion Ketos]], they encounter the Viper Queen and Sathia recognizes Colin amongst her guards. Sathia pleads for Colin to come home and get away from the Viper Queen. Colin doesn't respond and the Viper Queen says Colin doesn't respond unless she gives him the order. Colin is completely under the Viper Queen's control.

When Sathia reveals Tharion is her husband, Tharion says something like pain showed in McCarthy's eyes. When the Viper Queen orders the guards to aim their guns at the couple. Tharion notes Colin's weapon trembled, fighting the Viper Queen's order with everything he had so as not to hurt Sathia.

After the encounter Sathia decides not to go with Tharion to the [[Eternal City]] and leaves him a note saying that she can't leave Colin in the Viper Queen's hands so she has gone to try to save him.

## Physical Description
Colin has bright, blue eyes. He has a broad chest and is very tall.

## Relationships
### [[Sathia Flynn]]
They used to have a romantic relationship before Sathia's father got involved which caused the couple to split. They both still appear to care for each other. '''Connor Holstrom''' was [[Danika Fendyr]]'s Second and part of [[The Pack of Devils]].

## Biography
### Early Life
When Connor was a teenager his parents died, leaving him and his younger brother [[Ithan Holstrom|Ithan]] alone. Connor raised Ithan on his own, supporting his brother's love of [[sunball]] but insisting he go to college before starting a sporting career. Connor and Ithan were very close, and his little brother idolized him.

At some point Connor met Danika Fendyr and they became friends, with her ultimately choosing him to be the Second in her Pack of Devils. Through Danika Connor met [[Bryce Quinlan]], who was attending [[Crescent City University|CCU]] with her. Connor developed feelings for Bryce but obeyed Danika's orders to not make any moves towards her, though they had a mutual interest in each other and he was not subtle in his disapproval of her various partners.

After Danika graduated and the Pack of Devils began serving in the [[Auxiliary]], Connor became a respected enforcer of [[Lunathion]]'s laws, performing many violent and unsavory acts to keep the city safe but never complaining about his role as Danika's Second or the torture he sometimes had to inflict in the name of keeping the peace.

## ==
Connor, along with the rest of the Pack of Devils, is at [[Danika and Bryce's Apartment|Danika and Bryce's apartment]] after going to Ithan's sunball game for Pack Night. Connor, with permission from Danika, finally asked her out. Both of them spent the next few hours looking forward to their date but Connor was killed in Danika and Bryce's apartment before they had a chance to pursue a relationship.

[[Micah Domitus]] then hatched a plan to get revenge on Danika and try and find [[Luna's Horn]]. He waited until [[Philip Briggs]], a [[human]] [[Ophion|rebel]], was released from prison so he could frame him for the pack's deaths. He went to Danika and Bryce's apartment where the Pack of Devils, including Connor, were at the time and used his [[magic]] to disable them. He then injected Danika with [[Synth]], which caused Danika to rip her pack apart before turning on herself. Micah then summoned a [[Kristallos]] [[demon]] to cover his traces. When Bryce came home to the massacre, she recognized what remained of Connor was near Danika's remains, clearly protecting her until the end.

Philip Briggs was then accused of their murders due to him being imprisoned by Danika previously, leading the authorities to believe he did it for revenge. However, Micah admitted to Bryce that he was the one to kill Connor and the Pack.

## Personality
Connor had a strong, loyal character. He was content with his position in Danika's Pack and never complained about the things he had to do to keep Lunathion safe. It was common knowledge that Connor would have been Alpha of the Pack of Devils if Danika wasn't there, but Connor didn't resent it as his ambitions didn't run that way. Connor greatly enjoyed sports and closely followed his favorite teams. He is very observant.

## Physical Description
Connor was a good-looking male, with well-defined muscles, golden eyes, and golden skin. As a member of the Pack of Devils he had the group's insignia of a horned wolf tattooed on the side of his neck. In his wolf form, like all shifters, he is three times bigger than his regular counterpart, however, Bryce described him as being much larger than the average shifter. He had calloused fingers, a clearly muscular back, and broad shoulders. He had a wicked, flirtatious grin and a sensuous mouth.

## Powers & Abilities
### Powers
* '''Shifter Physiology''': Connor had enhanced speed, strength, and healing abilities because of his [[Vanir]] status. He had more acute senses and could heal from injuries much faster than a human. He could effortlessly change between his human and animal forms. 
* '''Shifting''': Connor possessed the ability to transform into a magically enhanced animal form. His animal form was that of a larger than average wolf. In this form he was stronger, faster, and had keener senses than in his human form.
* '''Immortality:''' Having made the [[Drop]], Connor was immortal which not only granted him a slowed and expanded lifespan and allowed him to survive injuries that not even his accelerated healing would be able to regenerate from.

### Abilities
* '''Combat Skills:''' Connor had been trained in various forms of combat and self-defense to an exceedingly high level as a member of the Aux.

## Relationships
### [[Bryce Quinlan]]
Connor and Bryce met at university through Danika. Connor had feelings for Bryce for years, but Danika told him he wasn’t allowed to make a move in until Bryce was ready. Connor had always been able to read Bryce and understand her intentions. After five years, Connor let her know how serious he was for her. It was the night of the raid on Philip Briggs's lab that Bryce realized that Connor was done waiting. Their five years of dancing around each other was going to end, and he was going to make his move soon. Bryce broke up with her boyfriend, [[Reid Redner]], and decided to give him a chance. However, he was murdered before they got the chance to go on the pizza date they had planned.

'''Cormac Donnall''' was the Crown Prince of the [[Avallen]] [[Fae]]. He was a cousin to [[Ruhn Danaan]] through his [[Lorin|Fae mother]]'s kin, and distant cousin to [[Bryce Quinlan]] through [[The Autumn King|her father]].

## Biography
### Early Life
Cormac was the Crown Prince of the Avallen Fae. He spent his whole life thinking he'd get the [[Starsword]] one day, that he'd go into the [[Cave of Princes]] and be proven worthy. He then studied all the lore, learned all the lineage, pored over every account detailing the variations in the power. His father, the King of the Avallen Fae, instead made him fight for it, alongside his twin cousins. This was known as his [[Ordeal]]. They went up against his other cousin, Ruhn Danaan, plus Ruhn's two best friends, [[Tristan Flynn]] and [[Declan Emmet]]. He lost the sword to them, and held a grudge about it ever since.

## ==
At a house party hosted by [[Ruhn Danaan|Ruhn]], [[Declan Emmet|Declan]], and [[Tristan Flynn|Flynn]], Hunt and Bryce are enjoying each other’s company and flirting. They enjoy spending time with Fury and Juniper, who are now openly in a relationship. The party is interrupted when Prince [[Cormac Donnall|Cormac]] of the [[Avallen]] [[Fae]] breaks through the wards. Ruhn, Declan, and Flynn are on guard- Cormac is Ruhn and Bryce’s cousin and fought Ruhn and his friends during Ruhn’s ordeal. They have never seen eye to eye, especially because Cormac thought he would be the one to inherit the “Chosen One” title and the Starsword. Cormac announces that he wants to meet his bride- Bryce. [[Einar Danaan|The Autumn King]] made a deal with [[Morven Donnall|the Avallen King]] to betroth Cormac and Bryce. Bryce balks at this and leaves with Hunt.  

Later, Cormac visits Bryce at the Fae Archives, where she now works. The star at the center of her chest glows in his presence, which he takes as a sign that their union is favorable. He tells Bryce that [[the Oracle]] told him that he was destined to unite with a princess with a star in her heart and that their union would allow their people to prosper.  

[...]  

Bryce returns to her apartment only to find [[Aidas|Prince Aidas]] waiting there in cat form. Aidas asks Bryce why she hasn’t mastered her powers yet and continues to be cryptic about his intentions with Bryce. He also tells Bryce that her star power comes from the ancient Fae queen [[Theia]] and that Hel’s armies are waiting for Bryce. Bryce and the gang are confused because Hel has always been their enemy. Aidas tells them that isn’t true and to think about how The Asteri have been the ones to write, and often re-write, history. Aidas explains that Bryce’s starlight comes from Theia, and Ruhn’s comes from [[Pelias|Prince Pelias]]. Aidas explains that Pelias forcibly wed Theia’s daughter, [[Helena]], and commandeered the throne for himself after Theia died. The Starsword is supposed to belong to Theia’s female heir. Aidas says that this brewing war is the same war they fought 15,000 years ago and the same war Hunt fought 200+ years ago. A war against the Asteri. He urges Bryce to get ready. She responds with something along the lines of “How? By aligning with Ophion rebels?” Aidas essentially states that if that is how she must be ready for the war, then yes. She says that she doesn’t even know any rebels. Aidas begins to disappear and says, “There’s one right behind you…Hello, Agent Silverbow.” Agent Silverbow is revealed as Prince Cormac.  

Bryce and her group (including Hunt, Tharion, and Ithan) are shocked and demand answers from Cormac. Except he wants answers of his own- namely, he wants to know where Sophie is and what they know about her. Bryce says that she only learned of Sophie yesterday because of Tharion. Tharion states that he was sent by the River Queen to confirm Sophie’s death. Cormac asks if he confirmed it, and Tharion says that her chains were unlocked. The gang deduces that Cormac and Sophie were in a relationship. Hunt asks Cormac how he was able to get into Bryce’s apartment, as it is very well-warded. Aidas led Cormac there and enabled him to get in. Cormac wants to work with them because he senses that they, too, want to know where Emile is. He wants to guard Emile because it was the last thing he promised Sophie before she went missing.  

Cormac says that multiple parties are interested in finding Emile, many with bad intentions. Pippa Spestos, a commander in the Ophion rebel group, is a fanatical and ruthless leader who wants to use the Renast’s powers to terrorize the Vanir. Cormac says that he came to Crescent City to ally with Bryce and Ruhn. Bryce resents the fact that he trapped her in an engagement, but he states it was the only way his father would allow him to come here. To avoid suspicions rising, Cormac needed a good excuse to stay in Crescent City. Additionally, he wanted to form a relationship with Ruhn because of Ruhn’s mind-speaking abilities. There is an agent—Agent Daybright—who Cormac needs to re-establish communications with. This agent is higher up in the Asteri command, so it is virtually impossible to reach them without a mind-speaker available. Bryce and the group are unsure what role they will play in all of this. Bryce states that she will look for Emile and help Cormac in that area only if he can eventually get her out of the engagement and teach her how to use her starborn powers.

Later, Ruhn and Cormac meet up to discuss working together. Ruhn agrees to try to contact Agent Daybright. Ruhn goes by “Night” and calls Agent Daybright, “Day.” She is a woman whose identity we don’t know since she is cloaked in fire. Ruhn’s concealment comes in the form of the night sky. She tells him about a rebel mission to intercept mech suits.

In part II, Ruhn and Ithan and Cormac are in the City to get intel from a spy disguised as a vagrant. Cormac and the spy exchange information, but as they are leaving Mordoc and a group of dreadwolves walk nearby. The trio is hidden through magic, but Mordoc has a keen sense of smell, being a wolf. Ruhn, Cormac, and Ithan are fearful- especially because Mordoc knows Ithan’s scent because he used to visit Moonwood. Mordoc is Danika’s father.

Cormac teleports them to a bar as a cover for their illicit activity. He has to leave, but Ruhn and Ithan stay, and Ruhn asks Ithan to move in with him, Declan, and Flynn. Their time is interrupted when the Hind and the Harpy show up. The Hind taunts Ithan about his feelings for Bryce, which he feels guilty about because his brother, Connor, also had strong feelings for Bryce.

Cormac learns that the rebel mission to intercept the mech suits was successful. The rebels are now on their way to an island, Ydra. Cormac wants them to find them so Pippa and the rebels cannot use the mech suits to wreak more havoc on people.

Cormac teleports to the island, which exhausts him. He tells Pippa that he wants Hunt to inspect the mech suits and analyze them. Pippa ends up killing several Vanir who were helping her team simply because they are Vanir. She also hints that she knows where Emile is. The team decides that no matter what, they can’t let a fanatic like Pippa possess the suits, so Hunt electrocutes the suit. The Hind and The Harpy show up. Pippa fights back, and the group has to hide their identities for fear of the Hind identifying them as working with the rebels. The Hind is able to see that it is Hunt and his friend group. She taunts them and drops something in the water. Bryce uses her starlight, and Hunt uses his lightning. Ophion sends ships after the group. The group is rescued by a giant mer-operated ship, saying they showed up on the scene because they got their signal.

While on the ship, many things happen. Bryce and Hunt finally have sex and solidify that they are mates. During sex, Bryce accidentally teleports. They figure that Hunt is the key to fully figuring out Bryce’s powers. They also learn that Sophie is officially dead. The mer-people found her body. Sophie has numbers and strange markings on her arms. Cormac says his goodbyes.

In part III, Bryce must attend the celebration as her role of fae Princess. She is also technically still engaged to Cormac even though Hunt is her mate. They go to the party where she snubs her father and talks with the angels first. She introduces Hunt as her mate and prince, which outrages the Autumn King and surprises Cormac. The Asteri video calls the party, which sets everyone on edge.

Later, The group forms a plan. Cormac and Tharion will target an Asteri lab just outside of the Eternal City to draw the Asteri away from the Crystal Palace, where Hunt, Bryce, and Ruhn will be. Ithan stays back to protect the mystic wolf because he anticipates that now that Sabine knows about her location, she will try to kill her. Cormac and Tharion successfully blow up the lab. Cormac waited for Tharion to escape before erupting into a ball of flame, destroying [[Pippa Spetsos]] and the mech-suits. [[Rigelus]] confirmed to Bryce that Cormac incinerated himself and the lab.

## Personality
Cormac could be described as cold, humorless, emotionless, arrogant, cocky, annoying, and deceitful. He often acted like a prick that manages to get under everyone's skin while he himself felt dead on the inside. He had a blunt personality and ruthlessly maintained his title as Crown Prince of the Avallen Fae. He held resentment for Ruhn and his friends due to the Starsword and their encounters when they were young. However, Cormac showed potential beneath his hard exterior. He made efforts to grow close to Bryce and her friends and showed love toward Sofie, revealing he was capable of the feelings and wasn't as terrible as everyone makes him out to be. He teaches Bryce how to teleport, and slowly becomes a bit of a friend to their circle. In the end he makes up with Ruhn, and they vow to be closer as cousins and not let their fathers dictate how they should be.    

## Physical Description
Cormac had tan skin, light brown eyes, and blond hair. Bryce described him as someone who would be ruggedly good-looking except for the utter lack of emotion on his face. He had a tattoo of strange symbols that encircled his left biceps. His hair has been described as glowing like molten metal and sparking embers. His appearance was mostly pristine.

## Powers & Abilities
### Powers
*'''Fae Physiology''': As one of the Fae, Cormac had enhanced senses and physical abilities, including a sense of smell much stronger than that of humans. He also healed from injuries much faster than a regular human.
*'''Avallen Magic''': As the son of the High King of the Avallen Fae, Cormac had the ability to manipulate shadows. This included summoning and walking through them, and cloaking himself and others in darkness.
*'''Teleportation''': Cormac was able to teleport between places, but after prolonged distances he became drained and pale. These powers could be hindered by a gorsian bullet, which is designed to split apart into shrapnel on impact and make sure the magic is suppressed for a long period of time.
* '''Fire Manipulation''': Cormac could manipulate fire.

## Relationships
### [[Sofie Renast]]
Sofie Renast was Cormac's known lover. The two of them communicated when she was imprisoned and he aided in her escape. They are both spies known as Agent Silverbow and Agent Cypress. During Sofie's escape, she went missing and Cormac sought to find her. He said he loved her and was hoping to find a way to spend his life with her. 

### [[Bryce Quinlan]]
Cormac was also in an arranged marriage with Bryce Quinlan, courtesy of the Autumn King and High King of the Avallen Fae. Bryce's star glowed every time Cormac was near. Despite the two of them being distant cousins, it was decided for political reasons. Bryce's joining with Cormac would have preserved the royal bloodline and strengthened the magic within it. Cormac believed Bryce was a good person and would make a good princess.

## Trivia
*Cormac had ten royal names and titles.
*When Bryce failed to bribe Cormac into dropping their engagement, he informed her of his considerable wealth and no need for money.

'''Cthona''' is the earth goddess and the lover of [[Solas]]. She calls to members of the [[House of Earth and Blood]]. 

## Biography
Cthona is the earth goddess of [[Midgard]]. Her [[mate]] is the sun god Solas and her sister is [[Ogenas]], the goddess of the seas. Cthona presides over mother-goddess domains.

Cthona is said to be merciful and people pray to her for strength. She is depicted in artwork as having a lush body. Priestesses of Cthona wear black robes.

## Trivia

* Cthona is commonly invoked in various phrases in Midgard.

'''Danika Fendyr''' was the Alpha of [[the Pack of Devils]] and [[Bryce Quinlan]]'s best friend. She and her entire pack were slaughtered.

## Biography
### Early Life
Danika was born on the 28th of April in the year [[Timeline|15,010]] [[V.E.|V.E]] to [[Sabine Fendyr]], the Prime Apparent of the [[Valbara]]n wolves and [[Mordoc]]. Her father wasn't part of her life. He tried to visit once when Danika was seventeen, but she refused to recognize him as her father. When she was older, Danika attended [[Crescent City University]] as a history major, where she met Bryce. The two quickly became close friends. They remained best friends throughout college and beyond.

During their senior year, Danika uncovered an illegal animal smuggling ring run by asp [[shifter]]s. Plagued by guilt at the thought of the animals in cages, Danika and Bryce snuck into their hideout to free the animals but were caught, resulting in them being chased and injured by the shifters. Cornered and about to be killed, Bryce told Danika to close her eyes and killed some of the shifters with her [[Starborn]] power, while the others were killed by police.

After college, Bryce and Danika moved in together in an [[Danika and Bryce's Apartment|apartment]] in [[the Old Square]] in [[Lunathion]]. Danika went on to become Alpha of the Pack of Devils. There were whispers that Danika should be tapped to be the Prime Apparent, bypassing her mother. Danika's grandfather, the [[Fendyr Prime]], had given the [[Fendyr Sword|family's heirloom sword]] to her after the blade had "called" to her on her eighteenth birthday.

Danika also acquired a reputation for being a party girl. Many of [[the 33rd Legion]] called her "The Party Princess," because of her party lifestyle, taking drugs often, and drinking excessively. She served as a member of the [[Auxiliary]].

###  
Danika and the Pack of Devils was brutally murdered by [[Micah Domitus]], the Governor of Crescent City. He wanted [[Luna's Horn]] so that he could summon an army to decimate the [[human]] [[Ophion|rebels]]. Micah blackmailed Danika into stealing the Horn. However, Danika did not trust Micah, so she did not give him the Horn after stealing it. Instead, she crushed the Horn and mixed it with ink and then convinced a drunk Bryce to get a tattoo, secretly using the Horn-infused ink on Bryce's back. This ploy was so that no one could find it and only Bryce could use it. However, Bryce was unaware that the Horn was in the tattoo.

Micah hatched a plan to get revenge on Danika and try and find the Horn. He waited until [[Philip Briggs]], a human rebel, was released from prison so he could frame him for the pack's deaths. He went to Danika and Bryce's apartment where the Pack of Devils were at the time and used his [[magic]] to disable them. He then injected Danika with [[Synth]], which caused Danika to rip her pack apart before turning on herself. Micah then summoned a [[kristallos]] [[demon]] to cover his traces and frame Phillip.

Philip Briggs was then accused of their murders due to Danika getting him imprisoned the first time, leading the authorities to believe he did it for revenge. However, Micah admitted to Bryce that he was the one to kill Danika and the Pack.

## Personality
Danika was an amazing friend, always being there for her friends when they needed her, as well as someone willing to protect and serve. She also had a kind heart, and helped anyone she could. Being a total badass, a skilled leader, and a good friend were all qualities she possessed. It was rare for Danika to let others' opinions of her, especially Sabine's, get to her. She was generally full of swagger and joy. As an Alpha, it was instinctual for her to protect others at all costs. Danika was not someone afraid of trouble or who was limited by rules. Lunathion's government had seven files on her, starting with petty theft when she was ten and continued until she reached her majority five years before her death. Then her file went eerily quiet. [[Isaiah Tiberian]] described her as someone who "seemed poised to start trouble wherever she went." Bryce thought that Sabine's cruelty made Danika a kinder and more thoughtful person, as she saw how Sabine treated others and was so disgusted that she wanted to become the opposite. Danika lived in terror of turning into her mother. 

## Physical Description
Danika had pale skin, caramel eyes, and silvery blonde cornsilk hair highlighted with streaks of rose, amethyst, and sapphire. She had a slim jaw and a neck tattoo of a horned, grinning wolf, the sigil of the Pack of Devils. The other one was on her spine, which said ''Through love, all is possible'' and she had gotten it inked with Bryce. In her wolf form, Danika had gray fur and was the size of a small horse. Only her caramel eyes remained the same in this form. She had long, sharp fangs, fuzzy ears, and a bushy tail. She radiated pure menace and grace.

## Powers & Abilities
Danika was one of the most powerful shapeshifters in the city. She was the granddaughter of the Prime of the Wolves, and she would have had a sea of power that would have succeeded that of the Prime Apparent, her mother Sabine, if she had not died before making the [[Drop]].

### Powers
* '''Shifter Physiology''': Danika had enhanced speed, strength, and healing abilities because of her [[Vanir]] status. She had more acute senses and could heal from injuries much faster than a regular human. She could effortlessly change between her human and animal forms.
* '''Shifting''': Danika possessed the ability to transform into a magically enhanced animal form. Her animal form was that of a larger than average wolf. In this form, she was stronger, faster, and had keener senses than in her human form. As an experienced shifter, she could hold a state between her two forms, such as changing her hands into claws or revealing her wolf eyes and fangs. She could communicate well while in her wolf form. 
* '''Bloodhound''': A trait from her father, Danika inherited the ability to smell out an individual's heritage, family ties, and bloodline.

### Abilities
* '''Combat Skills''': Danika had been trained in various forms of combat and self-defense to an exceedingly high level.

## Relationships
### [[Sabine Fendyr]]
The relationship between mother and daughter had been strained. Sabine was the Prime Apparent of the Valbaran wolves and had been her aging father's heir for centuries and Danika had officially been second in line to the title. However, there had been whispers that Danika should be tapped to be the Prime Apparent, bypassing her mother. Sabine's father had given his granddaughter their family’s heirloom sword after centuries of promising it to Sabine only upon his death, claiming that the blade had called to Danika on her eighteenth birthday. On top of that, Danika had been expected to claim a sea of power that would have put her ranking far past Sabine’s after her Drop.

However, despite their strained relationship, Sabine still showed some semblance of love for her daughter as she was distraught over her death, and became enraged by Bryce's involvement in her life. Her love for Danika was further emphasized when she was outraged and heartbroken at Bryce's and [[Hunt Athalar|Hunt]]'s accusations of killing her own daughter to become the Prime Apparent. She even admits that she was protecting Danika from the authorities when she stole the Horn by tampering with the security footage, and was fully aware that Danika's soul was present with Bryce when she made the Drop. Even tearfully echoing the same quote found on Bryce's tattoo, "''Through love, all is possible",'' during Bryce's final moments with Danika back at [[the Summit]].

### [[Mordoc]]
Danika was not on good terms with Mordoc. He tried to visit her once when Danika was seventeen, and she refused to recognize him as her father.

### [[Thorne]]
It was a long shot for an Omega like Thorne to ever be noticed by an Alpha like Danika. There seemed to be a gravitational pull that happened whenever Danika and Thorne were in a room together, like they were two stars orbiting each other. Thorne panted after Danika, who never seemed to be bothered by it until right before she died.

### [[Baxian Argos]]
Unknown to most, Danika and Baxian were [[Mate|mates]].

## Trivia
* The name Danika (''pronunciation: DAN-ick-ah'') is of Slavic and Latin origin, meaning "morning star." In Slavic mythology, Danica (''pronunciation: DAH-nee-tza'') is the personification of the morning star and in some regions is the goddess of dusk.
* Danika was a fan of the band [[The Banshees]].
* When communicating with [[Sofie Renast]], she used the name ''BansheeFan56''.
* Bryce describes Danika's snarl as being "like death incarnate, soft and husky and cold."

'''Declan Emmet''' is a hacker and part of the [[Auxiliary|Aux]], as well as [[Ruhn Danaan]] and [[Tristan Flynn]]'s best friend.

## Biography
### Early Life
Declan comes from a wealthy, but not noble, [[Fae]] family. He had a relatively normal childhood, and thus is the most well-adjusted of him, Ruhn, and Tristan. His parents truly care about him. Tristan and Declan have been inseparable since birth and both have been best friends with Ruhn almost their entire lives.

About fifty years ago, Declan went through his [[Ordeal]] with Ruhn and Tristan. Afterwards, the three of them made the [[Drop]]. They have been roommates for the past fifty years, often partying together at their [[Ruhn's House|house]]. He has a lucrative career he's built on a foundation of hacking into the Republic's websites and then charging them ungodly amounts of money to reveal their critical weaknesses. Declan is also a member of the city's Auxiliary.

## Personality
Declan does not appreciate anyone belittling his career as a hacker. [[Bryce Quinlan|Bryce]] claims Declan and Tristan are the biggest gossips in the city.

## Physical Description
Declan has dark red hair and amber eyes, as well as a deep voice. Declan is always armed with at least one gun and wears a knife in his black boots, even in his own home. 

## Powers & Abilities

* '''Fae Physiology''': As one of the Fae, Declan has enhanced senses and physical abilities, including a sense of smell much stronger than that of [[humans]]. 
*'''Healing Magic''': Declan is one of the few Fae who possesses the rare gift of healing.
* '''Immortality''': Since he has made the Drop, Declan is immortal, which not only grants him a slowed and expanded lifespan but allows him to survive injuries that not even his accelerated healing would be able to regenerate from.

### Abilities
* '''Combat''': Declan is trained in various forms of combat and a skilled warrior. Since he has trained his entire life to be a lethal, efficient killer, he's as well-trained as any of the angels' elite fighters.

Declan and Ruhn have been best friends almost their entire lives, who are often seen around each other. The two of them and Flynn used to live together for almost fifty years and still serve in the Aux. Going through their Ordeals together cemented their bond.

Declan and Flynn and have been best friends since childhood and practically have been inseparable since birth. Anything Declan knows, he automatically shares with Flynn in a matter of seconds. Going through their Ordeals together cemented their bond.

Declan and Marc met during a fancy party at one of the big engineering companies in the [[Central Business District]], and began dating.
