'''Einar Danaan''', better known as the '''Autumn King''', was one of the seven Heads of [[Lunathion]] and had control over [[Five Roses]].

## Biography
### Early Life
Not much is known about Einar's early life. He is nearing five hundred years old and has worn the golden crown of the Autumn King for most of that time.

Einar met the [[human]] [[Ember Quinlan]] when she was only nineteen years old. He pursued her, seduced her, tried to keep her, and became so possessive that he started to abuse Ember. When she realized she was carrying his child, she ran before he could scent it and lock her up in his [[The Autumn King's Villa|villa]].

The Autumn King had eyes at every depot, but a priestess got Ember out of the city with the hope of getting all the way to the [[House of Earth and Blood]] headquarters in [[Hilene]], where Ember could beg for asylum, as it was one of the few places where the Autumn King wouldn't dare infringe on their territory. However, because it was a three-day drive, and none of the [[Korinth]] priestesses had the ability to defend Ember against [[Fae]] warriors, she drove the five hours to [[Solas]]' Temple in [[Oia]]. Before Ember reached Hilene, the Autumn King's people found them. They expected to find a helpless and hysterical female, but found the legendary sharpshooter [[Randall Silago]], and Ember, who would move the earth itself to keep her daughter safe.

The House of Earth and Blood deemed Ember a vessel for [[Cthona]], which amounted to an official order of protection, meaning the Autumn King was unable to act further without creating any significant political issues for himself. She gave birth to their daughter and named her [[Bryce Quinlan]]. Randall and Ember fell in love and married each other.

Twelve years ago, when Bryce was a coltish thirteen years old, she came to the Autumn King's villa to meet her father and his people. Einar had insisted the visit be discreet. He took her to [[the Oracle]], as was custom for the [[Valbara|Valbaran]] Fae, to check if she had any power. However, when he found out her power level was negligible at best, he threw her and her belongings out of his house. He offered Bryce "mountains of gold marks in exchange for her eternal silence" about their relationship. Bryce refused to take anything from him.

## ==
The Autumn King, as one of the Heads of the city, was in attendance at the meeting to discuss the release of human rebel [[Philip Briggs]]. The Autumn King, [[Micah Domitus]], and the Oracle had searched for any possible work around the loophole Briggs had used to get out of prison, but were unsuccessful.

After [[Isaiah Tiberian]] meets with Ruhn to discuss the murder of [[Maximus Tertian]], Ruhn visits Einar at his villa. Einar informs Ruhn that after [[Hypaxia Enador]] is recognized by the [[Asteri]] as the new [[witch]]-queen of Valbara at the Summit, Einar will announce Ruhn and Hypaxia's engagement. Ruhn is upset with this.

## ==
[[Einar Danaan|The Autumn King]] made a deal with [[Morven Donnall|the Avallen King]] to betroth Cormac and Bryce. Ruhn argues with his father, The Autumn King, about Bryce’s betrothal to Cormac. Ruhn is also in an arranged engagement with [[Hypaxia Enador]], the queen of the [[Witch|witches]].

[...]

Bryce uses her title as princess to get a meeting with Celestina. She asks Celestina to please allow Hunt to leave the barracks so that they can go to the island. She tells Celestina that she needs Hunt to have a night away so that Hunt can meet her parents. Celestina understands. Because Bryce has been using her princess title around town, Fae law dictates that she officially accepts her title. This means the Autumn King has more control over her now.

Bryce must attend the celebration for Celestina and Ephraim’s official mating as her role of fae Princess. She is also technically still engaged to Cormac even though Hunt is her mate. They go to the party where she snubs her father and talks with the angels first. She introduces Hunt as her mate and prince, which outrages the Autumn King and surprises Cormac. The Asteri video calls the party, which sets everyone on edge.

###  
After a failed attempt to travel back home, [[Bryce Quinlan]] ends up teleporting straight into the Autumn King's study, upon her return from [https://acourtofthornsandroses.fandom.com/wiki/Velaris Velaris]. Bryce outsmarts her sire by locking him in his closet to escape his home. Once Bryce escapes, the Autumn King and [[Morven Donnall]] track her down to the caves below [[Avallen]]. His heart is then impailed by [[Starsword|the Starsword]], held by his son, Ruhn. As he dies, Ruhn reveales he has had the ability to speak through people's minds. He also tells the Autumn King about the prophecy that [[the Oracle]] gave Ruhn. Ruhn then uses the sword to sever his neck and kill him for good. 

## Personality
Einar was a power-hungry, self-absorbed, and cruel man who was obsessed with appearances, all while hiding in his office, where he performed science experiments and monitored the positions of the planets in their world, searching for patterns. Einar had long desired to find and travel to the Fae home world. He was very rigid when it came to conforming to political and hierarchical rules, especially concerning others showing him respect, due to his high ranking in the Lunathion government. When he had strong emotions, fire licked along his shoulders and in his hair. Einar was neither for nor against the Asteri, just an opportunist. He wanted whatever would give him more power. He claimed to be concerned with the Fae, but actually just cared about advancing himself. According to Ruhn, his father got his power and a thrill not from the sheer force of his power, but in the control over the self and his impulses.

As noted by his children, one of the main concerns Einar worried over was the constant degradation of the Fae's magic and power. With this being his primary motivation in having his children married off in arranged weddings to other powerful, influential bloodlines, as this ensured the continued strength of his bloodline and magic. When Bryce revealed the source of the Fae's gradual loss of power, the parasite the Asteri had placed in Midgard's water supply that ensured the need for the [[Drop]] and the harvesting of the [[Firstlight]], Einar was surprised and quickly theorized that perhaps the Asteri was taking too much from the Fae. Nevertheless, this revelation did not dissuade Einar's hunger for power, authority and land, as shown where after Bryce revealed her trip to the original homewrorld of the Fae, Einar had already begun entertaining the idea of conquering it and giving it to the Asteri in exchange for a good chunk of it. As noted by Bryce, Einar exhibited much of the same avaricious and power hungry ambitions as their ancestor [[Theia]].

## Physical Description
Einar was described as a tall and muscled man with an impossibly beautiful but intense face beneath his long wine-red hair, the exact same shade and silken texture as his daughter's, [[Bryce Quinlan]]. Bryce and her father also share the same amber colored eyes. He has a long, thin nose. Despite his age, he still showed no sign of aging.

## Powers & Abilities
Due to his age and status, the Autumn King was incredibly powerful, universally respected, and feared.
* '''Fae Physiology''': Einar had enhanced senses, physical abilities, and healing abilities because of his Vanir status. Moments of emotional intensity temporarily heightened his powers and abilities. He had the superhuman speed and grace characteristic of the Fae.
* '''Immortality''': Having made the [[Drop]], Einar was immortal which not only granted him a slowed and expanded lifespan but allowed him to survive injuries that not even his accelerated healing would be able to regenerate from. Ruhn had said that it was entirely possible for Einar to live for several more centuries due to how little he had aged in his life to date.
* '''Pyrokinesis''': Einar could create, use, and control fire. Due to his age and status, Einar was incredibly powerful.

## Relationships
### [[Ember Quinlan]]
Einar met Ember when she was nineteen and started an affair with her. He was deeply in love with her and wanted her to become his queen. But he became very territorial which led him to be physically abusive. This led Ember to leave him when she was pregnant with Bryce. Later, even after decades of their separation, Einar had a mixture of love and hatred for Ember. With him hating her for leaving him and marrying Randall, but at the same time deeply loving her and regretting his actions that lead to her leaving him, albeit Einar himself refuses to admit his mistakes.

### [[Bryce Quinlan]]
The Autumn King had a complicated relationship with his daughter. Due to his abuse of both Bryce and her mother, Bryce herself had stated to despise her father and wanted nothing to do with him. Einar, in turn, had shown to possess a combination of love and dislike for his daughter. The Autumn King had shown to regard Bryce with a certain level of dislike for her. He had not spoken Bryce's name in twelve years, which was when she came to his villa for the first and last time. He only referred to her as "''that girl''" and showed active annoyance whenever she was brought up. However despite his past actions towards her and how he treated her, he has shown active pain and heartbreak at hearing Bryce's contempt for him and her statement that Randall Silago was her true father and was the one who mattered to her. Bryce resented her father for making her choose between the House of Earth and Blood and the [[House of Sky and Breath]]. The only person she hates more than [[Sabine Fendyr]] is her father.

### [[Ruhn Danaan]]
The relationship between Einar and his son was strained at best. Einar depicted Ruhn to be a failure and did a better job at ignoring him than caring for him. Einar would abuse Ruhn multiple times during his childhood, using his fire magic to burn Ruhn as punishment. The scars never healed, so Ruhn has tattooed over them. Upon returning from [[Avallen]] and enduring his [[Ordeal]], Ruhn moved out of his father's house. As Ruhn possesses the Starsword, it provides him some leverage against his father.

### [[Randall Silago]]
The Autumn King revealed that he was in truth deeply jealous of Randall Silago, with him having in the end, been the one to marry Ember and raising Bryce. Hints of this jealousy were first seen when Bryce stated that Randall was her true father, with Einar reacting with visible pain at this statement of her effectively denouncing him. Einar himself later admitted to this jealousy when confronted by his daughter.
*Einar's scent was described to be a mix of ashes and nutmeg.
*Einar would've burned Avallen into ashes if he could.

'''Ember Quinlan''' is [[Bryce Quinlan]]'s [[human]] mother.

## Biography
### Early Life
When Ember was sixteen, she took a test so she wouldn't get drafted into the [[Human Conscription Service]]. She aced the test and was allowed to skip the service. Ember was nineteen years old when she met [[Einar Danaan]]. He pursued her, seduced her, and went so far into the possessive territory that he started to abuse Ember. When she realized she was carrying his child she ran before he could scent it and lock her up in his [[The Autumn King's Villa|villa]].

Ember found shelter in a temple to [[Cthona]] in [[Korinth]], as the priestesses took her in and sheltered her, naming her a holy pregnant vessel. She gave birth to Bryce and spent the following three years at the temple. Ember did the laundry to earn their keep.

The Autumn King picked up a rumor that Ember had a child and sent some [[Fae]] to hunt her down, wanting his child dragged back to him at any cost. The Autumn King had eyes at every depot, but a priestess got Ember out of the city with the hope of getting all the way to the [[House of Earth and Blood]] headquarters in [[Hilene]], where Ember could beg for asylum and where the Autumn King wouldn't dare infringe on their territory. However, because it was a three days' drive, and none of the Korinth priestesses had the ability to defend Ember against Fae warriors. So she drove the five hours to [[Solas's Temple]] in [[Oia]], partially to rest, but also to pick up a holy guard. It turned out to be [[Randall Silago]]. He was two weeks away from swearing his vows when the High Priest asked him to escort Ember to Hilene. Randall was the only human so he was chosen since the High Priest guessed Ember wouldn't trust a [[Vanir]] male. Right before they reached Hilene, the Autumn King's people found them. Where they expected to find a helpless, hysterical female, they found a legendary sharpshooter and Ember, who would move the earth itself to keep her daughter.

After that, the House of Earth and Blood deemed Ember a vessel for Cthona and Randall a vessel for [[Solas]], which amounted to an official order of protection meaning the Autumn King couldn't do anything. Ember and Randall later got married and raised Bryce together in a [[Ember and Randall's House|house]] outside the town of [[Nidaros]]. The only time she contact the Autumn King was to have him get Bryce appointed to full civitas status and thus make her exempt from the draft.

###  
Ember is hysterical upon hearing what happened to [[Danika Fendyr]] and the [[The Pack of Devils|Pack of Devils]]. [[Isaiah Tiberian]] sends a patrol from the 32nd Legion to bring Ember and Randall to [[Lunathion]]. They stay with Bryce for at least a week.

Ember sets up multiple therapy appointments for Bryce for a year after Danika died, but Bryce refuses to go to any of them.

###  
TBA

###  
[[Fury Axtar]] had been the one to tell Ember that [[Hunt Athalar]] and [[Ruhn Danaan]] are in the [[Asteri]] dungeons and Bryce is no longer in [[Midgard]].

## Personality
Ember is fiercely protective of Bryce, and can come across as nagging and overbearing. Bryce describes her as someone who makes "overprotectiveness a competitive sport." She is also killed at the guilt trip. She is opinionated, straightforward, and never backs down from a fight. Ember refuses to tolerate hatred and bigotry towards humans, having heard it all her life. She feels like Bryce's wild and willful nature is sometimes a mirror to herself. She has a motherly instinct. She is intelligent and resilient. Even Hunt is intimidated by her.

## Physical Description
Ember looks a lot like her daughter Bryce and is generally beautiful. She has long black hair that frames her face,  freckled skin, full lips, and high cheekbones that make her dark, depthless eyes striking. She has the same face as Bryce, just with difference coloring, but has a small scar on her face due to Einar.
* Ember has an artistic vein and makes pottery.

'''Fionn''' was the first and last High King of the [[w:c:acourtofthornsandroses:Prythian|Fae Homeworld]] and husband of [[Theia]].

## Biography
Fionn originated from the Fae Homeworld, where he served the [[Asteri]], though they were known as [[w:c:acourtofthornsandroses:Daglan|Daglan]]. At one point he started a rebellion with his then-secret lover, [[Theia]]. For that feat, he received the [[Starsword]] - their rebellion ended in the usurpation of power. Fionn then became High King and made Theia his High Queen. Together they became the parents of two daughters - [[Helena]] and [[Silene]] - and later announced that he would pass his throne to his oldest daughter instead of his wife. Theia then decided to kill her husband with the help of her [[Pelias|general]]. Fionn has never been mentioned in the histories of [[Midgard]] or in its lore. 

## Personality
Fionn is described by Silene as someone who had never been a generous or giving type.

## Physical Description
Fionn had the appearance of a High Fae male. He was handsome and broad-shouldered with golden hair.

## Powers & Abilities
* '''Telepathy''': Fionn could speak to others mind-to-mind, also known as mind-speaking.

## Trivia
* While Fionn bore the Starsword after pulling it from the Cauldron. His best friend carried the sword's twin, [[Truth-Teller]]. After his friend's death, Fionn took Truth-Teller for himself, but chose not to use it.
* Silene mentioned that the land her father ruled seemingly died with him.

'''Fury Axtar''' is an infamous assassin.

## Biography
### Early Life
Not much is known about Fury's early life, but at some time she became an assassin and attended [[Crescent City University]], the latter only being a front for a mission. This is where she met [[Bryce Quinlan]], [[Danika Fendyr]], and [[Juniper Andromeda]]. Fury had made the [[Drop]] at age twenty-one, but hadn't chosen the age for herself. It is unknown whether it'd just happened, or had been forced upon her. While she's never revealed another form or any inkling of [[magic]] beyond the most basic, she is a civitas, meaning a full citizen, which would only happen if she was deemed worthy by those in charge.

## ==
Fury was out clubbing with Juniper and Bryce the night Danika died. It is unclear whether it was at that time that Fury and Juniper started dating. Fury suffered a lot from Danika's death and blames herself, mainly because her death was very close to her and that shouldn't be for a mercenary and contract killer. In order to forget the loss of her friend, Fury threw herself into work. She began traveling for many years on the continent [[Pangera]], and only returned to [[Lunathion]] when she was needed most. Bryce kept trying to get in touch with Fury throughout the years but never managed to so. Fury herself chose to only stay in contact with Juniper. When Fury ultimately returned, tensions arose between her and Bryce, which eventually subsided. During the big fight, she helped [[Hunt Athalar]] to get to Lunathion in her helicopter to come to Bryce's aid.

## ==
TBA

## ==
Fury is the one who has to tell [[Ember Quinlan]] that Hunt and [[Ruhn Danaan]] are in the [[Asteri]] dungeons and Bryce is no longer in [[Midgard]].

## Personality
Fury is frequently portrayed as a mysterious woman, and for good reason; her age, species, [[Vanir]] class, and motives are entirely unknown, even to Bryce. No matter how mysterious she may be, however, everybody knows that she is an assassin. Even [[Isaiah Tiberian]] and Hunt know to stay away from her. She often comes off as cold and blunt, even to her friends, but she appears to have a soft spot for Juniper, coming to her defense when Bryce upsets her.

## Physical Description
Fury has onyx hair, which was long but is now in a short bob. She also has an angular face, deep-set, dark chestnut-colored eyes, and light brown/tanned skin. She is tall, has straight white teeth and a slender build. She almost always wears head-to-toe black. Her voice is flat and cool, laced with a hint of amusement. She has the preternatural speed, grace, and reflexes of the Vanir.

## Powers & Abilities
### Powers
* '''Immortality''': Having made the Drop, Fury is immortal which not only has granted her a far greater lifespan, but has allowed her to live through injuries that not even her accelerated healing would be able to regenerate from.

### Abilities
* '''Master Combatant''': Due to her occupation it is safe to assume that Fury has been trained in various forms of combat and self-defense, in which she likely excels.
* '''Weapons Expert''': Fury is an expert marksman.
* '''Pilot''': Due to Fury owning and commanding her own helicopter, it is safe to assume that she trained to be a pilot, though it is unknown to which extend and how many aircrafts.

## Relationships
### [[Juniper Andromeda]]
Fury met Juniper when attending CCU, but though they initially appeared to be close friends, the two of them are actually lovers.

'''Hecuba Enador''' was the late Queen of the [[Valbara|Valbaran]] [[Witch|Witches]] and mother to both [[Lidia Cervos]] and [[Hypaxia Enador]].

## Biography
### Early Life
Hecuba came from a long line of witch-queens. She raised her daughter Hypaxia cloistered in her mountain fortress.

###  
Hecuba died two months before [[the Summit]]. Since then, Hypaxia, has been crowned the new witch-queen of Valbara.

## Personality
Hecuba had been a beloved, if enigmatic, ruler.

## Physical Description
She is described as having the same physical appearance as her daughter Lidia. Lidia is "the spitting image of our mother" as stated by her half-sister Hypaxia. Thus, it can be implied that Hecuba had the same blonde hair and golden eyes as Lidia.

## Powers & Abilities
* '''Flight''': Like other Witches, Hecuba could fly using her broomstick.
* '''Healing Magic''': Hecuba had the abilities of a medwitch and could treat wounds and heal various ailments. Hecuba was also able to create enhanced healing products. 
* '''Immortality''': Having made the [[Drop]], Hecuba was immortal which not only granted her a slowed and expanded lifespan but allowed her to survive injuries that not even her accelerated healing would've be able to regenerate from.

Lady '''Helena''' was the daughter of Queen [[Theia]], the wife of Prince [[Pelias]].

## Biography
Helena entered [[Midgard]] from [[w:c:acourtofthornsandroses:Prythian|another world]] during [[the Crossing]] together with her mother and other [[Fae]]. She was one of several Fae back then with the [[Starborn]] power. She married Pelias, who was her mother's High General. It is unbeknownst to most that Pelias killed Helena's mother, Queen Theia and then forced Helena to marry him and raped her in the process to continue the lineage with the Starborn.

## Personality
It was noted by her sister [[Silene]] that like her and unlike their mother, Helena did not harbor much ambition for power and conquest and while not shying away from the responsibilities of being a princes and heir to the throne of Prythian, she was otherwise content with what she had. However, when her mother [[Theia]], made her plans to go to other worlds to conquer them, Helena was complaisant in her mother's ambitions and went along with it. 

Helena was shown to have deeply loved her sister Silene, and when they were making their escape from [[Midgard]] back to their native world, Helena had decided to buy time for her to make her escape by holding off the [[Asteri]]'s forces sent after them. Effectively sealing her fate of being captured by the Asteri and forcibly married to [[Pelias]]. As noted by [[Aidas]], despite her circumstances, she never broke under them and continued to remain defiant against the Asteri. As she had continued to contact him and the other Princes of Hel as she made future plans to defeat the Asteri. 

During the course of her marriage with Pelias and being forced to bear his children, Helena was noted to have mixed feelings towards her children, particularly her sons, as she soon realized that they took after her husband in personality and enjoyed the privilege and power granted to them by the Asteri. Never the less, Helena continued to harbor hope that some of her future descendants would be better and left several clues to aid them should they decide to fight back against the Asteri's tyranny.

## Physical Description
She had night-colored hair and golden skin that poured starlight and shadows.

## Powers & Abilities
### Powers
* '''Fae Physiology''': As a Fae, Helena had enhanced senses and physical abilities than [[humans]]. She also healed from injuries much faster than a human. Moments of emotional intensity temporarily heightened her powers and abilities.
*'''Starlight''': Helena inherited a third of her mother's Starborn powers. This gave her the ability to wield starlight. This is the light of a pure star. It seemed to have no other power beyond creating light itself and blinding others. It is passed down through the maternal line.
**'''Starfire''': Helena inherited a third of her mother's Starborn powers. This gave her the ability to wield starfire, which acted like a laser.
*'''Shadow Manipulation''': Helena could manipulate shadows. This included summoning and walking through them and cloaking herself and others in them.
*'''Telepathy''': Helena could speak to others mind-to-mind, also known as mind-speaking. However, she couldn't actually read other's thoughts.

'''Hesperus''' was one of the seven [[Asteri]].

## Biography
TBA

## Personality
TBA

## Physical Description
Like all members of the Asteri, Hesperus' true form was unknown and often required a host body to contain her power, which wasted away as time passed due to the strain. She inhabited the body of a [[Nymph]] with blonde hair. She had dark eyes that gleamed with malice and long, slim fingers.

## Powers & Abilities
* '''Firstlight and Secondlight Manipulation''': Like all the Asteri, Hesperus could manipulate the power of [[firstlight]] and [[secondlight]] to achieve God-like feats. This resulted in the people of [[Midgard]] believing that the Asteri housed the power of a star within themselves.
* '''Energy Absorption''': Like all the Asteri, Hesperus consumed firstlight and secondlight to increase her power to that of a god. However, it was alluded to that this power was not infinite and without regular consumption, her powers would have weakened over time.
* '''Body Jumping''': Like all the Asteri, Hesperus could transfer her own essence into the body of others. But due to the nature of her power, the host body would eventually waste away from the toll.

## Trivia
* In Greek mythology, Hesperus is dubbed the "Evening Star."
* When Bryce had unwittingly awakened the imprisoned [[Vesperus]], she asked if she was related to Hesperus in some way, referring to Hesperus by her title as the Evening Star, and Vesperus reacted angerly and claimed that she was the Evening Star instead. This might hint to a possibly strained relationship with her fellow Asteri.

'''Hilde''' was a [[Witch]] who defected from the [[House of Earth and Blood]] and served the [[Asteri]] instead.

## Biography
### Ealy Life
Not much is known about Hilde. When younger, she flew in [[Hecuba Enador]]'s unit when both were in their fighting days. She defected from the House of Earth and Blood and swore allegiance to the [[House of Flame and Shadow]] because of Hecuba. According to Hilde, Hecuba was weak and spineless and had no taste for punishment. Later, she became an Imperial Hag under the employ of the Asteri and did their biddings. Hilde was also responsible for putting the power-dampening "halo" tattoos on the Asteri's enemies, like [[Hunt Athalar]] and the [[Sprite]] Queen [[Irithys]].

## ==
She was framed by [[Lidia Cervos]] and the [[Dreadwolf|Dreadwolves]] for having been in possession of a [[Comm-Crystal]], which were used by [[Ophion]] rebels and which Lidia had slipped into Hilde's pocket a few days earlier. She was thrown into the dungeons of the [[Crystal Palace]], where she was then interrogated by Lidia. The latter had Irithys with her and, testing Irithys, commanded her to burn Hilde, which she refused.

Even though Lidia left Hilde in her cell, she later struck a bargain with her for the witch's freedom. She was supposed to do one favor for Lidia when the time came and then could walk free. Lidia used that favor to have Hilde remove the tattoo from Irithys' neck. When freed from her [[Gorsian Stone|gorsian shackles]], Hilde sought to attack Lidia, but was stopped when the latter pulled a gun on her. She had no other way than to remove Irithys' tattoo, only after Lidia promised not to kill her, but was instead killed by Irithys' fire, as she now knew of Lidia's treason against the Asteri.

## Physical Description
Hilde had baleful, yellow-tinged eyes and thinning white hair, which glimmered like strands of wispy moonlight. She was a wretched, twisted creature, but one of hidden beauty. Before her death, she wore the imperial uniform, with the crest of the Republic fraying over her sagging breasts. The gums of her teeth were blackened.

## Powers & Abilities
* '''Flight''': Like other Witches, Hilde could fly using her broomstick.
* '''Enchantments''': Hilde was able to cast various enchantments. An example are the "halo" tattoos she placed on the Asteri's prisoners to bind their powers.
* '''Immortality''': Having made the [[Drop]], Hilde was immortal which not only granted her a slowed and expanded lifespan but allowed her to survive injuries that not even her accelerated healing would be able to regenerate from.

'''Orion "Hunt" Athalar''' is an infamous [[The Fallen|Fallen]] [[Malakh|angel]], who worked for the Archangels as a personal assassin. He is [[Bryce Quinlan]]'s [[mate]].

## Biography
### Early Life
Hunt, whose birth name is Orion, was born into a poor family, in [[Shahar]]'s territory in the southeast of [[Pangera]]. His [[Hunt's Mother|mother]] worked very hard to look after him but never told him who his father was. When he was old enough, he joined the army in order to support himself and his mother. Hunt would send money back to his mother, which she would store away. After hearing of this, a group of people broke into his mother's home, killing her to steal the money Hunt had sent. Days after his mother's death, Hunt made the [[Drop]] at twenty-eight years old. A sour-faced government employee had been appointed his [[Anchor]].

This led him to become a general in the uprising that sought to overthrow the [[Imperial Senate]]. Shahar was the leader of the uprising as well as Hunt's lover. Hunt was High Commander of all Shahar's legions. He spent five years with Shahar and the 18th. When the uprising failed, Shahar was killed, and Hunt became a slave along with everyone else who was part of the uprising. For the first seven years, he was tortured in the [[Asteri]] dungeons until the Archangel [[Ramuel]] became his master. Ramuel continued the pattern for two more years until he got bored with it and realized that Hunt would be more useful killing [[Demon|demons]] and doing his bidding than rotting away in torture chambers. By the time Hunt became Ramuel’s personal assassin, it had been nine years since Hunt has seen sunlight, been outside, or flown. Hatred was the only thing that fueled him enough to survive. Hunt was then purchased by another Archangel. His third owner was the Archangel [[Sandriel]], Shahar's twin sister. He had done most of the assassinating for Sandriel for the fifty-three years she'd possessed him. At first she tortured Hunt in her infamous dungeon, then she forced him to kill people, usually [[human]] rebels, in her blood-soaked arena. Sandriel had a particular hatred for Hunt for multiple reasons. It was her forces that Hunt had decimated at the [[Battle of Mount Hermon]], and Sandriel believed that Hunt had lured Shahar into the rebellion and thus it was Hunt's fault that she had to kill her sister despite Shahar starting and leading the rebellion. Hunt flew in Sandriel's 45th Legion.

Four years ago, Archangel [[Micah Domitus]] purchases Hunt from Sandriel for eighty-five million gold marks and brings him to [[Lunathion]] as part of his [[triarii]]. Micah offered Hunt a bargain: as Hunt had led the [[The 18th Legion|18th Legion]] on [[Mount Hermon]], he was responsible for all 2,217 deaths that he and the rebels he led took. This meant that Hunt must kill 2,217 people of Micah's choice, as Micah's personal assassin, in order to one day gain his freedom. After four years, Hunt has killed eighty-two people. 

## ==
Hunt is part of Micah's triarii and serves as [[Isaiah Tiberian]]'s Second. Hunt is famous around [[Midgard]] as the Umbra Mortis.

[...]

Micah orders Hunt to provide protection Bryce while she looks for the person responsible for [[Danika Fendyr]]'s murder. While Hunt sees this as essentially babysitting duty, he also knows Micah gives him this job as it will give him an excuse not to be at [[the Comitium]] while Sandriel and [[Pollux Antonius]] are there.

## ==
Bryce and Hunt are trying to return to their normal lives after the shocking events of the first book. Despite their strong romance, Bryce and Hunt have decided not to consummate their relationship until the Winter Solstice because Bryce wants to take time to process and feel out their relationship before taking that step.

At a house party hosted by [[Ruhn Danaan|Ruhn]], [[Declan Emmet|Declan]], and [[Tristan Flynn|Flynn]], Hunt and Bryce are enjoying each other’s company and flirting. They enjoy spending time with Fury and Juniper, who are now openly in a relationship. The party is interrupted when Prince [[Cormac Donnall|Cormac]] of the [[Avallen]] [[Fae]] breaks through the wards. Ruhn, Declan, and Flynn are on guard- Cormac is Ruhn and Bryce’s cousin and fought Ruhn and his friends during Ruhn’s ordeal. They have never seen eye to eye, especially because Cormac thought he would be the one to inherit the “Chosen One” title and the Starsword. Cormac announces that he wants to meet his bride- Bryce. [[Einar Danaan|The Autumn King]] made a deal with [[Morven Donnall|the Avallen King]] to betroth Cormac and Bryce. Bryce balks at this and leaves with Hunt.

With the Archangel [[Micah Domitus|Micah]] gone, Crescent City needs a new Archangel to lead. Hunt and his angel friends anticipate the arrival of the Archangel, [[Celestina]], and are unsure what to expect. Unfortunately, with [[Sandriel's Triarii|Sandriel’s]] death, The Asteri decided it would be best if her [[triarii]] split between Celestina and another Archangel, [[Ephraim]]. Because of this, The Hind (Lidia), [[Pollux Antonius|The Hammer (Pollux)]], [[Baxian Argos|The Hellhound (Baxian)]], and [[the Harpy]] form Celestina’s new triarii. Hunt and some of the other angels have a horrible history with this group. Hunt abhors The Hind and The Hammer especially, and suffered much torture at Sandriel’s command. While tensions are high between Hunt and the new triarii, Celestina seems kind and like she has good intentions. Hunt still does not trust her, though.

Tharion continues to look into Sofie Renast and hopes to find Emile. Signs point to him hiding out in Crescent City. Tharion also discovers that Sophie had been in contact with [[Danika Fendyr|Danika]]. Because of this, he talks with Bryce and Hunt about Danika’s involvement and wants to see if they know anything. Hunt is hesitant and fearful of getting involved in anything that could garner the Asteri’s attention. Conversely, Bryce wants to figure out the level of Danika’s involvement and also wants to protect Emile since she fears for him only being a helpless 13-year-old.

Later, Aidas says that this brewing war is the same war they fought 15,000 years ago and the same war Hunt fought 200+ years ago. A war against the Asteri. He urges Bryce to get ready. She responds with something along the lines of “How? By aligning with Ophion rebels?” Aidas essentially states that if that is how she must be ready for the war, then yes. She says that she doesn’t even know any rebels. Aidas begins to disappear and says, “There’s one right behind you…Hello, Agent Silverbow.” Agent Silverbow is revealed as Prince Cormac.

Bryce and her group (including Hunt, Tharion, and Ithan) are shocked and demand answers from Cormac. Except he wants answers of his own- namely, he wants to know where Sophie is and what they know about her. Bryce says that she only learned of Sophie yesterday because of Tharion. Tharion states that he was sent by the River Queen to confirm Sophie’s death. Cormac asks if he confirmed it, and Tharion says that her chains were unlocked. The gang deduces that Cormac and Sophie were in a relationship. Hunt asks Cormac how he was able to get into Bryce’s apartment, as it is very well-warded. Aidas led Cormac there and enabled him to get in. Cormac wants to work with them because he senses that they, too, want to know where Emile is. He wants to guard Emile because it was the last thing he promised Sophie before she went missing.

Later, Hunt and Bryce are sleeping when Hunt wakes up in a pocket-realm. He is dreaming, but what occurs is actually happening. The Prince of the Pit, Apollion, has called him there to insist he figures out more about his and Bryce’s powers.

The angels are called to assemble by Celestina. Pollux and Baxian got into a public altercation. Baxian attacked Pollux because Pollux was sexually harassing a woman. The brawl caused a scene and made it look like Celestina did not have a good grip on her triarii. Because of this, the Asteri want a happy distraction (and also to keep a tight leash on Celestina). They force Celestina to mate with Ephraim, which she accepts as her duty.

Celestina and Hunt talk privately. She tells him that she wants to look out for him because Shahar was her close friend. She confesses that she tried to buy him out of slavery numerous times but was denied every time. Hunt says that he wants to look out for her as well.

In part II, Bryce, Juniper, Fury, and Hunt go to the Meat Market to put out feelers for Emile. They have no luck.

Bryce and the group discover that Danika had told Sophie about a good hiding spot in Crescent City, “where the weary souls find respite.” Bryce suspects that this hiding spot is in the Bone Quarter. Her friends think that anyone would be crazy to go there. Still, Bryce and Hunt go – Baxian even tries to stop them. They go regardless, and the Under King says that no one is there. Bryce confronts him about the reapers because she thinks he sent them. He denies it and says that there’s more to the Bone Quarter than most know about. He reveals that he takes a “taste” of dead souls. Crescent City is powered by the firstlight generated when people make the Drop. The Bone Quarter is thought to be a peaceful afterlife for the dead. The Under King reveals that he feeds off the dead’s secondlight and that he has no intentions of letting Hunt and Bryce leave alive, because he wants to feed on their light. He sets his helhounds on the duo. A fight ensues, and both Bryce and Hunt are roughed up, but they manage to escape because they experiment with their powers. Hunt uses the Dead Gate to amplify his lightning, he aims at Bryce, and she serves as a conduit to his power. Tharion is in the river and hears the storm caused by Hunt and rushes to help them. They all escape.

Celestina and Ephraim host a celebration party to toast to their relationship. Celestina specifically asks Hunt to be there for support. Meanwhile, Bryce is breaking down what happened with the Under King to Ithan and the rest of the gang. Ithan is distraught because he doesn’t want his brother’s soul to be fodder. Their conversation is interrupted when Sabine enters the apartment and attacks Bryce because she wants her to stay out of wolf business, and hosting Ithan is staying involved. Bryce manages to shoot a text to Hunt, and he has to leave the celebration, which disappoints Celestina. Baxian follows Hunt. They manage to diffuse the tension and kick Sabine out. As punishment for leaving her celebration party early, Celestina orders Hunt to stay in the barracks for two weeks.

Bryce uses her title as princess to get a meeting with Celestina. She asks Celestina to please allow Hunt to leave the barracks so that they can go to the island. She tells Celestina that she needs Hunt to have a night away so that Hunt can meet her parents. Celestina understands. Because Bryce has been using her princess title around town, Fae law dictates that she officially accepts her title. This means the Autumn King has more control over her now.

Cormac teleports to the island, which exhausts him. He tells Pippa that he wants Hunt to inspect the mech suits and analyze them. Pippa ends up killing several Vanir who were helping her team simply because they are Vanir. She also hints that she knows where Emile is. The team decides that no matter what, they can’t let a fanatic like Pippa possess the suits, so Hunt electrocutes the suit. The Hind and The Harpy show up. Pippa fights back, and the group has to hide their identities for fear of the Hind identifying them as working with the rebels. The Hind is able to see that it is Hunt and his friend group. She taunts them and drops something in the water. Bryce uses her starlight, and Hunt uses his lightning. Ophion sends ships after the group. The group is rescued by a giant mer-operated ship, saying they showed up on the scene because they got their signal.

While on the ship, many things happen. Bryce and Hunt finally have sex and solidify that they are mates. During sex, Bryce accidentally teleports. They figure that Hunt is the key to fully figuring out Bryce’s powers. 

In part III, Bryce reveals to Hunt that she found Emile a bit ago. She had the Viper Queen and her people look for him in exchange for a favor. Hunt is mad about her lying. She has Fury arrange for Emile to get a new identity and be adopted by Bryce’s parents.

Bryce must attend the celebration as her role of fae Princess. She is also technically still engaged to Cormac even though Hunt is her mate. They go to the party where she snubs her father and talks with the angels first. She introduces Hunt as her mate and prince, which outrages the Autumn King and surprises Cormac. The Asteri video calls the party, which sets everyone on edge.

Bryce and Hunt break into a coat closet to fool around. Someone had the same idea. Turns out, Hypaxia and Celestina are in a secret relationship. Ruhn meets Day through mind-speak, he says he tried to meet with her but that the Harpy and the Hind were on his tail. They kiss but are interrupted by the same male from earlier. Apollion visits Bryce in her dreams. He tells her that Aidas loved Queen Theia and that the fae and forces of Hel united against the Asteri in the war. He also says that it is easier to communicate with Hunt because he was “bred” to interact with those from Hel.

Later, Hypaxia and Ithan attempt to summon Connor’s spirit. Instead, the Under King shows up and tests their power. It takes all night and they show up to Ruhn’s doorstep. The Under King forces Ruhn, Hunt, Bryce, and Hypaxia to meet him at Urd’s Temple, or he will feed on the Pack of Devils’ souls. The Under King informs the group that the reapers from before came from the Eternal City. He also tipped off Ophion and Pippa that they would be at the temple. The rebel group attacks. Bryce is able to teleport to the City gates and alerts everyone that rebels are attacking Urd’s temple. Members of Celestina’s triarii show up to fight them. Hunt and Bryce fight as well and manage to get away with Baxian’s help. Bryce points a gun at Baxian and demands to know why he’s always helping them. She is suspicious. Baxian reveals a tattoo across his heart that says, “Through love, all is possible.” He says Danika was his mate. 

Later, Hunt, Bryce, and Ruhn split up. Bryce looks into the archives. There, she finds plaques with the Asteri names on them and their power levels. She discovers that the Asteri feed on firstlight and need it to survive. She realizes they likely are not stars, as they claimed. She is infuriated that millions of people make the Drop at their command and give them their firstlight and that in death, the Asteri leech off of their secondlight.

It's later revealed that the Asteri made them think that they all had different ancestry to pit them against one another. Bryce teleports out of the room only to find that Hunt has been cornered. Mordoc also finds Ruhn. They are gathered together and shackled. The Harpy starts to taunt Hunt, Bryce, and Ruhn when The Hind enters and starts fighting her. 

Hunt, Ruhn, and Bryce try to fight their way out. Baxian helps, which gets him put in shackles as well. Rigelus admits that he impersonated Aidas earlier, because he needed Bryce to hone her powers for this very moment. He wants her to use the Horn to open a portal to another world. A world where the Fae had overthrown the Asteri’s brethren before. Rigelus wants to conquer it. Bryce agrees to help Rigelus as long as he agrees to free Ruhn and Hunt. They understand that she has another plan up her sleeve. Rigelus allows her to say goodbye. Ruhn tells her through mindspeak that she needs to go to Hel and have their armies come to Midgard’s aid. Bryce breaks her chains and runs.

Rigelus pursues her as she runs towards the crystal gate. Hunt and Ruhn fight the others, but Hunt is able to send a burst of lightning into Bryce, which allows her to open a portal. She falls through it, unsure where it is taking her.

Hunt, Ruhn, and Baxian prepare to be tortured. Rigelus tortures Hunt first, by putting a crown of thorns back on his brow and making him a slave.

## ==
TBA

## Personality
Hunt is a committed and focused male whose current mindset has been forged from the hardships and conflicts throughout his entire life. He is both highly lethal and observing with a rather imposing demeanor that causes others to feign back as a result of his reputation as "the Umbra Mortis." According to [[Ruhn Danaan]], Hunt is relentless, single-minded, and utterly brutal when it comes to eliminating threats. However, in truth, Hunt is incredibly self-loathing. Not only is his self-hatred attributed to the torture he endured at the hands of many Archangels for the failed rebellion against the Asteri, but also his status as a slave made to be a weapon for those who owned him at the time. Hunt can have a hair-trigger temper that has left entire imperial fighting units in smoldering cinders. His cool stare serves as the only warning to opponents, non surviving what happens next. Hunt cares absolutely nothing about royals and their rigid hierarchies. 

As a result of this, Hunt rarely makes appearances in social gatherings and rarely shows his snarky and dry-humored side unless it's with people he truly knows. However, Hunt is far more caring than what the public perceives of him. He is shown to be understanding, and protective. He isn't afraid to speak his mind when it comes to the well-beingdemeanoure he cares about. His more snarky and dry-humored behavior is more prominent when around individuals he's comfortable with such as his friends in [[the 33rd Legion]] and Bryce.

## Physical Description
Hunt has light brown skin, also referred to as tan and golden-brown, shoulder-length dark hair, and dark brown angular eyes. He is described to be very muscular. His angel wings are a darkish grey and are "softer than velvet, and smoother than silk." She repeatedly refers to him as an "alphahole." She thinks he "has a stick up his ass." Hunt, for his part, wants to chuck her into the [[Istros River]] and thinks she's a brat and shallow party girl. Bryce stated that she knew Hunt was her friend ever since he pulled out [[Jelly Jubilee]]. Their romantic relationship develops, and when Hunt was on his deathbed, Bryce whispered "I love you" to him before making the Drop. Hunt feels an unconditional and unending love for Bryce. Bryce feels that Hunt's strength "compliments her own, bolstering it and helping it thrive, not overpowering hers."
** Hunt's name is a nod to [[Apollion]] giving him some of his essence, with Orion being the master of Sirius, just like Apollion, who killed the Asteri of the same [[Sirius|name]].
** Orion is one of the constellations in Midgard.
* Hunt owns a [[sunball]] hat, which has a high sentimental value to him. It was the first thing he bought when he came to Lunathion, with the first paycheck he ever received from Micah.
* He smells of rain-kissed cedar.
* When acting as the Umbra Mortis, Hunt often wore a special [[Hunt's Helmet|helmet]], which the Asteri temporarily used as a trophy in their throne room.

'''Hunt's mother''' was an unnamed [[Malakh]].

## Biography
Not much is known about Hunt's mother. She lived in [[Shahar]]'s territory in the southeast of [[Pangera]] and knew his [[Hyrieus]] briefly, who himself was a volunteer fo breeding a child as a project for the Princes of [[Hel]]. He had any intention to stay with his partner, but was killed by [[Rigelus]] - still, he didn't betray Hunt's mother. When Hunt was born, his name was whispered into his mother's ear.

His mother worked very hard to look after him but never told him who his father was. When he was old enough, Hunt joined the army in order to support himself and his mother. Hunt would send money back to his mother, which she would store away. After hearing of this, a group of people broke into his mother's home, killing her to steal the money Hunt had sent.

## Powers & Abilities
* '''Malakim Physiology''': As an angel, Hunt's mother had physical abilities and enhanced senses that made her far stronger than both [[human]]s and [[Vanir]].
* '''Flight''': Through her Malakh wings, Hunt's mother was able to fly. She was presumably also capable of carrying people in her arms while she flew.
* '''Immortality''': Having made the Drop, Hunt's mother was immortal which not only had granted her a far greater lifespan, but had allowed her to live through injuries that not even her accelerated healing would have been able to regenerate from.

'''Hypaxia Enador''', a former [[Witch|Medwitch]] and Queen of the [[Valbara]]n [[Witch]]es, is the current Head of [[House of Flame and Shadow]].

## Biography
### Early Life
Not much is known about Hypaxia's early life. Upon the wishes of her mother, Queen [[Hecuba Enador]], Hypaxia grew up in near solitude in mother's mountain fortress. She was so sheltered that no photos of her even exist. Hypaxia's [[necromancer]] abilities made her mother think it was best she learn in seclusion. She was educated by three very ancient ghosts. Queen Hecuba thought they would be the best teachers of not only necromancy, but everything a queen needs to know.

Hypaxia's undead tutors predate the [[Asteri]]'s arrival and were long dead before the [[Northern Rift]] opened. They remember the time when [[Parthos]] still stood. One of her tutors, [[Palania]], was born in the city surrounding it and tells Hypaxia about the peaceful realm where the dead went before the arrival of the Asteri and the creation of places like [[the Bone Quarter]]. Hypaxia found a way to stop her mother's soul from going to an eternal resting place like the Bone Quarter knowing she would be harvested as [[secondlight]] if she did.

###  
Queen Hecuba has died a month ago. Hypaxia has been crowned the new witch-queen of Valbara. Her reign will officially be recognized by the Asteri at [[the Summit]] next month. [[Einar Danaan|The Autumn King]] plans to betroth his son [[Ruhn Danaan]] to Hypaxia soon after. 

She is helping Ruhn while he is investigating [[Synth]] and the drug's origins. She is working as a medwitch in [[Lunathion]] to get a taste of normal life before being formally crowned as the Witch Queen, after having spent most of her early life in seclusion. She provides Ruhn with research information about Synth and tells him to let her know if he can get his hands on some venom from a [[kristallos]] [[demon]] so she can continue her research on trying to create an antidote for a Synth overdose.

Ruhn mentions to [[Bryce Quinlan|Bryce]] and [[Hunt Athalar|Hunt]] that his medwitch contact needs more kristallos venom if they happen to get some, which leads Bryce to turn to Hypaxia for help when Hunt encourages her to have the scar on her leg checked out due to it constantly hurting her. They go to see Hypaxia, who successfully extracts and stabilizes the kristallos venom. She later sends a sample of the resulting Synth antidote to Bryce as a thank you, inadvertently saving Bryce from overdosing on the drug during her fight with [[Micah Domitus]].

Hypaxia's identity is revealed during the Summit when she is officially recognized as the new Witch Queen by the Asteri.

###  
A few months later, Hypaxia has formerly accepted the Autumn King's proposal to marry her to his son and has also began to form friendships with other inhabitants of Lunathion, including [[Tharion Ketos]]. 

Hypaxia asks Ruhn to officially accompany her to [[Celestina]] and [[Ephraim]]'s mating celebration, expressing that she doesn't trust the Coven of Witches meant to be her subordinates and advisors due to the fact they were chosen by her mother and not her. Having been raised away from the rest of the witch court, Hypaxia is estranged from the rest of the coven and they're uncomfortable around her due to her necromantic abilities. She is concerned the coven might think to replace her with her sister, the [[Lidia Cervos|Hind]]. She has no witch gifts, making her nothing but a figurehead and allowing her mother’s general, [[Morganthia Dragas|Morganthia]] to rule. The coven wants a closer bond to the Asteri, at the cost of their relative autonomy, where Hypaxia dreams of restoring the witches to their former glory.

As her official fiancé, Ruhn hires [[Ithan Holstrom]] as her bodyguard in exchange for Hypaxia using her necromancy to help Ithan contact his dead brother. Hypaxia agrees, but tells Ithan it can only happen on the night of the Autumnal Equinox, the same night as the mating celebration. Ruhn also uses the excuse of commandeering [[Ariadne]] for use as a bodyguard as a cover for Ithan's foolish actions when he stole Ariadne and three fire [[Sprite|sprites]] from [[the Astronomer]], who was enslaving Ariadne and the sprites.

She and Bryce begin to form a friendship after Bryce asks her out for pizza and "girl talk" when Bryce needs someone to confide in about the Auntumn King's plot to wed her off to [[Cormac Donnall]]. Bryce also tries to talk up her brother to the Witch Queen, hoping that the two of them will come to form an attraction to one another and have a happy marriage. Hypaxia confides to Bryce that she had a lover prior to her engagement to Ruhn, which is the source of why she is distant and hesitant of actually forming a relationship with him, because she's already in love. 

When leaving the restaurant, they're attacked by a couple of [[Deathstalker]] demons and Ithan's throat is ripped out, rendering him unable to protect Hypaxia and Bryce. Ariadne is nowhere to be found, having abandoned her assignment and running off. Hypaxia and Bryce team up to defeat the demons, with Hypaxia using her [[magic]] to supercharge Bryce's special powers so she is able to teleport to Ithan's dying body and get his gun. Bryce is able to then shoot and kill one of the demons while Hypaxia takes out the other with a knife to the skull by jumping from her broom. Hypaxia then uses her healing abilities to save Ithan, as he wasn't healing fast enough. She takes him back to the Consulate where she is staying in order to keep an eye on his healing for the night. Ithan tells Hypaxia he heard what she told Bryce about how she did something to save her mother's soul from going to the sleeping realms to be harvested for secondlight and asks her to do the same to [[Connor Holstrom|Connor]]. He also tells Hypaxia about the wolf mystic being held by the Astronomer, and asks if there is anything she can do. She tells him there probably isn't anything more she could do that his other friends couldn't. 

Hypaxia and Ruhn attend Celestina and Ephriam's mating celebration together, but Hypaxia is distracted and looking around for her sister. She tells Ruhn that she has never met Lidia and is nervous about it now. Ruhn offers to go with her. They approach the Hind and [[Pollux Antonius|the Hammer]] and Hypaxia dismisses Pollux so she can talk to her sister. Ruhn also uses this opportunity to excuse himself to go meet up with Agent Daybright, whose identity he hasn't discovered yet. At some point, Hypaxia excuses herself from the party, claiming stomach cramping. Bryce and Hunt sneak off to find a place to have sex and accidentally walk in on Hypaxia and Celestina in the middle of a tryst. Celestina is ready to go on the offensive to keep their relationship a secret, but Bryce and Hypaxia are able to diffuse the situation and calm both Celestia and Hunt, keeping the [[Malakh|angels]] from fighting one another in defense of their respective lovers. Bryce and Hunt promise to keep their secret.

Hypaxia returns to the party to find that Ruhn has left without telling anyone, so she leaves to meet Ithan for the summoning ritual. During the summoning, Hypaxia remarks how strong Connor's spirit seems to be and Ithan feels pride for this until the spirit finally manifests and is revealed to actually be [[The Under-King]]. The Under-King tells them Connor is well cared for, but whether he remains that way is up to Ithan. The Under-King doesn't tell him what that means, only says he wants to play a game with them. He is curious about Hypaxia's abilities. He disappears, but they suddenly can hear the hunting hounds of the House of Flame and Shadow.

Ithan and Hypaxia eventually meet up with the others at the training center and explain how the Under-King toyed with them, trapping them in the orchard with his dogs. It took Hypaxia a while to find a way out of the wards that wouldn't kill them. They inform Hunt that the Under-King wants to see Bryce at [[Urd's Temple]] and if she doesn't go, then he will send Connor and the rest of [[the Pack of Devils]] through the [[The Dead Gate|Gate]] and make them into secondlight.

## Personality
Hypaxia is brave, confident, and intelligent. She is young, but wise and responsible. She has a sense of kindness to her and a quiet joy. She isn't ashamed of her necromancy gifts. She finds it works hand in hand with her healing abilities. She is guarded, but still open and friendly. There's a clarity in her eyes.

## Physical Description
Hypaxia has a curvy body, glowing brown skin, sleek black waist-length curly hair, full lips, high cheekbones, velvety lashes, and large, brown eyes. She is described as alarmingly beautiful, a beauty in its rawest form. Her laugh is like silver bells.

## Powers & Abilities
### Powers
Due to her heritage from her parents, Hypaxia has the abilities of both a healer and a necromancer.

* '''Necromancy''': Due to her father's bloodline, Hypaxia can raise and speak with the dead. She has inherited the full force of her father's talents.
* '''Healing Magic''': Due to her mother's bloodline, Hypaxia has the abilities of a medwitch and can treat wounds and heal various ailments. Hypaxia is also able to create enhanced healing products. An example being silver nitrate balm, which is used to treat burns.
* '''Wind Magic''': Hypaxia can create, use, and manipulate wind magically. It's unknown what the extent of her abilities are.
*'''Immortality''': Having made the [[Drop]], Hypaxia is immortal which not only grants her a slowed and expanded lifespan but allows her to survive injuries that not even her accelerated healing would be able to regenerate from.
*'''Flight''' (formerly): Like other Witches, Hypaxia was able fly using her broomstick. She lost the ability after she was usurped and defected to [[House of Flame and Shadow]].

### Abilities
* '''Medical Training''': As a medwitch, Hypaxia has completed some form of medical training to enhance her healing magic.

## Relationships
### [[Ruhn Danaan]]
Ruhn and Hypaxia were betrothed during the Summit. They are not interested in each other romantically and have sworn no vows of faithfulness. Ruhn respects and admires Hypaxia.

### [[Celestina]]
Hypaxia has an illicit affair with the Archangel Celestina. Because of their social status and their respective engagements they are keeping their relationship quiet.

'''Isaiah Tiberian''' is a [[Malakh]] and the Commander of [[the 33rd Legion]].

## Biography
### Early Life
Isaiah was part of an uprising that sought to overthrow the [[Imperial Senate]]. He became part of the [[Shahar]]’s elite [[The 18th Legion|18th Legion]]. When the uprising failed, Shahar was killed, and Isaiah became a slave along with everyone else who was part of the uprising. Isaiah endured brutal torture in the [[Asteri]]'s dungeons and in various Archangels' dungeons for years.

Ten years ago Isaiah was purchased by [[Micah Domitus]] and brought to [[Lunathion]] to shore up the city's defenses. He was made Commander of the 33rd Imperial Legion and leads Micah's [[triarii]]. Isaiah refers to them as Micah's "little cabal." Despite being a member of [[the Fallen]], he has risen back to the rank of commander. Isaiah has enjoyed every second in Crescent City since he arrived. He worked his way up the ranks by being agreeable and doing his job, in the hopes Micah would free him from being a slave.

###  
Isaiah and his unit are the ones who respond to [[Bryce Quinlan|Bryce]]'s call for help after she finds [[Danika Fendyr]] and [[the Pack of Devils]] murdered, is attacked by a [[demon]] who is attacking an angel.

Twenty-two months after Danika and her pack's murder, Isaiah and Hunt are ordered by Micah to speak with Bryce regarding the murder of [[Maximus Tertian]], as the [[vampyr]] was killed in an identical manner as the pack.

When Hunt is assigned to assist Bryce, Isaiah is his liason back in the [[The Comitium|comitium]]. Hunt uses this several times to comunicate with Isaiah instead of Micah. Isaiah is the one to arrange for Hunt to stay out of the Comitium while [[Sandriel]] is in [[Lunathion]], and gives Hunt and Bryce information on the conversation the two [[Malakh|Malakim]] had with [[Sabine Fendyr]] after Danikas murder. 

When Sandriel arrives in Lunathion, Isaiah is present when Bryce and Hunt confront her briefly. 

When Hunt gets arrested for buying [[Synth]], Isaiah is the one to put manacles on him. Later, he visits Hunt in his cell, expressing regret at what is going to happen and reminscing on their shared past. He also seems hurt that neither Hunt nor the other two members of Micahs Triarii, [[Justinian Gelos|Justinian]] and [[Viktoria Vargos|Victoria]], told him about their plan. 

During the Summit he is "shown off" as Micah's commander of the 33rd, wearing a ceremonial armor Hunt remembers to be horribly uncomfortable. He is the first of the angels to bow to Hunt when he regains his full powers and doesn't hesitate to join Hunt against the armies of [[Hel]].

## Personality
Isaiah is aware of his standing and position and generally tries to avoid trouble, especially after the failed uprising. He is normally unflappable. He has always known how to play the game of politics and stomachs the protocols and hierarchies. This ability is why he has risen back to the rank of commander after becoming Fallen.

He is painstakingly detailed in his work, having specific records of year old interrogations. 

## Physical Description
Isaiah is beautiful and handsome. He has tight brown curly hair, dark brown skin, and brown eyes. He has white angel wings. He has a magical thorn "halo" tattoo on his forehead which controls and binds his power and has a slave's mark on his wrist. He has calloused fingers. He usually wears a suit and tie. Isaiah is instantly recognizable due to being on the nightly news and in gossip columns often enough that there's no way to mistake him for someone else.

## Powers & Abilities
### Powers
* '''Malakim Physiology''': Isaiah has enhanced physical abilities and senses that make him far stronger than both [[Human|humans]] and [[Vanir]]. He also possesses regenerative abilities that are far superior to humans and Vanir.
* '''Flight''': Through his Malakim wings, Isaiah can fly at high speeds. He is also capable of carrying people in his arms while flying. His wings also act like a shield to protect him from some attacks.
* '''Immortality''': Having made the [[Drop]], Isaiah is immortal which not only grants him a slowed and expanded lifespan but allows him to survive injuries that not even his accelerated healing would be able to regenerate from.
*'''Wind Magic''': Isaiah has the ability to create, use, and manipulate wind magically. He is capable of keeping a charging enemy at bay. He was depicted summoning a breeze to ward off [[Sabine Fendyr]].

### Abilities
*'''Combat Skills''': Isaiah has been trained in various forms of combat and self-defense to an exceedingly high level. This includes training in various types of weapons. Even with his power limited to a tenth of its full strength due to the "halo" tattoo, his skills as a warrior are formidable.

## Relationships
### [[Hunt Athalar]]
Hunt and Isaiah have been friends since their time in the 18th Legion. Despite technically out-ranking him, Isaiah knows how powerful Hunt is and would never dare to order him around. Hunt believes Isaiah is the best of the Malakhm, the best of the Fallen, and all the legionaries Hunt has ever served with. Hunt believes that Isaiah should have been in the Asterian Guard due to his skills and pristine white wings, but he would never be selected because he wasn't highborn.

## Trivia

* Isaiah killed at least three dozen Imperial Legionaries during the [[Battle of Mount Hermon]].
* In times of heightened tension or emotions he tastes ether on this tongue.

'''Ithan Holstrom''' is a [[Shifter]] and the younger brother of [[Connor Holstrom]].

## Biography
### Early Life
Ithan was raised by his older brother Connor since their parents died. He idolized his brother and always listened to him, which resulted in the [[The Pack of Devils|Pack of Devils]] used to call Ithan "Connor's Shadow." He was an unofficial member in the Pack. The Holstrom family were Alphas, leaders, and warriors in their own right.

###  
Ithan was a history major and the star of [[Crescent City University|CCU]]'s [[sunball]] team. He was rumored to be going pro as soon as Connor gave the nod. The reason he didn't go pro after high school was that the elder brother he idolized so much insisted that his degree came first, sports second.

He was a cocky boy who tried flirting on [[Bryce Quinlan|Bryce]] so he could try the successful techniques on [[Nathalie]]. He relentlessly questioned Bryce about when she'd finally start dating his brother and didn't take "never" for an answer.

On hearing about Connor and the Pack's murder, Ithan called Bryce twenty-one times and sent a half-dozen audiomails. The messages started from being sobbing and panicked, then shifted to worry after Bryce didn't respond. After the chats from Bryce's phone were leaked, Ithan sent one last audiomail to her, and the tone was nothing but razor-sharp coldness. Ithan told Bryce that she was not welcome at the [[Sailing]]. He wept as he pushed his brother's remains, and was so distraught that his sunball teammates had to hold him up. 

After Connor's death, Ithan dropped out of CCU and quit playing sunball. No one could convince him to return. He'd walked away from the sport and never looked back. Ithan made the [[Drop]] and stepped into his brother's shoes by joining the [[Black Rose Wolf Pack]], the pack that replaced the Pack of Devils. Ithan became the Second of the Black Rose Wolf Pack.

###  
When Bryce comes back to her place and opens the door, she smells blood in her apartment. She finds Ithan beaten. Hunt and her place him on the coffee table in order to take care of his injuries. He tells them that it was his punishment for disobeying Sabine's orders during the Spring attack and defending Bryce in a press article. Bryce decides to shelter him.

Ithan works multiple times with Tharion or Ruhn for researching Emile.

When Sabine learns Bryce shelters him, she pops up at Bryce's apartment and assaults her. Ithan refuses to submit and drops the information about Mordoc being Danika's father.

After overhearing Bryce and Hunt in the apartment, Ithan accepts Ruhn's proposal to live at their place. He continues working with the Aux faes.

After Bryce's revelations about what really happens to souls in the Bone Quarter, Ithan, with Bryce and Tharion, go visit the Astronomer to ask about Connor's soul.

On his way back to the Mystics, Ithan then receives a call from the Prima offering him to become the Alpha of his own pack and asks for time to think about it. When arriving there, the female Mystic is out of her tank, he smells that she is a Wolf and offers to exfiltrate her but she refuses.

## Personality
Before Connor's death, Ithan was a cocky boy who harmlessly flirted around and basked in the glory of being a sunball star player. He loved sports and closely followed his favorite teams. After his brother's death he became serious and grew into a honed predator and a gifted warrior. As per [[Naomi Boreas|Naomi]], Ithan is a solid male: fair-minded, smart, and loyal.

## Physical Description
Ithan is tall and muscular with golden-brown hair, tan skin, and brown eyes. Ithan's wolf form wears a grey fur and is the size of a horse. 

## Powers & Abilities
* '''Shifter Physiology''': Ithan has enhanced speed, strength and regenerative abilities because of his [[Vanir]] status. He has more acute senses and can heal injuries much faster than a regular [[human]].
* '''Shifting''': As a shifter Ithan possesses the ability to transform into a magically enhanced animal form. His animal form is that of a larger than average wolf. In this form, he is stronger, faster and has keener senses than in human form.
*'''Elemental Magic''': Since taking the cure to rid himself of the [[Asteri Parasite]], Ithan can manipulate snow.
*'''Immortality''': Having made the Drop, Ithan is immortal, which not only grants him a far greater lifespan, but allows him to live through injuries that not even his accelerated healing would be able to regenerate from.

'''Jesiba Roga''' was a cursed [[human]] and art dealer who owned [[Griffin Antiquities]].

## Biography
### Early Life
Jesiba was born as a human before [[Midgard]] was overtaken by the [[Asteri]] and [[Vanir]]. She worked as a priestess at [[the Great Library of Parthos]] and was present when the Library was attacked. She boarded a ship, ''[[the Griffin]]'', to smuggle some of the books out. She slipped through the Vanir nets and sailed across the [[Haldren Sea]] and found safe harbor in [[Valbara]]. She was aided by the [[Mer]] across the sea, at the behest of [[the Ocean Queen]]. But before she was able to reach the sea, [[Apollion]] found ''the Griffin''. He had heard about the doomed stand at Parthos, the ships, and the priestesses burned with their books. He was curious about what might be so valuable to the humans that they were willing to die for it. He didn't understand when Jesiba told him it was no power beyond knowledge, no weapon beyond learning. He refused to believe her and instead assaulted and cursed her for her impudence in denying him the truth. Her curse was to live, unchanging, until she showed him the true power of the books. She also gained her [[magic]] that day, as when Apollion found her, he was ripe with power after just consuming [[Sirius]]. He didn't intend it, but when his magic touched her, something transferred over.

In Valbara, Jesiba decided to join the [[witch]]es when the witch-dynasties rose, in hopes of finding similar companionship with them. It is unknown why Jesiba left the witch kingdom later on, but she defected from the witches centuries ago and joined the [[House of Flame and Shadow]] and is said to be [[the Under-King]]'s emissary.

###  
Jesiba was believed to be a four-hundred-year-old sorceress. She was the owner of Griffin Antiquities and the boss of [[Bryce Quinlan]]. Jesiba was frequently gone for long stretches of time acquiring objects from across [[Pangera]]. Later, Jesiba meets via video call with [[Micah Domitus]] when he came to Griffin Antiquities.

###  
Jesiba greeted [[Ithan Holstrom]] when he came to the House of Flame and Shadow. He told her what happened to [[Sigrid Fendyr]] and Jesiba offers to help Ithan work with a [[necromancer]] to raise Sigrid from the dead in exchange for him working as her assistant.

## Personality
Jesiba was a very strict person and didn't like people disagreeing with her. People that opposed her, or simply annoyed her, risked being turned into animals. She was decidedly one of the most feared people in [[Lunathion]], maybe even the whole country. Few people could get away with talking to the Archangels the way Jesiba did. She played as hard as she worked.

## Physical Description
Jesiba had a curvy body and cropped ash-blond hair cut shorter on the sides and longer on the top. She was described as effortlessly chic and casual. Her face was young and wise, bedroom-soft yet foreboding. She had pale gray eyes which gleam with glittering magic, alluring and deadly. At times they can seem to glow like flames. Her nails were painted a sparkling silver. Without her disguise, she looked younger and had long, curling tresses. Her voice was distinctive, arch, cultured, and bored and yet could also be beautiful and lilting.

## Powers & Abilities
* '''Animal Transmutation''': Jesiba could turn others into animals of her choosing at will.
* '''Sorcery''': Jesiba possessed an arsenal of various spells to a high level. Her power was enough to rival any sorcerer or necromancer.
* '''Enchantments''': Jesiba had significant knowledge and skill in casting enchantments and wards. She possessed an arsenal of spells to rival any sorcerer or necromancer in any of the Houses. For example, she casts golden wards to seal the Parthos Library onto the shelves in her office.
* '''Immortality''': As a result of the curse placed upon her, Jesiba was essentially immortal as she was made to be "unchanging."

## Relationships
### [[Bryce Quinlan]]
Jesiba was the employer of Bryce. The relationship between these two was best described as complicated. While Jesiba often threatened Bryce to turn her into a lower creature or the like, she was also seen to care for or be concerned about her. Jesiba gave Bryce the [[Archesian Amulet|Archesian amulet]] which provided Bryce considerable protection. She also trusted her to essentially run and protect Griffin Antiquities when Jesiba would leave to acquire artifacts. Jesiba gave up her immortality for Bryce and left all her assets to Bryce.

### [[Lehabah]]
Jesiba was the former owner of the enslaved [[Sprite]]. Lehabah sometimes complained about Jesiba's coldness and they weren't particularly close, but there was no conflict or disdain between them.

Not much is known about Julius' life. '''Juniper Andromeda''' is a famed dancer and [[Bryce Quinlan]]'s close friend.

## Biography
### Early Life
Not much is known about Juniper's life. She had a [[Julius Andromeda|brother]], who died. She also went out with [[Fury Axtar|Fury]] and Bryce the night [[Danika Fendyr|Danika]] was murdered. She later became a soloist at the [[Crescent City Ballet]].

## ==
In the coming months after Danika's death, she had called Bryce one night. Bryce had just received thousands of lyric sheets of "''I Just Hooked Up with Someone in the Bathroom, Don't Tell Connor''" and mock album artwork of photos taken to shame her for what happened the night of Danika's death. Juniper, sensing something wrong by the hollowness in Bryce's voice, jumped into a cab to reach her. She didn't dare hang up the whole ride to [[Bryce's Apartment|Bryce's apartment]] and upon arriving only knew where to go because Bryce had mumbled something about sitting on the roof. She stayed with her to help burn the copies and possibly prevented Bryce from committing suicide, and then went back to Bryce's apartment to watch TV. She stayed with her for three days to make sure she was okay.

Juniper gets the chance to be promoted to principal at the Crescent City Ballet. If she gets the promotion, she would be the first faun ever to hold that position. For the summer season she's an understudy for the main roles in two of the ballets and a soloist in all five of them.

Further into the months when Bryce and [[Hunt Athalar|Hunt]] are aligned together, she invites Bryce to [[the White Raven]]. She had not seen her in a while and took the chance to contact her while she also had to meet with other members of the ballet there. Hunt followed and Bryce's mood soured, driving her to leave to the bathroom. She followed, wanting to console Bryce, but when Bryce turned back to walk to the booth the club exploded. Bryce covered Juniper's body to protect her from the debris, only for her to be ripped from under Bryce by [[Ruhn Danaan|Ruhn]]. She was covered in dust and green blood from someone else. Realization hitting her, she ripped herself from Ruhn's grip and started yelling at Bryce. She did it more out of fear than actual anger, because Bryce had not made the [[Drop]] yet. The harm done to her would have been minuscule compared to what could have happened to Bryce. Out of fear, she slapped Bryce while reprimanding her, but gave up on her demands when Hunt and Ruhn made it clear that Bryce was in shock.

## ==
TBA

## Personality
Juniper is sweet and lovely. Usually Juniper is quiet, but when dancing on a night out, she can become quite the exhibitionist. She's sweet and mild, but still shines.

## Physical Description
Juniper has dark brown skin and a dancer's body, with long, striped legs that end in delicate hooves. She also has an exquisite face with curly black hair, from which gentle arching horns protrude on the top of her head. Juniper is beautiful. She has a quicksilver laugh and a husky voice. Though she's thin, she is leanly muscled and very strong.

## Powers & Abilities
* '''Immortality''': Having made the Drop, Juniper is immortal which not only grants her a far greater lifespan, but allows her to live through injuries that not even her accelerated healing would be able to regenerate from.

## Relationships
### [[Fury Axtar]]
It is currently unknown how or where Juniper met Fury, but though they initially appeared to be close friends, the two of them are dating. They even moved in together.
