'''Justinian Gelos''' was a [[Malakh]] and member of [[the 33rd Legion]].

## Biography
### Early Life
Not much is known about Justinian's early life, but he was the youngest member of [[Micah Domitus]]'s [[triarii]] and a member of the 33rd Legion. Micah had never shown much interest in Justinian beyond his popularity with the grunt troops of the 33rd Legion.

###  
[[Hunt Athalar]] asks Justinian to research the [[kristallos]] [[demon]] after Hunt and [[Bryce Quinlan]] meet with [[Ruhn Danaan]] and discuss [[Luna's Horn]].

Justinian was crucified after he was found to be conspiring with Hunt and [[Viktoria Vargos|Viktoria]] against Micah. He fought as his body was held on the crucifix as the iron spikes went into him, and he began screaming at the pain. He was left bleeding out in [[the Comitium]] atrium, his ravaged body spiked to the iron crucifix in its center, and the glass box covered in dripping blood. He was given something to slow his healing, while his wings were cut off and dumped on the marble floor beneath him. Justinian's body tried and failed to heal his taxed lungs. Ultimately, he died and his rotting corpse stayed in the atrium for seven days. His body was then pulled off the crucifix and dumped into the [[Istros River]] for the river beasts to feast on. He received no [[Sailing]] as he was dubbed a traitor of the Republic.

## Personality
Justinian had no problem pushing Hunt, even when it was clear Hunt didn't want to be bothered. He was not normally serious.

## Physical Description
He wore black legionary armor.

## Powers & Abilities
* '''Malakim Physiology''': As an angel, Justinian had physical abilities and enhanced senses that made him far stronger than both [[human]]s and [[Vanir]].
* '''Flight''': Through his Malakim wings, Justinian was able to fly. He was capable of carrying people in his arms while he flew.
* '''Immortality''': Having presumably made the [[Drop]], Justinian was immortal which not only granted him a far greater lifespan, but allowed him to live through injuries that not even his accelerated healing would be able to regenerate from.

## Trivia
* Justinian is a Latin name meaning "just, upright" or "righteous." In Greek mythology, Gelos is the divine personification of laughter.

'''Lehabah''' was a guardian at [[Griffin Antiquities]] and [[Bryce Quinlan]]'s friend.

## Biography
### Early Life
Lehabah was extremely open regarding the fact that she was marked with the letters of slavery only moments after she was born.

At an unspecified point in time, she was purchased by [[Jesiba Roga]] to guard the forbidden books and contraband hidden in the main archive room of [[Griffin Antiquities]]. Lehabah had been guardian of the gallery's [[The Great Library of Parthos|library]] for decades. She befriended Bryce after she got a job there.

## ==
TBA

## Personality
Lehabah was prone to gossip and incurably nosy. She liked to talk and watch trashy dramas instead of focusing on her work at Griffin Antiquities. She did, however, take her job seriously when appropriate and would keep the books in the remains of the Parthos library in line if one tried to escape or misbehave. She was incredibly perceptive and was able to see and understand the true nature of others, such as when she told [[Hunt Athalar|Hunt]] that Bryce "acted mean" but was really "a big softie."

Despite her somewhat flippant behavior, she cared deeply about Bryce and her friends, though she had on occasion admitted that [[Danika Fendyr]] was "mean" to her. She was also somewhat interested in men, showing vague and passing interest in and physical attraction to Hunt and [[Ruhn Danaan]], among others. Lehabah was prim and a bit prudish. She was extroverted.

## Physical Description
Lehabah was tiny in size, no longer than a human hand, and had a tattoo with the letters SPQM on her simmering skin, which had been stamped on her arm within moments of her birth. The slave tattoo was visible amid the simmering flesh of her arm. She tended to change color depending on her emotions, as she was described as turning raspberry red when she was blushing from embarrassment and a bright blue when she became furious and threatening. She'd also give off smoke that swirled around her when she was embarrassed. Lehabah had a tangle of flaming yellow hair that floated and twirled above her head. She looked like a flame given a plump female shape. She had a round chin, dainty toes, and plump arms.

## Powers & Abilities
* '''Sprite Physiology''': As a [[Sprite|fire sprite]], Lehabah was completely made of flames. She was able to change the color of her flames (and presumably the temperature of them as well) based on her moods, ranging from a yellow-orange to raspberry to a cool blue shade. 
* '''Fire''': She was able to project fireballs from her body that was powerful enough to shatter the glass tank of a [[Nøkk]] that had been protected with spells by Jesiba. Her power was an ember compared to the Fae's flames.
* '''Flight''': As a fire sprite, Lehabah possessed some form of flight or levitation.

## Relationships
### [[Bryce Quinlan]]
Lehabah and Bryce were quite close. The sprite found Bryce cold at times and they sometimes had small fights, but overall they cared for each other. Lehabah cared about Bryce so much that she sacrificed her life in the Griffin Antiquities archive to buy Bryce some time to kill [[Micah Domitus]] when he attacked them during the Summit. Likewise, Bryce cared about Lele so much that she added to her already considerable debt with Jesiba in order to purchase her freedom.

### [[Jesiba Roga]]
Jesiba was Lehabah's most recent master and owner. Lehabah complained sometimes about Jesiba's coldness and they weren't particularly close, but there was no conflict or disdain between them.

'''Lorin''' is [[Ruhn Danaan]]'s mother. She was chosen for planned breeding to continue the royal bloodline for the Autumn King, [[Einar Danaan]].

## Biography
Lorin is the daughter of a prominent noble house that had defected from [[Avallen]]'s court. Lorin's mother was a Donnall. When the Autumn King decided after a few centuries of ruling that he wanted an heir, Lorin was selected for one purpose, breeding. Despite not having been given a choice in the matter, Lorin says she did her duty gladly, grateful for the eternal privilege it offered.

Though she was chosen only to sire the King's heir she developed feelings for him that he was unable to reciprocate. After she became pregnant with Ruhn. Einar Danaan kept her in a villa where she has been living ever since. Her son visits her once a week to check on her and the house. It is down the block from Ruhn's villa. Lorin is not queen, or a consort or mate.

## Personality
Lorin has obeyed everyone her entire life. She tells Ruhn she was glad to do her duty to continue the royal line and is grateful for the eternal privilege it offers. She is so meek and unassertive to an almost defenseless degree she doesn't want to inconvenience anyone when she struggles with something. She won't even try to call a repairman or workers to fix her home's appliances. Lorin still holds an unrequited love for Einar.

Lorin is sweet and kind. Ruhn has never heard her speak one ill word about the Autumn King or the life she's been conscripted to. She cares deeply for her son. She is gentle and loving. She isn't even jealous of [[Ember Quinlan]]. There had been so many females before and after Ember, yet none of them had been formally chosen, as she was, to continue the royal bloodline. The few times she has met [[Bryce Quinlan|Bryce]], Lorin was kind, even doting. Lorin has talked about Bryce with an open fondness and love. Lorin seems completely content with her position in life. Ruhn can't decide if he admires his mother for never questioning the gilded cage she lives in, but he does feel a fierce pride in taking after her bloodline.

## Physical Description
Lorin is beautiful, like all the [[Fae]], but with a gentleness to her face. Her deep blue eyes are the same color as Ruhn's and hold a sadness in them. Lorin has a beautiful, delicate face and silken black hair. Despite being two hundred years older than Ruhn, they look the same age. She's always perfectly put together, always pristine, and ready for a visit that will never happen.

## Powers & Abilities
* '''Fae Physiology''': As one of the Fae, Lorin has enhanced senses and physical abilities, including a sense of smell much stronger than that of [[humans]]. She also heals from injuries much faster than a human.
* '''Telepathy''': Due to her heritage, Lorin should be a telepath, meaning she can communicate with people via their minds, also called mind-speaking. However, she can't read their thoughts.
* '''Avallen Magic''': Also due to her heritage, Lorin should have the ability to manipulate shadows. This includes summoning and walking through them, and cloaking herself and others in darkness.
* '''Immortality''': Since she has made the [[Drop]], Lorin is immortal which not only grants her a slowed and expanded lifespan but allows her to survive injuries that not even her accelerated healing would be able to regenerate from.

'''Luna''' is the patron goddess of [[Lunathion]] and favors some members from both [[House of Earth and Blood]] and [[House of Sky and Breath]]. She is the moon goddess.

## Biography
Luna is the moon goddess of [[Midgard]]. She is the sister of [[Solas]], the sun god.

Her priestesses are female virgins who wear white gowns. Acolytes wear a golden waxing crescent moon on a delicate chain across their brow. Upon becoming full-fledged priestesses when they reach maturity, they trade it for the full circle of Luna, or the full moon charm. When they age and fade, they trade the charm for a waning crescent moon. The priestesses all have their own reasons for offering themselves to Luna, but they forsake their lives beyond the temple grounds and embrace the goddess's eternal maidenhood. Just as Luna had no [[mate]] or lover, so would they live.

The cypress tree is the sacred tree of Luna. Thousands were planted in Lunathion in her honor as patron goddess of the city. Both wolves and [[Fae]] claim Luna as their patron goddess. They have gone to war over whom she favored in millennia long past.

## Trivia
* For Lunathion's [[Luna's Temple|temple]], a statue of Luna was crafted from a single block of marble hewn from the famed [[Caliprian Quarries]] by the [[Melanthos River]] up north. The temple was the first thing built upon the city's founding five hundred years ago.
* The old Fae relic [[Luna's Horn]] is named after the goddess.
* Luna is often depicted carrying a bow and with two hunting dogs and a stag and thus shares a stark resemblance to the Greek goddess Artemis or her Roman counterpart Diana.
* [[Lidia Cervos]] is said to look exactly like Luna.

'''Maximus Tertian''' was a [[vampyr]].

## Biography
### Early Life
Maximus was a two-hundred-year-old vampyr, unwed and unmated. He was the son of [[Lord Cedrian]], richest of the [[Pangera|Pangeran]] vamps, and the most monstrous according to rumors. His father shielded him from any fallout from his hideous behavior.

## ==
Maximus met with [[Bryce Quinlan]] at [[the White Raven]] to seal the deal for a five-thousand-year-old onyx bust of a long-dead vampyr lord. The deal had been a triumph for Bryce after weeks of sending out feelers to potential buyers, taunting them with the chance to buy a rare artifact before any of their rivals. She’d had her eye on Maximus, and during their endless phone calls and messages, she’d played him well, drawing upon his hatred for other vampyr lords, his fragile ego, and his unbearable arrogance.

Afterwards, Maximus tried to seduce Bryce into staying with him, causing her to feel rather uncomfortable. Right when she was about to flee, her brother [[Ruhn Danaan]] stepped in and pushes Maximus to leave. Two hours later, Maximus is killed in the same way as [[Danika Fendyr]] and [[the Pack of Devils]] on the outskirts of [[the Meat Market]].

When [[Micah Domitus]] commandered Bryce into investigating Danika's murder, he told her that Maximus and Danika were friends. Bryce automatically did not believe him and was able to discover that Maximus had a girlfriend, a [[draki]] who works at a nail salon in [[the Old Square]]. Bryce was able to gather from the girlfriend that Maximus and Danika were definitely not friends.

Eventually, Bryce and [[Hunt Athalar]] discover that Maximus was killed after being injected with [[Synth]] by Micah, so he would rip himself apart due to a side-effect of the Synth. Maximus was chosen as a target to give an excuse to Micah so he could come to Bryce to bring her into the whole investigation.

## Personality
Maximus was known for being monstrous and cruel. He hated the other vampyr lords. He had a fragile ego and an unbearable arrogance. He was full of male, immortal entitlement. He was a creep who couldn't take no for an answer. He liked to brag about who he knew and what he had.

## Physical Description
Maximus had a bulky frame, long slicked back black hair, a sharp-boned face, and inky eyes. His smile was incredibly smooth and elongated canines.

'''Micah Domitus''' was an [[Malakh|Archangel]] and former Governor of [[Lunathion]].

## Biography
### Early Life
Micah was born into his power and had never known a life as anything but as one of the major forces on the planet. Despite fighting to put down [[Shahar]]'s rebellion, nearly all of his [[triarii]] was made up of [[the Fallen]]. He bought many slaves after the uprising to be part of [[the 33rd Legion]] since they would all be easy to control. The 33rd Imperial Legion serves as his personal army. Two hundred years after the uprising Micah bought [[Hunt Athalar]] from [[Sandriel]] and offered him a bargain: a kill for every life Hunt took on [[Battle of Mount Hermon|Mount Hermon]]. Every Angel he'd slaughtered during the battle, he was to pay back in the form of a death for a death. It was a total toll of two thousand two hundred and seventeen. Despite Hunt not killing that amount of people, Micah told him that because he was Shahar's general of the elite [[The 18th Legion|18th Legion]], he was responsible for everyone's death since he marched them into battle. Once Hunt had completed his task, Micah would consider giving Hunt his freedom back.

###  
Micah was the current governor of Lunathion and one of the Heads of the City. He was also the Archangel of Lunathion and the Head of the [[Central Business District]]. He ruled over all the other Heads of the City. Beyond his city titles, he was Archangel of [[Valbara]], meaning he was the ruler of the entire territory and answerable only to the [[Asteri]] in the [[Eternal City]]. Hunt, as the Umbra Mortis, was Micah's personal assassin. 

Micah went to [[Griffin Antiquities]] with [[Isaiah Tiberian]] and Hunt to order [[Bryce Quinlan]] to investigate [[Danika Fendyr]] and [[the Pack of Devils]]' murder. He also assigned Hunt to assist and guard Bryce. Outside the gallery, Micah told Hunt that if he could solve the murder case before [[the Summit]], he would lower Hunt's debt to ten more lives. Before leaving, Micah also warned Hunt to keep to himself and not to touch Bryce. 

During the Summit, Micah left to visit Griffin Antiquities while Bryce was working and revealed his true nature. He was the true murderer of Danika and the Pack of Devils, and he realized that the tattoo on Bryce's back is actually [[Luna's Horn]]. He told Bryce that he was the one summoning the [[kristallos]] [[Demon|demons]] to hunt for the Horn, and then he pieced it together that the Horn was mixed with ink to be tatted onto her back. He planned to utilize her to open the [[Gates]] of an unknown world to command them, and he injected [[Synth]] into her system to control her and open the [[magic]] of the Horn. However, instead of the drug harming her as he hoped, it only healed her wounds and she ended up killing him and burning his body. Unbeknownst to him, Micah did manage to open a portal in every Gate of the city straight to [[Hel]].

Bryce killed Micah when she shot him in the head with a golden bullet fired from the [[Godslayer Rifle]], then dismembered his body with the [[Fendyr Sword|Fendyr sword]], burned it, vacuumed up his ashes, and dumped them in the bin out back to ensure he didn't regenerate.

## Personality
Micah was seen as a cold and vindictive person who enjoyed being in control of the citizens in his territory. Many knew not to challenge his authority, the exception being Danika and [[Jesiba Roga]]. Micah would sentence people who had done wrong in the eyes of law to a public crucifixion. Nothing was out of reach for him, if he wanted something then he got it. He was "a king among angels and law unto himself, especially in Valbara" because it's so far from the [[Eternal City]]. If he deemed someone a threat or in need of justice, there was no investigation or trial, just his command. 

Micah was also known publicly for generally being a male of progress and tolerance.
* Micah is a Hebrew name meaning "who is like God." Domitius was a plebeian family in Rome. It means "having been tamed."
* When Hunt broke [[Ruhn Danaan]]'s nose, Micah gave him a week "suspension," but one that came with an especially padded paycheck and three deaths taken off his debt.

'''Nathalie''' was [[Danika Fendyr]]'s Third and a member of [[the Pack of Devils]].

## Biography
### Early Life
As a member of the Pack of Devils, Nathalie would have patrolled [[The Old Square|Old Square]] making sure tourists didn't get hurt or come across the wrong person. They also made sure the bars, cafes, music halls, and shops stayed safe.

###  
Nathalie along with the rest of the Pack of Devils, is at [[Danika and Bryce's Apartment|Danika and Bryce's apartment]] after going to [[Ithan Holstrom]]'s [[sunball]] game for Pack Night.

[[Micah Domitus]] hatched a plan to get revenge on Danika and to try and find the [[Luna's Horn|Horn]]. He waited until [[Philip Briggs]], a [[human]] rebel, was released from prison so he could frame him for the pack's deaths. He went to Danika and Bryce's apartment where the Pack of Devils, including Nathalie, were at the time and used his [[magic]] to disable them. He then injected Danika with [[Synth]], which caused Danika to rip her pack apart before turning on herself. Micah then summoned a [[Kristallos]] [[demon]] to cover his traces.

Philip Briggs was then accused of their murders due to him being imprisoned by Danika previously, which led the authorities to believe he did it for revenge. However, Micah admitted to [[Bryce Quinlan|Bryce]] that he was the one to kill Nathalie and the pack.

## Personality
Nathalie was not always particularly nice towards Bryce.

## Physical Description
Nathalie had sleek black hair and onyx-colored eyes.

## Powers & Abilities
### Powers
* '''Shifter Physiology''': Nathalie had enhanced speed, strength, and healing abilities because of her [[Vanir]] status. She had more acute senses and could heal from injuries much faster than a human. She could effortlessly change between her human and animal forms.
* '''Shifting''': Nathalie possessed the ability to transform into a magically enhanced animal form. Her animal form was that of a larger than average wolf. In this form she was stronger, faster, and had keener senses than in her human form.
* '''Immortality''': Having made the [[Drop]], Nathalie was immortal which not only granted her a slowed and expanded lifespan but allowed her to survive injuries that not even her accelerated healing would be able to regenerate from.

### Abilities
* '''Combat Skills''': Nathalie had been trained in various forms of combat and self-defense to an exceedingly high level as a member of the Aux.

## Trivia
* Nathalie's drink of choice was a cloudy, milky-tasting beer that's heavy on the hops.

'''Nesta Archeron''' is a [[Fae]] from the [[w:c:acourtofthornsandroses:Prythian|Fae Homeworld]], whom [[Bryce Quinlan]] meets when she escapes [[Midgard]].

## Biography
:''This character is part of the [[w:c:acourtofthornsandroses:A Court of Thorns and Roses series|A Court of Thorns and Roses series]]. To view this character's full biography, please proceed to [[w:c:acourtofthornsandroses:Nesta Archeron|her page]] in the [[w:c:acourtofthornsandroses:A Court of Thorns and Roses Wiki|A Court of Thorns and Roses Wiki]].''

## ==
After Bryce Quinlan manages to escape the [[Asteri]] by opening a Gate to travel through the worlds to reach [[Hel]] in order to request aid from the Princes, she accidentally lands somewhere in Velaris near the Sidra River instead, carrying with her the [[Gwydion|Starsword]]. [[Azriel]] finds her, blindfolds her, and flies her to a residence, carrying her to the living room.

A moment later, the doors to the foyer open and Amren, Cassian and Nesta appear. Bryce sees Amren first, then has the idea to try to communicate with them in the only other language she knew, the ancient language of the Fae, of the [[Starborn]]. Amren stagers back, surprised to hear her speak the language as no one has spoken it in their world for fifteen thousand years, and the others gape. Amren notices the Starsword and nods to Azriel's knife, [[Truth-Teller]], at his side. The knife is the twin to the sword as it glimmers with dark light in answer to the sword's white light. Amren whispers that the Starsword is actually Gwydion.

Bryce tells Amren she needs to find Prince Aidas or [[Apollion]]. She replies saying she doesn't know them and that their world is not Hel. At this moment, the front door opens again, Feyre enters the room, [[Rhysand]] entering after her. Bryce pleads her case to Amren and she translates to the others. Feyre then asks Bryce for her name, and Amren translates the question. Bryce addresses Rhysand and Feyre noticing they possess an air of authority, and tells them her name. Rhysand steps forward and introduces himself in the Old Language.

## ==
TBA

## Personality
Nesta is protective of her family and her world. She is a fierce warrior and should not be underestimated. Nesta shares some of her history and hints at her traumatic past with Bryce. [[Ember Quinlan]] describes Nesta as "imperious and unbending" in the face of Rhysand's wrath. She is not someone who smiles easily or regularly. She seems young, despite being a warrior and Fae. Bryce describes her as someone "clearly not accustomed to displays of kindness."

## Physical Description
Nesta has golden-brown hair and blue-gray eyes.

## Powers & Abilities

### Powers
* '''Fae Physiology''': Nesta has enhanced senses, physical abilities, and healing abilities that all Fae have. She has the superhuman speed and grace characteristic of the Fae.
* '''Pyrokinesis:''' Nesta has the ability to conjure silver flames which are capable of burning anything in her path, although the magntitude of her power has been greatly reduced since getting it.
* '''Dread Trove Connection:''' Nesta is connected to the [[Dread Trove]] and is one of the few capable of using them, although extensive periods of time can cause her both physical and psychological damage. 

### Abilities
* '''Combat Skills''': During her time as a fae, Nesta has become a capable warrior, particularly in the arts of swordsmanship. Her skills allowed her to  kill  [[Vesperus]], the last living Asteri on the fae homeworld.

## Trivia
* Nesta tells Bryce that seven months ago she killed a contemporary of the Daglan, or Asteri, when she killed [[w:c:acourtofthornsandroses:Lanthys|Lanthys]].

'''Palania''' was a [[human]] tutor.

## Biography
Palania, like generation of her family, was born in [[Parthos]] and taught mathematics and science at [[The Great Library of Parthos]]' academy. She died long before the [[Northern Rift]] opened or the [[Asteri]] walked [[Midgard]].

Millenias later, [[Hecuba Enador]] used her powers to summon three ancient, wise spirits to teach her daughter [[Hypaxia Enador|Hypaxia]]. One for battle and physical training, Palania for mathematics and sciences, and the other for history, reading, and languages. They were Hypaxia's only companions while growing up.

Prince '''Pelias''' was the first [[Starborn]] prince.

## Biography
Pelias originated from the [[w:c:acourtofthornsandroses:Prythian|Fae Homeworld]], where he was a general under High King [[Fionn]] and High Queen [[Theia]], after they defeated their [[Asteri|masters]]. He later helped Theia kill Fionn, so she could reign herself, when he refused to pass his throne onto her before he died - naming his older daughter [[Helena]] as next in line instead. Theia set her sights on conquering other worlds like her former masters did. When the [[Asteri]] were defeated, they left behind some of their [[Dread Trove|weapons]], of which she used the [[Luna's Horn|Horn]] and the [[The Harp|Harp]] to make [[the Crossing]] with some of her people, thus entering [[Midgard]] from her island. Pelias was part of that entourage, but later turned onto Theia with the help of [[Rigelus]]. He killed Theia on the battlefield, took both the [[Starsword]] and the Horn from her, named himself the first [[Starborn]] Prince of Midgard, forced Helena to marry him and raped her in the process to continue the lineage with the Starborn.

Sometime, [[Apollion]] had to steal Pelias's blood to make the [[kristallos]] to hunt the Horn — it contained that essence in order to wield the Horn. Pelias himself wielded the Horn during the First Wars and later on, until he died. He was buried in the [[Cave of Princes]] on [[Avallen]] together with the Starsword.

'''Philip Briggs''' is a [[human]] rebel who was arrested in 15.033 [[V.E.]].

## Biography
### Early Life
At some point during his life Philip Briggs became affiliated with a group of human rebels in [[Lunathion]], the [[Keres]], and rose to become their leader. He has done his best to bring the human rebellion to [[Valbara]]. He was responsible for planning a series of bombings in Lunathion, but was apprehended by [[Danika Fendyr]] and [[the Pack of Devils]] in his bomb lab, located just outside the city walls. A few weeks before his initial arrest, he spoke to Danika, who expressed minor sympathy for his cause and told him to cease his bomb plot or else she would be forced to arrest him, which she didn't want to have to do, but he continued with his plot and was subsequently arrested. In his bomb lab, Danika finds his blueprints for blowing up [[the White Raven]] club.

###  
Danika arrives at [[Griffin Antiquities]] and tells [[Bryce Quinlan]] that Briggs is being released that day due to a technicality. An undescribed loophole in the law allows him to be released despite the myriad of very clear evidence stacked against him. Later that night, Briggs is seen via television being escorted out of prison in a white jumpsuit. Within hours of this he is re-arrested by [[Naomi Boreas]] for murdering Danika and the Pack of Devils, with authorities claiming he had a clear motive and the means to commit the murders. He'd also supposedly been caught purchasing black summoning salts in the weeks before his initial arrest. The [[demon]] who killed Danika and pack would have required the deadly black salt to summon it into [[Midgard]]. After a trial that he described as a "sham," he was imprisoned in [[Adrestia Prison]], an island prison just off the coast of Lunathion, and began to be regularly tortured by law enforcement.

On April 12th 15.035 V.E. he is brought to [[the Comitium]], where [[Hunt Athalar]] and Bryce talk to him. Briggs says that he did not desire revenge against the Pack of Devils or Danika. He tells them he even possesses knowledge that could've proven his innocence, but didn't reveal it out of a desire to protect anyone associated with Danika who might also be sympathetic to the human rebels and out of his regret over having failed his fellow humans by being caught.

## Personality
Philip Briggs' goal is no secret. He wants a global conflict that would cost the lives of millions on either side. He views those lives as expendable if it means a possibility for humans to overthrow their [[Vanir]] oppressors. His bombs were small but deadly. They were designed to trigger a war between humans and Vanir. [[Isaiah Tiberian]] describes him as someone who "hadn't seemed dumb, just a fanatic hell-bent on starting a conflict to mirror the war raging" in [[Pangera]].

## Physical Description
Philip Briggs is a relatively young dark-haired man of average height and build. He has dark blue eyes which Bryce describes as empty and hopeless during their meeting. After he is imprisoned, his hair is buzzed short and he becomes noticeably gaunt.

## Powers & Abilities
As a human, he possessed no [[magic]], but it can be assumed that he was skilled in chemistry and bomb-making.

'''Pollux Antonius''', also known as '''The Hammer''', was the commander of [[Sandriel's Triarii]] until transferring to [[Ephraim]]. He was later transferred to [[Celestina]]'s [[triarii]] at [[Lunathion]] along with [[Baxian Argos]].   

## Biography
### Early Life
Not much is known about Pollux's early life. He was [[Sandriel]]'s triarii commander and her Second. His name, the Hammer, was a reference to being the "smasher of battlefields."

###  
Pollux accompanied Sandriel to Lunathion for the Summit.

###  
With the Archangel [[Micah Domitus|Micah]] gone, Crescent City needs a new Archangel to lead. Hunt and his angel friends anticipate the arrival of the Archangel, [[Celestina]], and are unsure what to expect. Unfortunately, with [[Sandriel's Triarii|Sandriel’s]] death, The Asteri decided it would be best if her [[triarii]] split between Celestina and another Archangel, [[Ephraim]]. Because of this, The Hind (Lidia), [[Pollux Antonius|The Hammer (Pollux)]], [[Baxian Argos|The Hellhound (Baxian)]], and [[the Harpy]] form Celestina’s new triarii. Hunt and some of the other angels have a horrible history with this group. Hunt abhors The Hind and The Hammer especially, and suffered much torture at Sandriel’s command. While tensions are high between Hunt and the new triarii, Celestina seems kind and like she has good intentions. Hunt still does not trust her, though.

Later, the angels are called to assemble by Celestina. Pollux and Baxian got into a public altercation. Baxian attacked Pollux because Pollux was sexually harassing a woman. The brawl caused a scene and made it look like Celestina did not have a good grip on her triarii. Because of this, the Asteri want a happy distraction (and also to keep a tight leash on Celestina). 

###  
Pollux had commandeered [[Mordoc]] and the [[Dreadwolf|dreadwolves]] from [[Lidia Cervos|Lidia]] to find [[Bryce Quinlan]]. He had taken [[the Harpy]]'s death personally and believed that Bryce killed her.

## Personality
Pollux was a cruel male who delighted in carnage, fear, pain, and torture, and was generally was merciless. He was well known for his favorite activities: slaughter and torture. He took cruelty and punishment to new levels.

## Physical Description
Pollux had golden hair, cobalt eyes, tanned skin, and soft, white wings.

## Powers & Abilities
### Powers
* '''Malakim Physiology''': As an [[Malakh|angel]], Pollux had physical abilities and enhanced senses that made him far stronger than both [[Human|humans]] and [[Vanir]]. These abilities had served him well under Sandriel and her triarii, being strong enough to contend with other malakim such as [[Hunt Athalar]] on numerous occasions. Due to his reputation on the battlefield, it can be assumed that he was one of the most physically powerful malakim currently alive.
* '''Flight''': Through his wings, Pollux was able to fly. He was also capable of carrying people in his arms while he flew.
* '''Immortality''': Having made the [[Drop]], Pollux was immortal, which not only had granted him a far greater lifespan, but had allowed him to live through injuries that not even his accelerated healing would have been able to regenerate from.

### Abilities
* '''Combat Skills''': Pollux was trained in various forms of combat and self-defense to an exceedingly high level. This included training in various types of weapons. 
* '''Master Interrogator''': Since Pollux was well-known for his love of torture, he likely had extraordinary interrogation skills.

## Trivia
* The name Pollux comes from the star Pollux, which is the brightest star in the constellation of Gemini. Pollux refers to the twins Castor and Pollux in Greek and Roman mythology.
* Antonius was an important plebeian family of ancient Rome, which includes Marcus Antonius, who claimed the family was descended from Anton, a son of Hercules.

'''Randall Silago''' is [[Bryce Quinlan]]'s step-father, who raised her since she was three years old. He is a legendary war hero and a sharpshooter.

## Biography
### Early Life
Randall took a test so he wouldn't get drafted into the [[Human Conscription Service]], but missed the cutoff by a single point. He was shipped to the front in [[Pangera]] two weeks later. Randall had just gotten back from the front after serving for three years, but the stuff he did and saw while he was serving messed with him. He didn't want to go home and face his family. So he offered himself as an acolyte to [[Solas]] in [[Oia]], hoping that it'd somehow atone for his past. He was two weeks away from swearing his vows when the High Priest asked him to escort [[Ember Quinlan]] and her three-year old daughter to [[Hilene]]. Together they faced a group of [[Fae]] sent by [[Einar Danaan|the Autumn King]] to find Ember and bring back his child.

After that, the [[House of Earth and Blood]] deemed Ember a vessel for [[Cthona]] and Randall a vessel for Solas, which amounted to an official order of protection meaning the Autumn King couldn't do anything. Ember and Randall later got married and raised Bryce together. He trained Bryce to be a sharpshooter and taught her survival tactics. He would talk to Bryce about his battles in Pangera and would whisper when he mentioned the infamous [[Hunt Athalar]].

###

'''Reid Redner''' is a [[human]] and ex-boyfriend of [[Bryce Quinlan]].

## Biography
### Early Life
Not much is known about Reid's early life. He works for his father at [[Redner Industries]], a very successful company that creates products that combine human technology with [[Vanir]] [[magic]]. Reid knew [[Danika Fendyr]] casually through the part-time security work she did for his father’s business, and they both had been in the same presentation one afternoon. Since Danika's best friend and roommate, Bryce, had been single and complaining constantly about it, Danika had given her number to Reid in a last-ditch effort to preserve her own sanity, and Bryce and Reid started dating soon after.

###  
He was first mentioned by Danika in the kitchen of [[Danika and Bryce's Apartment|Danika and Bryce's apartment]], when she reminded Bryce that the latter had a date with him, referring to him as "what's his name," that night, a date that Bryce was not very enthusiastic about attending. Bryce and Reid had been dating for two months and slept together twice. During this conversation, Danika and her Second, [[Connor Holstrom]], who had a huge crush on Bryce, voiced their displeasure about Bryce's relationship with him, the latter more so than anyone. Bryce, not eager to go on this date, spent only a third of the time she normally spent getting ready to prepare and took two shots of gin before she left.

She arrived at the restaurant, [[Pearl and Rose]], and waited for forty-five minutes for Reid to show up and began to worry that she was about to be stood up as she couldn't afford the fancy wine she had ordered while she waited. He eventually arrived without an apology or an excuse, only remarking that he got caught up in a long phone call with the [[Malakh|Malakim]] leadership about the human-Vanir conflict in Pangera after Bryce asked for an explanation. The two briefly discussed the conflict and whether or not it would come over to [[Valbara]] before Reid, irritated, stated that it was a "complicated issue" and Bryce dropped the subject.

The two sat in silence while they looked at the menu until Bryce's phone buzzed, which seemed to irritate Reid even more, though he did not hesitate to begin texting on his cell phone once she checked to see who was contacting her. Bryce's message had been from Danika, urging her to dump Reid and "put Connor out of his misery" by dating him instead, which she was initially going to ignore before she realized that she really was not having a good time. 

When she tried to get Reid's attention, he didn't look up from his phone, only raised his index finger as a silent "hold on." Their food arrived, and he continued his text conversation, only speaking to give Bryce permission to start eating without him, which she did. She then informed him that she was about to go, which he said was fine due to the fact that he believed she was excusing herself to go to the ladies' room, forcing her to reiterate that she was leaving before thanking him for dinner. When he began to protest, she simply replied that this wasn't working out before she said her goodbyes and wished him good luck with his work. She then took the bottle of expensive wine from the table and left with it, swigging straight from the bottle on her way out the door.

## Personality
Reid has the easy manners of someone brought up with money, education, and no doors locked to his desires. He is very self-absorbed, narcissistic, and cared little about the lives and needs of the people around him, even that of his then-girlfriend, Bryce. This was evidenced by the fact that he showed up forty-five minutes late to their date at the Pearl and Rose and spent most of his time on his cell phone even once he showed up. He is addicted to his phone. 

Bryce's best friend Danika, who set them up in the first place, made it a point of teasing her about the fact that Reid barely waited until he and Bryce were done having sex before he began checking his cell phone, indicating that he saw the people around them as only as valuable as what they were providing him. Bryce had also mentioned that while their sexual relationship was "fine," and she had to "work for it," also implying that he was more concerned about his own pleasure than that of his partner.

## Physical Description
Reid has golden skin and dark brown hair. According to Bryce, he is "hot as Hel." Every tie he wears is specifically selected to bring out the green in his hazel eyes, and his suits are always impeccably cut to his toned body.

## Powers & Abilities
As a human, Reid has no known supernatural powers. His power in life comes from his wealth and status as the heir to Redner Industries, making him one of the few humans in [[Lunathion]] who is generally immune to the generalization that humans are inferior believed by most Vanir. He worked out with a physical trainer regularly, both to keep his physique in attractive shape, but also to ensure that he was able to physically defend himself and run quickly in case he ran into trouble with human-hating Vanir.

## Relationships
### [[Bryce Quinlan]]
Reid and Bryce were in a brief relationship that seemed to be more about sex than romance for both parties. Bryce was dating him because she didn't want to date a Vanir who would "demand a three-day sex marathon and then call her his mate, lock her in his house, and never let her out again." Bryce eventually tired of his inattention and belief that he was entitled to sex with her in exchange for dinner at fancy, expensive restaurants and dumped him after she was asked on a date by Connor Holstrom, who had crushed on her for years and who seemed to genuinely care about her.

'''Rhysand''' is a [[Fae]] from the [[w:c:acourtofthornsandroses:Prythian|Fae Homeworld]], whom [[Bryce Quinlan]] meets when she escapes [[Midgard]].

## Biography
:''This character is part of the [[w:c:acourtofthornsandroses:A Court of Thorns and Roses series|A Court of Thorns and Roses series]]. To view this character's full biography, please proceed to [[w:c:acourtofthornsandroses:Rhysand|his page]] in the [[w:c:acourtofthornsandroses:A Court of Thorns and Roses Wiki|A Court of Thorns and Roses Wiki]].''

## ==
After Bryce Quinlan manages to escape the [[Asteri]] by opening a Gate to travel through the worlds to reach [[Hel]] in order to request aid from the Princes, she accidentally lands somewhere in Velaris near the Sidra River instead, carrying with her the [[Gwydion|Starsword]]. [[Azriel]] finds her, blindfolds her, and flies her to a residence, carrying her to the living room. Bryce is there for a few moments with Azriel, Amren, Cassian and [[Nesta Archeron|Nesta]], until Feyre and Rhysand arrive. Feyre enters the room first, Rhysand entering after her. When Bryce sees Rhysand she gasps and calls out her brother's name, [[Ruhn Danaan|Ruhn]], remarking on their similarities, eyes the same shade of violet blue, hair of the same gleaming black and the same face and posture as her brother although Rhysand's skin was browner. Rhysand only stares at her, also noticing the sword. Bryce pleads her case to Amren and she translates to the others. Feyre then asks Bryce for her name, and Amren translates the question. Bryce addresses Rhysand and Feyre noticing they possess an air of authority, and tells them her name. Rhysand steps forward, smiling slightly and introduces himself in the Old Language saying: "Hello, Bryce Quinlan. My name is Rhysand."

'''Rigelus''' was the head of the [[Asteri]].

## Biography
### Early Life
TBA

###  
Rigelus first appeared over video call with the other Asteri before the [[Valbara]]n Summit. He, along with his siblings, formally recognized [[Hypaxia Enador]] as the Queen of the Valbaran [[Witch|Witches]]. They expressed their wish for the attendees of the Summit to discuss a way to end the war and then depart, though not before Rigelus took note of [[Hunt Athalar]] staring straight at him.

After [[Bryce Quinlan]] revealed her status as a very powerful [[Starborn]] [[Fae]] while protecting the city of [[Lunathion]] from an infestation of [[demons]], Rigelus called her over the phone to thank her for saving the city and apologized for nearly destroying it with the Asteri's brimstone missiles, although Bryce recognized that his apology is a cover-up for the fact that the Asteri were attempting to kill her. He granted Hunt his permanent freedom, complete with a "C" for freedom marked over his brand, and surreptitiously threatened Bryce's mother in order to remind her to stay in her lane and not cause trouble for the Asteri.

###  
At the very end of House of Sky and Breath, Rigelus captures [[Hunt Athalar]], [[Ruhn Danaan]], and [[Baxian Argos]] and places them in the Asteri Dungeon. Before being led there, Rigelus places the slave halo back around Hunt's forehead and removes the "C" marked on his wrist, making him a slave again to the Asteri.

###  
Rigelus uses [[the Hawk]] and [[Pollux Antonius]] to torture infomation out of Hunt, Ruhn, and Baxian. Rigelus continues to hunt for [[Bryce Quinlan]] after her other wordly departure. 

## Personality
Rigelus is cold, calulated, and manipulative. He embodies the parasitic nature of the Asteri regumine. His calm and cool exterior makes him a formidable power.

## Physical Description
Like all members of the Asteri, Rigelus' true form was unknown and often required a host body to contain his power which wasted away as time passed due to the strain. 

He inhabited the body of a slim and lanky teenage Fae boy, with cold eyes, a delicate chin, and a chilling smile. He is known to have short, dark hair and skinny fingers.

## Powers & Abilities
* '''Fae Physiology''': As he inhabited the body of a Fae, it can be presumed that Rigelus had enhanced senses, physical abilities, and healing abilities because of a Fae's [[Vanir]] status. As this was not his true form, it can also be presumed that Rigelus' power surpassed that of all Vanir. The Asteri were strong enough to contend with the Princes of [[Hel]].
* '''Firstlight and Secondlight Manipulation''': Like all the Asteri, Rigelus could manipulate the power of [[firstlight]] and [[secondlight]] to achieve God-like feats. This resulted in the people of [[Midgard]] believing that the Asteri housed the power of a star within themselves. A fraction of his power was enough to easily incapacitate Hunt when he attempted to cover Bryce's escape.
* '''Energy Absorption''': Like all the Asteri, Rigelus consumed firstlight and secondlight to increase his power to that of a god. However, it was alluded to that this power was not infinite and without regular consumption, his powers would have weakened over time.
* '''Body Jumping''': Like all the Asteri, Rigelus could transfer his own essence into the body of others. But due to the nature of his power, the host body would eventually waste away from the toll.
* '''Enchantments''': While not as adept as witches, Rigelus had significant knowledge and skill in casting magical enchantments. An example being the "halo" tattoos he placed on prisoners to bind their powers. According to Hunt, the "halos" tattooed by Rigelus felt different than other ones, as if they were alive.
* '''Shapeshifting''': Rigelus could shapeshift into anyone of his choosing with perfect mimicry. This was seen when he disguised himself as [[Aidas]] to steer Bryce and Hunt into the rebellion and grow their powers with neither one of them being able to tell him apart from the real Aidas.

## Trivia
* The name Rigelus is a Latinization of the name "Rigel," which comes from the Arabic "الرجل" (al-Rijl), meaning "foot." Rigel is usually the brightest star in the Orion constellation (though sometimes outshone by Betelguese), and is dubbed "the foot of Orion," as it is at the base of the constellation.

He and Bryce once were close, but after a fight about 10 years ago in which Ruhn angrily called Bryce a "half-breed sl*t," Bryce stopped speaking to him unless necessary. Because Bryce wasn't an acknowledged daughter of the Autumn King, she doesn't want anyone to know of her paternity. Ruhn goes along with the explanation that Bryce is a distant cousin. Ruhn is extremely protective of her, much to Bryce's annoyance.

After Bryce finds [[Danika Fendyr]] and [[the Pack of Devils]] murdered in her and Danika's [[Danika and Bryce's Apartment|apartment]], she chases the [[demon]] she found fleeing the building and saves an [[Malakh|angel]] who was brutally attacked by the demon on the street. She is taken into protective custody by [[The 33rd Legion|the 33rd]] and questioned about the night's events, but before they can really get many answers from her, they’re informed to release her. Command relays that "Ruhn Danaan called, he’s angry the [[House of Sky and Breath]] wasn’t notified" because the attack on Bryce technically falls under Fae jurisdiction. They're told they can't question her or hold her in custody without the permission and presence of the Autumn King or one of his representatives. The 33rd releases her but Ruhn shows up minutes later, bypassing security with his shadow powers, and demands that they hand her over. [[Hunt Athalar]] tells him they let her go five minutes ago and Ruhn leaves.

Two years later, Ruhn shows up at [[the White Raven]] just as Bryce is about to be assaulted by [[Maximus Tertian]]. His presence alone is enough to distract Maximus so that Bryce can slip away from him. Ruhn is there to tell Bryce, who has the reputation of being a wild party girl, that the Autumn King wants her to keep a low profile until after [[the Summit]] so as not to embarrass the Autumn King when he has to meet with the [[Asteri]], Archangels, and other Vanir leaders. Unbeknownst to Ruhn or anyone else, Bryce has stopped drinking and partying since Danika's murder.

Ruhn texts Bryce and asks her to meet at [[Luna's Temple]] to talk because there are no cameras there. He tells Bryce the Autumn King has tasked him with finding [[Luna's Horn]]. The Autumn King thinks Ruhn can find the Horn because his [[Starborn]] power was woven into the Horn by Prince [[Pelias]], the first Starborn Prince.

Hunt and Bryce show up at Ruhn's house after being caught in a bombing at the White Raven. Ruhn tells Bryce the Autumn King knows they're looking for the Horn together and insists that Bryce needs round-the-clock protection, which Ruhn has volunteered for. She declines and says Hunt will do, since he's already watching her all the time thanks to [[Micah Domitus|Micah]] and the investigation. Ruhn then asks her for access to [[Jesiba Roga|Jesiba]]'s underground [[The Great Library of Parthos|library]] to search for information on the Starborn gifts and their connection to the Horn. She agrees on the condition that he gets Declan to use his hacking skills to help her investigate. 

In the library, Ruhn finds a book that talks about the history of Prince Pelias and his bride Lady [[Helena]]. It says that the Horn worked only for the Starborn and required to be filled with the Starborn power to be used. The Horn, however, is broken and Fae legend claims it can only be repaired by '"light that is not light, [[magic]] that is not magic."'

Ruhn goes to see a [[Witch|medwitch]] about the case to ask if she could think of any way a magical object might be fixed that people haven’t tried yet. The unnamed medwitch says she doesn’t know, but maybe with modern healing technology it could be possible. He asks if she has any idea what "Light that is not light, magic that is not magic" could be, to which she answers that she does not.

He goes back to the unnamed medwitch days later and asks what she knows about a drug called [[Synth]]. She tells him about its properties as a synthetic form of healing magic but that it's addictive in high doses and dangerous. She provides him with some published research on the drug and asks him to please send her some samples of [[kristallos]] venom if he finds any in the murder investigations because she is researching its use in an antidote for a Synth overdose. Ruhn shares this information with Bryce and Hunt, which eventually leads to Bryce going to see the medwitch about her leg.

When Micah punishes Hunt by returning him to [[Sandriel]], Bryce publicly offers to buy Hunt for 97 million gold marks and her Archesian Amulet. Sandriel declines, melting the amulet. Bryce offers to trade herself for Hunt's freedom, and Sandriel thinks about it, musing how valuable it would be to have control of the Autumn King's only daughter, publicly revealing Bryce's secret. Ruhn appears, panicked and furious, and publicly lays claim to her as his sister and thus by Fae law property of the Autumn King. Therefore blocking Sandriel from taking possession of Bryce. After Sandriel leaves with Hunt in chains, Bryce tells Ruhn he's dead to her and deletes his contact info from her phone. 

Ruhn attends the Summit with his father. There he reveals to Hunt that he can mind-speak; a secret he has kept from everyone, including his father. Hunt thanks him for saving Bryce from Sandriel. When the new Witch Queen is called forward to be formally presented and recognized by the Asteri, Ruhn realizes she is the medwitch he spoke with about synth reseatch and the Horn.  

When Jesiba Roga interrupts a Summit session after receiving an S.O.S text from Bryce and broadcasts her security feed to the attendees of the Summit, it's revealed that Micah lied to Sandriel about having to attend a different meeting and is instead at Jesiba's [[Griffin Antiquities|gallery]] to interrogate Bryce and try to force her to give him the Horn. Ruhn immediately dispatches the Aux to the gallery to save his sister, because he is over an hour away by angel's flight and can't save her himself. He can do nothing but sit and watch as Micah admits to being behind Danika's murder before attacking Bryce in an attempt to use the Horn. He is also a witness to the video feed when Bryce uses the Godkiller gun to kill Micah.  

At the Summit, Ruhn and Hunt use mind-speak to communicate their fear of what will happen to Bryce for what she did to Micah, but Declan patches into the video feeds of the city and realise that Micah’s experiment with the Horn didn’t fail, as portals open at each of the [[Gates]] and demons come pouring out of [[Hel]] into the city. They watch Bryce battle her way through the city, trying to save people from the demons and get them to the security shelters. She is soon joined by [[Ithan Holstrom]] and other wolves, against [[Sabine Fendyr]]'s orders.  

Everyone at the Summit is powerless and just watch as the city is torn apart and Bryce shoves Ithan ahead of her through the small gap in the door as the last shelter closes. Bryce calls Hunt's phone, now in Sandriel's pocket, and Hunt asks Sandriel to let him answer it. On the call, Bryce tells him to tell her parents she loves them and to apologize to her friends for things she did. She also asks him to tell Ruhn she forgave him for his insult a while ago and that she's sorry from hiding the truth from him because she didn't want to take his title from him. She then reveals what she means by this by using her hidden Starborn power, much stronger than Ruhn's, to close one of the Gates.

## Personality
Ruhn is often seen to be both funny and sarcastic, but also very protective of his friends and especially his sister, Bryce. He is kind, as seen with his treatment of his mother and the three [[Sprite|sprites]] he has allowed to stay in his home. Ruhn is also an open-minded person, thinking beyond the traditional Fae beliefs, which has led him to accept Ithan and Hunt as well as to rebel against the Asteri themselves. He is disgusted by Fae hierarchies and politics. He also hates that everything he has is bankrolled by his father. Despite being trained to be a lethal, efficient killer, Ruhn doesn't enjoy being a warrior. His Starborn power is just about the only part of himself he likes.

### [[Einar Danaan]]
The relationship between Einar and his son was strained at best. Einar depicted Ruhn to be a failure and did a better job at ignoring him than caring for him. As a child, Ruhn desperately wished for his father to care about him. Einar would abuse Ruhn multiple times during his childhood, using his fire magic to burn Ruhn as punishment. The scars never healed, so Ruhn has tattooed over them. Upon returning from Avallen and enduring his Ordeal, Ruhn moved out of his father's house. As Ruhn possesses the Starsword, it provides him some leverage against his father. Einar's treatment of Bryce opened Ruhn's eyes to his father's true nature and Einar has never forgotten this. Einar resents Ruhn for having shadow magic.

### [[Lorin]]
Ruhn is shown to have a very affectionate relationship with his mother. As shown, Ruhn is constantly worried for his mother, particularly due to her non-assertive and meek personality, with him often visiting her home to make sure that all of her appliances and house is in good condition and him arranging to have any of them fixed should they be broken and Lorin too shy to call or ask for anyone to help due to not wanting to bother others.

As shown, Ruhn can be frustrated with his mother at times, although he himself never shows it in front of her. This can be seen where he internally laments his mother's contendness with her lot in life, particularly of her negative treatment from Einar and of how she’s not  bothered or hurt by the fact that she was essentially only used to breed the Autumn King an Heir and then abandoned. Greatly frustrating Ruhn as his mother has repeatedly shown to not only be not bothered by it, but actually feels honored at being "chosen" to continue the Royal Line of the King, pressuring Ruhn even further due to his prophecy.

'''Sabine Fendyr''' was the former Prime Apparent of the [[Valbara|Valbaran]] wolves, Alpha of the [[Scythe Moon Pack]], and mother of [[Danika Fendyr]].

## Biography
### Early Life
Sabine grew up with her oler brother [[Lars Fendyr|Lars]], who originally had been Prime Apparent. At some point Sabine challenged him and won the title for herself. It was believed that after he lost to Sabine, she killed her brother. Instead, she sent him into exile. Because Lars and his wife had no pack, they roamed the tundra of [[Nena]] with their eleven children. Since then, Sabine has been the heir of her father, the [[Fendyr Prime|Prime]] of the Valbaran wolves. Her daughter Danika was officially second in line to the title. Sabine became also the Alpha of the Scythe Moon Pack, which is chief among the [[shifter]] units in [[Lunathion]]'s [[Auxiliary]].

###  
Sabine and her father attend the meeting of the Heads of the City to discuss the release from prison of [[human]] rebel [[Philip Briggs]]. He has found a loophole that has allowed him to be freed. While at the meeting, Sabine publicly humiliates and blames Danika for the two [[Crescent City University]] human research students who got killed near [[Luna's Temple]] during the recent blackout. She tells Danika their deaths are her fault because it happened during her shift and in her section of the city. Sabine orders the Scythe Moon Pack to watch Philip Briggs.

###  
TBA

###  
Sabine kills the prime. Shortly after, Ithan Holstrom kills Sabine by biting her neck. Upon her death, Ithan becomes the Prime.

## Personality
Sabine was a cold, petty, ambitious, and cruel female with a narcissistic streak who only cared about her position of power and the image placed upon herself as a result of others' actions. This behavior had been one of the main strains between her and her daughter's relationship up until her death, which only amplified when Danika had officially been given the title of next in line as the Prime of all Wolves instead of her.

Sabine was ruthless and smart and was not afraid to make examples out of those who disappointed her, her daughter included, and held a deep prejudice towards other [[Vanir]] and humans, as shown with her attitude towards [[Bryce Quinlan|Bryce]] whom she refered to as a "half-breed trash." She wasn't afraid to challenge other members of authority regardless of the different Houses. [[Hunt Athalar|Hunt]] stated that Sabine "wore every emotion on her face and never once bothered to hide it." Despite this, Sabine seemingly only cared for a few people, particularly those in her family, such as her aging father and Danika, even if it may have seemed fleeting. 

## Physical Description
Sabine was described as looking barely older than her daughter with a fine-boned face, long corn-silk silvery blond hair, and blue eyes. Unlike Danika, Sabine had made the [[Drop]]. Though it was never seen, it can be assumed that Sabine's wolf form was much bigger than regular wolves and had a similar grey fur color like Danika.

## Powers & Abilities
* '''Shifter Physiology''': Sabine had enhanced speed, strength, and healing abilities because of her Vanir status. She had more acute senses and could heal from injuries much faster than a human. She could effortlessly change between her human and animal form.
* '''Shifting''': Sabine possessed the ability to transform into a magically enhanced animal form. Her animal form was that of a larger than average wolf. In this form, she was stronger, faster, and had keener senses than in her human form. She was shown to be a particularly skilled shifter, able to perform partial transformations such as revealing her claws and fangs in her human form.
*'''Immortality''': Since Sabine has made the Drop, Sabine was immortal which not only granted her a slowed and expanded lifespan but allowed her to survive injuries that not even her accelerated healing would be able to regenerate from.

## Relationships
### [[Danika Fendyr]]
The relationship between mother and daughter had been a strained one. Sabine was the Prime Apparent of the Valbaran wolves and had been her aging father's heir for centuries and Danika had officially been second in line to the title. But for years there had been whispers that Danika should be tapped to be the Prime Apparent, bypassing her mother. Sabine's father had given his granddaughter their family’s heirloom [[Fendyr Sword|sword]] after centuries of promising it to Sabine only upon his death. The blade had called to Danika on her eighteenth birthday like a howl on a moonlit night, the Prime had said to explain his unexpected decision. On top of that, Danika had been expected to claim a sea of power that would put her ranking far past Sabine’s after her Drop. 

However, despite their strained relationship, Sabine still showed some semblance of love for her daughter as she was distraught over her death, and became enraged by Bryce's involvement in Danika's life. Her love for Danika was further emphasized when she was outraged and heartbroken at Bryce's and Hunt's accusations for killing her own daughter to become the Prime Apparent. She even admitted that she was protecting Danika from the authorities when she stole the [[Luna's Horn|Horn]] by tampering with the security footage. She was also fully aware that Danika's soul was present with Bryce when she made the Drop, even tearfully reciting the same quote found on Bryce's tattoo, "''Through love, all is possible,"'' during Bryce's final moments with her daughter back at the Summit.

'''Sandriel''' was an [[Malakh|Archangel]], Governor of the northwestern quadrant of [[Pangera]], and [[Hunt Athalar]]'s former master.

## Biography
### Early Life
Sandriel and her identical twin sister [[Shahar]] were born to two prominent Archangels in Pangera as the result of an experiment conducted by the [[Asteri]] to find out what would happen when they crossed the bloodlines of two incredibly powerful Archangels. The official record states that their parents were killed by an assassin. Sandriel and Shahar became Archangels when their parents' titles passed to them upon their deaths. Shahar, however, was convinced that Sandriel had killed their parents and framed the assassin so that the sisters could rule without ''interference''. Shahar rebelles against her fellow Archangels and the Asteri because of this. She'd wanted a world free of rigid hierarchies, but she'd also wanted her sister to pay for killing their parents. Despite this, Sandriel believed Hunt had lured Shahar into the rebellion and that it was Hunt's fault that she had to kill her sister.

She was Hunt's third owner after the defeat of [[the Fallen]] at the [[Battle of Mount Hermon]]. She was arrogant enough to believe that despite the two Archangels before her who had failed, she would be the one to break him. First Sandriel tortured Hunt in her horror show of a dungeon, then she pitted him against warriors who never stood a chance in her blood-soaked arena in the heart of [[Ravilis]]. Then Sandriel commanded Hunt to do what he did best, ending lives. Hunt had done most of the assassinating for Sandriel for the fifty-three years she'd possessed him. She enjoyed nothing more than strolling through her castle dungeons, which were crammed full of [[human]] rebels, and selecting ones to put in her arena. The arena was just for her pleasure of destroying prisoners in various ways. She would host battles to the death, hold public torture events, and unleash Lowers and basic animals against the prisoners.  

Four years ago, Archangel [[Micah Domitus]] purchases Hunt from Sandriel for eighty-five million gold marks. 

###  
Sandriel is coming to [[Lunathion]] on the Asteri's orders for the Summit. Micah has been told to host her as his "guest" for a month. She is bring [[Pollux Antonius]] with her.

## Personality
Sandriel was a cruel female who delighted in pain and bloodshed and despair, with a tenacity for mocking her victims and torturing them, such as the prisoners in her dungeons. There was no end to her creativity regarding ways to hurt, torture, and kill. Hunt described Sandriel as a psychopath. She was arrogant. She looked down upon other [[Vanir]] whom she doesn't see value in, and is very much dismissive of ongoing programs affecting their individual communities. Despite her power-hungry nature, Sandriel is also very strategic and intelligent, capable of finding out crucial information about those she wishes to learn more of efficiently, such as learning of [[Bryce Quinlan|Bryce]]'s familial ties to [[Einar Danaan|the Autumn King]]. As noted by Hunt, Sandriel was also an avid gambler and the reason why she had to sell him was due to the large debt she accumulated gambling.

## Physical Description
Sandriel had thin arms, full lips, and sharp brown eyes that were honed by a millennia of cruelty and near-unchecked power. Being Shahar's twin, Sandriel looked near identical to her sister, and had possessed white wings like all Archangels.

## Powers & Abilities
* '''Malakim Physiology''': Sandriel had physical abilities and enhanced senses that made her far stronger than both humans and Vanir. Due to her Archangel status, it can be presumed that she was stronger than other malakim, with only other fellow Archangels rivaling her strength.
* '''Flight''': Through her wings, Sandriel was able to fly. She was also capable of carrying people in her arms while she flies.
* '''Wind Magic''': Sandriel possessed the ability to manipulate their air around and create large gusts of wind. Due to her Archangel status, this ability can be presumed to have been highly destructive, as Sandriel was capable of warding off [[Ruhn Danaan|Ruhn]] with very little effort before he could approach her. She was even capable of breaking Ruhn's own shadows with a simple flick of her power.
* '''Raw Power''': Like other Archangels, Sandriel possessed a form of raw power that she'd used to effortlessly melt Bryce's Archesian amulet in her grip.
* '''Immortality''': Having made the [[Drop]], Sandriel was immortal which not only had granted her a far greater lifespan, but had allowed her to live through injuries that not even her accelerated healing would be able to regenerate from. 

## Trivia
* It was Sandriel's forces that Hunt had decimated at the Battle of Mount Hermon. Sandriel had been Shahar's prime target and ordered Hunt to take her out by whatever means necessary.

'''Sathia Flynn''' is a [[Valbara]]n [[Fae]], sister of [[Tristan Flynn]], and wife of [[Tharion Ketos]].

## Biography
### Early Life
Not much is known about Sathia's life, but she grew up with her parent's expectations of marrying soon and carrying children for their bloodline. During her childhood, she was friends with [[Colin McCarthy]], who later became a guard in the [[The Hawthorne Villa|Flynn household]]. At some point, he and Sathia started a romantic relationship. Unbeknownst to her, her father [[Padraig Flynn]] would not let Colin court Sathia. Padraig introduced Colin to [[the Viper Queen]] and somehow got him to become one of her drugged-out guards and assassins. Padraig lied to Sathia and told her Colin had left because he had found a new position in [[Korinth]]. It was a difficult time for Sathia, so much so, that Colin leaving was her [[Ordeal]].

###  
Sathia was visiting [[Ruhn's House]] to inform her brother and his friends of [[Cormac Donnall]]'s arrival in [[Lunathion]] and his declaration of making [[Bryce Quinlan]] his bride. Even though Tristan, [[Ruhn Danaan|Ruhn]], and [[Declan Emmet|Declan]] already knew of this, Sathia wanted to discuss this engagement further. She was thrown out by her brother instead. Later, she was present at [[Celestina]]'s and [[Ephraim]]'s ceremony when Bryce declared her broken-off engagement with Cormac.

###  
Along with her parents, Sathia evacuated Lunathion and fled to [[Avallen]]. There, her parents and [[Morven Donnall]] tried to marry her off to either [[Duncan Donnall]] or his brother [[Seamus Donnall|Seamus]], which Sathia refused. When Tharion Ketos spontaneously offered to marry him instead, she accepted. The ceremony was fast and unexciting. She then accompanied Tharion when he went back to Lunathion to meet [[the River Queen]] and asking her to shelter innocents from the city. Later they encountered the Viper Queen and Sathia found Colin in her entourage. He was drugged-out and under her influence. It almost broke Sathia's heart and later, after the [[Asteri]] were defeated, she left Tharion to go find and help Colin.

## Personality
Ruhn describes Sathia as having the rotten soul of a [[Reaper]] and being a professional snob. She often appears to be cold and controlled in her manners, but admits that underneath she knew what her life entailed; her father marrying her off, and she needed a way to ward off suitors he presented to her. Sathia appears to love holding court amid Fae male nobility. She has been raised to "bring honor to her family name." Her real personality seems softer, kinder, and even more timid than everyone had been led to believe before, but she is still resilient and independent. She is pure predator at heart. Nothing and no one scares her.

## Physical Description
Sathia is beautiful and petite. She has dyed, silken black hair that is naturally light brown. She has a pretty, heart-shaped face and green eyes. She has tan skin. Due to her family's wealth and her upbringing, Sathia wears little makeup as well-bred Fae females are supposed to do and she dresses immaculately. She wears solid sapphire studs in her pointed ears.

## Powers & Abilities
* '''Fae Physiology''': As one of the Fae, Sathia has enhanced senses and physical abilities, including a sense of smell much stronger than that of [[Human|humans]]. 
* '''Earth Magic''': Sathia has Earth [[magic]], just like her brother and father, and when Avallen is liberated, she grows berry bushes with a touch.
* '''Immortality''': Since she presumably has made the [[Drop]], Sathia is immortal, which not only grants her a slowed and expanded lifespan but allows her to survive injuries that not even her accelerated healing would be able to regenerate from.

## Relationships
### [[Colin McCarthy]]
Colin was her childhood friend and ex-boyfriend. Because he was poor, her father had sold him to the Viper Queen and lied to Sathia about him leaving Lunathion. It broke Sathia's so much, that the loss of Colin was her Ordeal. When she found out he was alive and in the employ of the Viper Queen, Sathia decided to find and help him.

### [[Tharion Ketos]]
Sathia married Tharion out of convenience. Her family sought asylum from the Asteri in Avallen and her father allowed Morven to hold Sathia hostage until she wed. Morven and Lord Hawthorne wanted her to choose one of Morven's nephews, Seamus or Duncan, but she refused. Bryce and all her friends show up to Avallen, and seeing another person in a bad place, Tharion stepped up and offered to marry her, knowing that it would help both of them out of tough situation. Sathia agreed and they wed five minutes later.

## Trivia
* Sathia is a decade younger than Tristan.
* At one time, Sathia suspected a maid stole her jewelry.

'''Shahar''', formerly referred to as the '''Daystar''', was an [[Malakh|Archangel]], the leader of the uprising against the [[Imperial Senate]], and [[Hunt Athalar]]'s lover.

## Biography
### Early Life
Shahar and her twin sister [[Sandriel]] were born in the southeastern regions of [[Pangera]] to two prominent Archangels as the result of an experiment conducted by the [[Asteri]] to find out what would happen when they crossed the bloodlines of two incredibly powerful Archangels. The official record states that their parents were killed by an assassin. Shahar and Sandriel became Archangels when their parents' titles passed to them upon their deaths. Shahar, however, was convinced that Sandriel had killed their parents and framed the assassin so that the sisters could rule without ''interference''. Shahar rebelled against her fellow Archangels and the Asteri because of this. She'd wanted a world free of rigid hierarchies, but she'd also wanted her sister to pay for killing their parents. 

Shahar initiated her campaign against the Imperial Senate, leading [[the 18th Legion]], her most elite soldiers. Amidst the rebellion, she had met Hunt who worked his way up the ranks of her legions and fell in love with her vision for the world and eventually her. Seeing value in the unique lightening powers Hunt possessed, Shahar had utilized Hunt's skills well in battle and made him her general of the 18th Legion before becoming his lover. Hunt became High Commander of all Shahar's legions. He spent five years with Shahar and the 18th. 

In the final days of the rebellion, Shahar pushed her efforts to destroy Sandriel at [[Battle of Mount Hermon|Mount Hermon]], with Shahar ordering Hunt to take her twin sister out by any means necessary. However, the battle was short-lived and resulted in the defeat of the 18th Legion and capture of their survivors. Shahar had personally attacked Sandriel herself, and despite her efforts, Sandriel killed Shahar, finally ending the rebellion. 

After the rebellion, Shahar's body was taken back to the [[Crystal Palace]], the home of the Asteri in the [[Eternal City]], where Sandriel's second-in-command, [[Pollux Antonius]] urinated over her corpse. Her wings were stripped from her body and were hung above the thrones of the Asteri alongside the other angels who died for her cause. The survivors, including Hunt, were labelled as "[[The Fallen|fallen]] angels" and as punishment their [[magic]] was bound and limited by "halo" tattoos on their head. As such, they had lost their rights and were passed around to different Archangels as slaves for eternity for their transgressions. Forty years later, the [[Human|humans]] began their own [[Ophion|rebellion]] against the Imperial Senate for their freedom from the rigid hierarchies in the northern reaches of Pangera.  

## Personality
Shahar was seen as an inspiring and strong-willed individual who, despite her upbringing as the daughter of two Archangels, saw the corruption and the true nature of the Imperial Senate. She held onto her beliefs till the every end of her life, including the theory that Sandriel was the murderer of her parents. As such, many were able to side with her and saw her as a way to free themselves from the strict hierarchies that oppress the inhabitants of [[Midgard]]. 

## Physical Description
Shahar and Sandriel looked nearly identical. Thus it can be presumed that Shahar had the same brown eyes, full lips, thin arms, and dark hair as Sandriel. She also had the pristine white wings that all Archangels do.

## Powers & Abilities
### Powers
* '''Malakim Physiology''': Shahar had enhanced physical abilities and senses that made her far stronger than both humans and Vanir. She also possessed regenerative abilities that were far superior to humans and Vanir. As an Archangel, Shahar had stronger powers and abilities than other Malakim.
* '''Flight''': Through her Malakim wings, Shahar could fly at high speeds. She was also capable of carrying people in her arms while flying. Her wings also acted like a shield to protect her from some attacks.
* '''Wind Magic''': Shahar possibly possessed the ability to create, use, and manipulate wind magically much like Sandriel. Due to her Archangel status, this ability can be presumed to have been highly destructive.
* '''Raw Power''': Like all Archangels, Shahar possessed a form of raw power.
* '''Immortality''': Having made the [[Drop]], Shahar was immortal which not only granted her a slowed and expanded lifespan, but allowed her to survive injuries that not even her accelerated healing would be able to regenerate from.

### Abilities
* '''Combat Skills''': Shahar had been trained in various forms of combat and self-defense to an exceedingly high level. 

## Relationships
### [[Hunt Athalar]]
Hunt and Shahar were lovers, even though he later realized that he and Shahar had never actually told each other that they loved each other. Hunt recalls how Shahar had always let him talk out various strategies or issues. She listened, asked questions, and challenged him.

## Trivia
* In ancient Semetic mythology, Shahar is a god of the dawn. The name Shahar is a cognate of ‎שָׁ֫חַר (''shahar''), which means dawn in Hebrew.
