'''Sigrid Fendyr''' is a Wolf [[Shifter]] and the lost [[Fendyr Family|Fendyr]] heir.

## Biography
### Early Life
Not much is known about Sigrid's early life. While her [[Lars Fendyr|father]] had been Prime Apparent long ago, his [[Sabine Fendyr|sister]] had challenged him and won. After that, he was sent into exile and because Sigrid's parents had no pack, they roamed the tundra of [[Nena]] with her and her ten siblings. They never named her and they were only known to her as Mother and Father. Her gifts became apparent when she was three. By four, she was sold to [[the Astronomer]] and put into a [[Mystic]]'s tank. At some point, some official came by to mark her [[Drop]], though she is unable to recall how long it was ago or how old she was by that time.

###  
Sigrid is seen for the first time when [[Bryce Quinlan|Bryce]], [[Ithan Holstrom|Ithan]] and [[Tharion Ketos|Tharion]] come to visit the Astronomer and his mystics, as they want to know what happened to [[Connor Holstrom|Connor]]'s soul. She is one of the three Mystics and Ithan is imediately drawn to her presence. 

Later, [[Ithan Holstrom|Ithan]] comes back to deal with the Astronomer and smells that Sigrid is actually a Wolf. He offers to help her escape but she refuses. She does not remember her name, parents' name or age. Ithan ends up taking her with him.

Upon being freed from the Astronomer, Sigrid and the fire [[Sprite|sprites]] have become friends. Wherever Sigrid goes, [[Sasa]], [[Rithi]], and [[Malana]] go with her.

###  
[[Ithan Holstrom|Ithan]] is forced to fight Sigrid to the death, to free his friends from the [[The Viper Queen|Viper Queen]]. While tangled in a battle, Sigris makes an unexpected move, which causes Ithan's blow to her shoulder to go straight through her neck, decapitating her.  

Upon her death, Ithan's guilt causes him to swear alligence to the [[House of Flame and Shadow]] and search for a necromancer. He meets [[Jesiba Roga]], who gets [[Hypaxia Enador|Hypaxia]] to attempt to ressurrect her. Unfortunately, this doesn't go as planned and Sigrid becomes a [[Reaper|Reaper.]] 

Sigrid appears with [[the Astronomer]] when Ithan goes to visit the [[The Wolves Den|Den]]. Ithan attempts to tell the Wolf Pack that Sigrid is the lost Fendyr Heir. The Astronomer tells the crowd that Sigrid was killed by Ithan, making him a "kin killer." The Astronomer and Sabine were able to retreive Sigrid from the Under-King so she could live in the Den as the Prime Apparant. [[Fendyr Prime|The Prime]] appreaches, telling them that he had not approved this request. He then strips Sabine of her title, and names Ithan as his heir.

Sabine then kills the Prime, to which Sigrid feasts on his soul. She then turns to [[the Astronomer]], and punches her hand through his chest, pulling out his still beating heart. She then feasts on him too.

## Personality
Sigrid insists on seeing and knowing everything. She is not one to cower and instead generally full of irritation and disdain. She has a lupine's curiosity, but doesn't know or care about humor. Much of her personality is affected by the trauma she experienced as a Mystic - she can't stand being locked in or being in enclosed places. Like all Fendyrs, Sigrid is a force of nature - she's intense and cocky. Even just two days out of the tank, she already assumes the role of leader like a true Alpha, she expects to be answered and obeyed and as an Alpha, her voice carries steel in it that demands submission from other wolves.

## Physical Description
Sigrid has a thin body, long chestnut-brown hair and pale skin - the latter presumably caused due to her being submerged in a tank. [[Ithan Holstrom|Ithan]] describes her to have a face that might have been pretty, if it weren't so gaunt and haunted. She is bony from her time in the tank, has a lean frame and brown eyes.

Once she becomes a Reaper, she is described as having acid-green eyes and has maintained her thin frame.

## Powers & Abilities
* '''Shifter Physiology''': Sigrid should have standard enhanced speed, strength, and regenerative abilities because of her [[Vanir]] status. She should have more acute senses and should heal from injuries much faster than a regular human.
* '''Shifting''': As a shifter, Sigrid should possess the ability to transform into a magically enhanced animal form. Her animal form is that of a larger than average wolf. In this form, she should be stronger, faster, and have keener senses than in [[human]] form.
* '''Immortality''': Since she has made the Drop, Sigrid is immortal which not only grants her a slowed and expanded lifespan but allows her to survive injuries that not even her accelerated healing would be able to regenerate from.

## Trivia
* Her scent is that of snow and embers.

'''Silene''' was the youngest daughter of Queen [[Theia]].

## Biography
Silene was born to Queen Theia and High King [[Fionn]], only five years after her sister [[Helena]]. They grew up in the [[w:c:acourtofthornsandroses:Prythian|Fae Homeworld]] and while both were raised as royalty, none of them had the wish to rule. Their mother, however, had an appetite for power and went on to kill her husband, after he announced he wanted to give his throne to his eldest daughter to make space for a new generation. With Theia on the throne, she thought about her legacy and wished for both of her daughter to rule - nothing less than a whole world each. With the help of her High General, [[Pelias]], they crossed into a new world - [[Midgard]]. The [[Fae]] soon had to learn that the world was aleady inhabited and so Theia fought a war against them. They only learned later, that the [[Asteri]], their old masters, whom they had overthrown, helped Pelias to lure them into Midgard. While Theia decided to fight, she gave parts of the [[Dread Trove]] - the [[Luna's Horn|Horn]] and the [[The Harp|Harp]] - to her daughters, along with some part of her [[Starborn]] powers. Both Helena and Silene tried to flee, to bring the Horn and the Harp back into their home world and thus banning the Asteri from ever crossing into other worlds again, but ultimately Helena sent her sister through the portal alone and stayed back. Silene locked the Fae out of their homeworld, refusing to even take the children, and leaving them to the Asteri's cruelty in Midgard.

Back home alone, Silene took it upon herself to both erase their history from their lands, but also keep the story alive - even thouh hidden on the island she formerly called home. She used her powers to carve her mother's story into the stone, warded it against outsiders and then proceeded to hunt down the monsters the Asteri had created and trapped them on the island. 

After that, she found her mate - the son of the High Lord of the newly formed [[w:c:acourtofthornsandroses:Night Court|Night Court]] - and married him. Even though he wanted to make her his High Lady, she refused, having seen what power had done to her mother. When her son was born, she brought him to the caves of the island, and keyed the wards into his bood. Only when he was older, she told him what she had left to him. She watched her son and her grandchildren and their children in return grow up, fighting for life and spinning stories of her mother into legend, erasing both herself and her sister from it, to keep her world safe. 

## Personality
Silene never desired power or the throne. She loved her sister Helena more than anyone else.

## Physical Description
Silene looked like a High Fae female. She was beautiful. She had long black hair, clear blue eyes, pale skin, and a heart-shaped face. According to [[Azriel]], she looked like [[Rhysand]]'s sister.

## Powers & Abilities
### Powers
* '''Fae Physiology''': As a Fae, Silene had enhanced senses and physical abilities than [[humans]]. She also healed from injuries much faster than a human. Moments of emotional intensity temporarily heightened her powers and abilities.
*'''Starlight''': Silene inherited a third of her mother's Starborn powers. This gave her the ability to wield starlight. This is the light of a pure star. It seemed to have no other power beyond creating light itself and blinding others.
**'''Starfire''': Silene inherited a third of her mother's Starborn powers. This gave her the ability to wield starfire, which acted like a laser.
*'''Shadow Manipulation''': Silene could manipulate shadows. This included summoning and walking through them and cloaking herself and others in them.
*'''Telepathy''': Silene could speak to others mind-to-mind, also known as mind-speaking. However, she couldn't actually read their thoughts.

### Abilities
*'''Combat Skills''': Silene had been trained in various forms of combat and self-defense to an exceedingly high level.

## Trivia
* Silene was noted to have greatly resembled her descendant, [[Rhysand]]'s sister, as noted by [[Azriel]].

'''Sirius''' was one of the seven [[Asteri]] and the first one to be killed.

## Biography
During the [[First Wars]], Sirius was attacked and killed by [[Apollion]], one of the [[demon]] Princes of [[Hel]], which earned him the moniker "Star-Eater." Her death subsequently became the subject of a ballad, which had become a favorite around war-camp fires.

###  
Sirius was referred to when [[Rigelus]] is seen on the news giving a speech on new trade laws, with his fellow Asteri seated behind him on their thrones. It is mentioned that the seventh throne was, as always, left empty in honor of her.

## Powers & Abilities
* '''Firstlight and Secondlight Manipulation''': Like all the Asteri, Sirius could manipulate the power of [[firstlight]] and [[secondlight]] to achieve God-like feats. This resulted in the people of [[Midgard]] believing that the Asteri housed the power of a star within themselves.
* '''Energy Absorption''': Like all the Asteri, Sirius consumed firstlight and secondlight to increase her power to that of a god. However, it was alluded to that this power was not infinite and without regular consumption, her powers would have weakened over time.
* '''Body Jumping''': Like all the Asteri, Sirius could transfer her own essence into the body of others. But due to the nature of her power, the host body would eventually waste away from the toll.

## Trivia
* The name Sirius comes from the Greek word meaning "glowing." Sirius is the brightest star in the Canis Major constellation, and is the brightest star in the night sky.
* After her death, it is presumed that Sirius's remains were eaten by the Star-Eater, earning him that title.

'''Sofie Renast''' was a rebel who was dedicated to finding her [[Emile Renast|brother]] after he was taken prisoner of [[Kavalla]] when their parents had been killed. 

## Biography
### Early Life
Sofie didn't care to make the [[Drop]], but her parents had encouraged it. She'd eventually done it under the radar, in a back-alley, highly illegal [[Drop Center]], where a leering [[Satyr]] had been her [[Anchor]], and handing over her [[Firstlight]] had been the cost of the ritual. She spent the years following learning to wear her humanity like a cloak, inside and out.

Sofie was studying at a university - though it is unknown which - with a minor in theater. About three year prior to her first appearance, she was staying late at the library with her friends. Arriving home after midnight, she had spied the broken windows and shattered front door, the spray paint on the siding of their ordinary suburban house reading ''Rebel Shits'' and begun running. She was lucky that the [[dreadwolf]] guard posted at the front door hadn't seen her. Later, she'd managed to confirm that her parents were dead - tortured until the brutal end by either [[Lidia Cervos|the Hind]] or her elite squadron of dreadwolf interrogators.

She then spent months working her way up through [[Ophion]]'s hierarchy in order to attain a report that had revealed that her grandparents had been herded off upon reaching the [[Bracchus]] camp in the north, and were subsequently shot in a lineup of other elders, their bodies left to crumple into a mass grave. Still, she hadn't been able to find anything on Emile until much later. For years, she'd been working with the Ophion rebels in exchange for any snippet of information about him or her family. 

Sofie also had contact with [[Danika Fendyr]], who relayed information to her concerning the wrongdoings of the [[Asteri]]. At one point, she'd finally done enough for Ophion that they'd informed her [[Emile Renast|Emile]] had been sent to the [[Kavalla]] camp, and had survived against all odds.

Sofie then managed a plan to get caught - she walked into Kavalla and stumbled into the path of a dreadwolf patrol, who found the fake rebel documents she'd planted in her coat. Ophion knew about the mission, but they did not know about the information Sofie had gathered from Danika; she had wanted to make sure that, before she was picked up, Pippa and Ophion knew she'd acquired that intel so that they wouldn't back out of their promises to retrieve her and Emile from the camp.

She was interrogated her for two days before the patrol had thrown her into a cattle car with others, moving her to the camp. The reek of the days crammed into the cattle car had covered the telltale Vanir scent in her blood.

###  
Sofie had spent the last two weeks in Kavalla, with the single plan to free her brother Emile. Instead, Sofie found herself not only with her thirteen-year-old brother, but with another five boys and six girls that Emile had managed to rescue from the barracks, aided by a gentle human sun-priest, who had been serving as lookout during their escape. Together, Sofie and the children escaped from camp with the help of [[Cormac Donnall|Agent Silverbow]], and were intended to be picked up by Ophion at [[Servast]]. While Sofie was able to get the children to safety, [[Rigelus]] wanted Sofie's lightning to himself, intending to use it to resurrect the dead. Due to this, he had sent The Hind and more dreadwolves to hunt Sofie down. Sofie used her powers to kill several of the dreadwolves, but was unable to reach the [[Bodegraven|ship]] that would get her to safety. She was then shot by a sniper, which forced her to lag behind and inevitably become recaptured and interrogated. Due to her unwillingness to cooperate, she was pushed into the [[Haldren Sea]] by [[Mordoc]], and drowned in the process. Unknown to the others, the Hind was working with Ophion and threw a [[Comm-Crystal]] after Sofie, in hopes that they could hopefully rescue her, or at the very least, so that the crew of [[The Ocean Queen]]'s city-ship, ''[[The Depth Charger]]'', could pick up her body and put it into the ships morgue, preventing the Asteri from experimenting on her corpse.

## Personality
Sofie was incredibly determined, as seen in the years she spent at Ophion, simply to gather information about her abducted - and killed - family, never giving up in finding information about them. Still, she was also easily shocked by the things she did, with all of her missions weighing heavily on her until the end of her life.

## Physical Description
Not much is known about Sofie's appearance, but she had brown hair.

## Powers & Abilities
### Powers
* '''Slowed Aging/Immortality''': Since Sofie had made the [[Drop]], she would have aged slower.
* '''Thunderbird Power''': According to [[Pippa Spetsos]], Sofie had the strange gift of "not only electricity, but [[firstlight]] power, too; energy of any type was hers to command, to suck into herself." She used this power to absorb electricity from the electric fence surrounding Kavalla in order to escape the death camp. She also used her power to destroy a fountain and electrocute a group of hellhounds who were pursuing her through a large puddle of water. She was even able to absorb the firstlight used to power four [[Omega Ship]]s, rendering them inoperable and sinking into the ocean.

## Relationships
### [[Cormac Donnall]]
Cormac Donnall, whom Sofie knew primarily as Agent Silverbow (but preferred to call Silver), was her superior officer but later became her lover.

'''Solas''' is the god of light and the lover of [[Cthona]]. He blesses members of the [[House of Sky and Breath]].

## Biography
Solas is the sun god in [[Midgard]]. His [[mate]] is the earth goddess Cthona and his sister is the moon goddess [[Luna]]. Solas and Cthona's offspring is meant to be Midgard. His followers are Priests.

A holiday in Midgard, the Winter Solstice, is about the death and rebirth of Solas and his final embrace with Cthona before dying. The story of the Winter Solstice is that Solas's light is extinguished with the setting sun, with only a single kernel of him surviving. That kernel represents hope, which is fully rekindled with the rising of the sun at dawn. This marks Solas's rebirth from Cthona's dark embrace through the long night of her mourning vigil. People represent the single kernel of Solas by lighting one candle and placing it in their windowsill. The High Priest of Solas and the High Priestess of Cthona have their own "embrace" to reenact the return of Solas to Cthona's side. People rise at dawn to welcome the rebirth of Solas.

The Embrace is symbolized by a circle sinking, or rising, between two mountain peaks. It is simplified into a symbol of a circle nestled atop two triangles.

## Trivia
* In [[Oia]] stands a [[Solas's Temple|temple]] dedicatd to the god.
* A commonly invoked phrase in Midgard is "Burning Solas."

'''Tharion Ketos''' is a [[Mer]], who was Captain of Intelligence for [[The River Queen]].

## Biography
### Early Life
Tharion, after taking [[the River Queen's Daughter]]'s maidenhood, shared vows with the promise to marry her in the future. He is later seen to regret it and felt burdened by that decision.

Tharion and [[Hunt Athalar]] later jointly investigated a high profile murder case where one of the victims was Tharion's sister [[Lesia Ketos]]. The murderer turned out to be a rouge Panther [[Shifter]] who was killed by Tharion as revenge. This earned him a promotion in the ranks of [[the Blue Court]].
.
###  
Tharion helps in Hunt and [[Bryce Quinlan]]'s search for information regarding any unusual sightings near the [[House of Many Waters]]. They suspected that the [[Kristallos]] [[demon]] might be traveling through the sewer tunnels or dumping the bodies of its victims there. They asked Tharion to warn the River Queen about the demon. 

Tharion later sent photographs of one of the Kristallos demon's victim's bodies retrieved from [[sobek]]'s lair along with his notes on it and analysis reports as conducted by [[the Blue Court]]. 

Bryce, after visiting [[the Meat Market]], and hearing from [[the Viper Queen]] about the dealing of the drug [[Synth]] at the river, sent a message to Tharion asking to meet. Tharion revealed that the Synth was being sold on the river and that he had received intel of [[Danika Fendyr]] on an [[Auxiliary]] boat with a crate Synth a day before her death. However, he was not sure if she was selling it or buying it, or simply confiscating it on behalf of the Aux. He then informs Bryce that Synth is a highly dangerous [[Magic|magical]]-synthetic drug. It started as an aid for healing, but someone apparently realized that in super-concentrated doses, it can give [[humans]] strength greater than most [[Vanir]].

Tharion later sends a message to Bryce via the [[Otter]] Postal Service about a deal of Synth that was about to go down at the river. He took Bryce with him on his water skimmer to the boat where they discovered that Hunt, [[Viktoria Vargos]], and [[Justinian Gelos]] were buying Synth from the Viper Queen to gain enough power to overthrow [[Micah Domitus]] and the [[Asteri]] and free themselves of the halos. However, their plan failed as they found out the Viper Queen had double crossed them and had informed Micah of their plans in exchange for a favor. 

When Micah opened the portals to [[Hel]] through the city [[Gates]], Tharion had ordered his people to go to the surface and bring as many people below the waters in order to save them from the demons from Hel. Tharion also joined Hunt, [[Ruhn Danaan]], [[Amelie Ravenscroft]], [[Fury Axtar]], and [[Tristan Flynn]] as they head to [[Lunathion]] during the demon attack to save the people and Bryce.

###  
TBA

###  
TBA

## Personality
Tharion is a charming, fun, and confident male. He desires a better world, free from hierarchies and imbalances of power of the current world. Tharion loves just about everything Above. But nothing is greater than his love for what it feels like to be in the water.

## Physical Description
Tharion has jaw-length auburn hair and light brown eyes. The reddish-brown scales of his long and massive tail catch the light like burnished copper. Black stripes slash across his body continuing up his torso and arms, like some sort of aquatic tiger. He has long, sharp, grey taloned hands and the bare skin of his arms and chest are tanned.

## Powers & Abilities
* '''Mer Physiology''': Tharion has enhanced physical abilities and senses than humans because of his Vanir status. He has more acute senses and can heal from injuries much faster than a human.
* '''Shifting''': As a Mer, Tharion possesses the ability to transform into a merman form. His Mer form has scales, a tail, and gills. He must submerge himself in water at least once a day. If he spends too long in his human form, it will become permanent with his scales drying up and flaking away into dust and his gills shrinking to nothing.
* '''Water Manipulation''': As a Mer, Tharion can use and manipulate water. Tharion is powerful enough to summon water from the air. After taking the cure to rid his body of the Asteri parasite, Tharion's power level drastically increases.
* '''Immortality''': Having made the [[Drop]], Tharion is immortal which not only grants him a slowed and expanded lifespan but allows him to survive injuries that not even his accelerated healing would be able to regenerate from.

## Relationships
### [[Lesia Ketos]]
From the way Tharion acted after his sister's death and how often he thinks of her, it can be assumed that Tharion loved Lesia very much. He was devastated after Lesia's murder and hunted down and killed her murderer himself.

### [[Sathia Flynn]]
Sathia married Tharion out of convenience. Her family sought asylum from the Asteri in Avallen and her father allowed Morven to hold Sathia hostage until she wed. Morven and Lord Hawthorne wanted her to choose one of Morven's nephews, Seamus or Duncan, but she refused. Bryce and all her friends show up to Avallen, and seeing another person in a bad place, Tharion stepped up and offered to marry her, knowing that it would help both of them out of tough situation. Sathia agreed and they wed five minutes later.

'''The Ocean Queen''' is the current ruler of the oceans.

## Biography
TBA

## Personality
The Ocean Queen is stated to initially appear as nothing more than a vain, ruthless and self-absorbed ruler no different from the [[Asteri]]. However, it is later revealed that the Ocean Queen is more benevolent and kind than she lets on. 

As stated and admitted by the Ocean Queen, she opposed the Asteri and had been opposition to them since they first arrived in [[Midgard]], as the [[Mer]] was one of the original magical races native to Midgard. However, due to the Asteri's immense power and dominance, The Ocean Queen cooperates with them on the surface to protect her subjects, with the queen having admitted and implied that in the past, she tried to resist their rule, but was ultimately defeated and forced to comply with their reign. Despite this defeat however, the Ocean Queen, along with her [[The River Queen|sisters]], instead chooses to continue defying the Asteri by helping and supporting other rebel groups who oppose them, as shown where the Ocean Queen provided refuge and assistance to  [[Ophion]] rebels and sheltering humans who are on the run from the Asteri's forces in her court to protect them. The Ocean Queen personally leads some of these clandestine operations against the Asteri herself, having agreed to shelter [[Lidia Cervos]] when she faked her kidnapping in order to secretly give birth to her sons and agree to care for the twins in her city ships in exchange for Lidia's services as a spy and pass vital information on to Ophion and act as an operative to help their rebel activities.

As shown, the Ocean Queen puts the protection of her subjects first and foremost, with her complying with the Asteri's reign to avoid endangering them. When [[Bryce Quinlan]] returned from the original Fae Homeworld, the Ocean Queen immediately proposed that she use the power of the Horn to open a gateway back to that world as a refuge and grant passage to allow as many of her people to escape the Asteri's clutches. 

## Physical Description
The Ocean Queen has a luscious body, skin as pale as the belly of a fish; her angular eyes are as dark as a shark's. Her heart-shaped face is neither pretty nor plain, and the rosebud-shaped lips are the reddish pink of a snapper. She also has black locks and walks with a strange sort of lightness — like she is unused to being on solid ground.

## Powers & Abilities
* '''Shifting''': It seems like the Ocean Queen is able to shift in some form. She can change her size on command, as she first appeared as barely more than four feet tall, but only a few moments later grew almost as tall as [[Hunt Athalar]]. Furthermore, she can change the shape of her teeth into those of a shark.
*'''Immense Power''': The Ocean Queen possess immense power, as after the loss of the [[Secondlight]] power grid after the Asteri's deaths, Bryce theorized that the Ocean Queen began using her own reserves of power to continue powering her six city-ships.

## Trivia
* The Ocean Queen has six city-ships for her court, one of them being ''[[The Depth Charger]]''.

'''The Oracle''' is one of the seven Heads of [[Lunathion]] and has control over [[the Old Square]].

## Biography
### Early Life
The Oracle is a [[sphinx]], one of the rarest creatures on the earth. Like all other sphinxes, she has been called to the service of the gods. No one knows how old they are and no one knows her name, only her title: Oracle. She resides in her temple in [[the Oracle's Park]] in [[Lunathion]].

The [[Fae]] come to see her as the first of their two Great Rites to enter adulthood. It had once been done to foretell marriages and alliances. Today, it is more to get a feel for a child's career and whether they'd amount to anything. [[Ruhn Danaan]] visited her for his reading, and the Oracle told him that the royal bloodline would end with him. It was such a harrowing experience for Ruhn that he puked afterwards.

## Personality
TBA

## Physical Description
The Oracle is so beautiful she makes one forget what to do with their body. She possesses the body of a golden lioness. Golden wings lay folded against her slender body,shimmering as if crafted from molten metal. Her lion's tail is long and tufted with dark fur at the end, and her paws have large, sharp claws. Her face is that of a flawless, golden-haired [[human]] woman. She has large brown eyes with lashes that brush against her light brown cheeks, sensuous lips and a cruel smile. Her canine teeth are sharp enough to shred flesh. She moves with a fluid grace and speaks in an unearthly whisper when giving readings. Her voice is young, full of light and amusement, yet tinged with ancient cruelty.

## Powers & Abilities
* '''Prognostication''': The Oracle presumably can see the future, as it is her job to predict futures for all who visit her. She reads the future in smoke.
* '''Immortality''': As a sphinx, the Oracle is essentially immortal.

## Biography
## ==

## Personality
The River Queen believes herself to be a benevolent ruler who only wants the best for her people, but she is actually just as harsh as the [[Asteri]]. The River Queen seldom deigns to leave the Istros River. [[Tharion Ketos|Tharion]] describes her to be vain and bored, just like her sisters. However, despite her negative aspects, the River Queen was later revealed  to indeed have a kinder, more benevolent side to her. Like [[The Ocean Queen]], The River Queen opposes the Asteri, as the [[Mer]] is one of the original races from [[Midgard]], and considers them invaders and conquerors. However, due to the Asteri's immense power and dominance, the River Queen is aware she cannot defy them openly and as such bows to them to avoid endangering her subjects. Instead, she along with her sisters, and the Ocean Queen, quietly rebels against the Asteri's rule in secret, such as providing assistance to various rebels who oppose them. 

Her kindness and benevolence were fully shown when she agreed to [[Tharion Ketos]]'s request to shelter human refugees when the war against the Asteri began. With her accepting his request greatly shocking even her own daughter, who looked at her as if she didn't know her. 

## Physical Description
The River Queen has long dark hair and brown skin. She has heavy breasts, elegant fingers and slightly pointed white teeth.

## Powers & Abilities
The River Queen is rumored to summon far worse than the water [[magic]] of her people. She could possibly wash away all of Lunathion, if provoked.

Queen '''Theia''' was a [[Fae]] queen with the [[Starborn]] power, who fought against the [[Asteri]].

## Biography
### Early Life
Theia originated from the [[w:c:acourtofthornsandroses:Prythian|Fae Homeworld]], where she served the Asteri - though they were known as [[w:c:acourtofthornsandroses:Daglan|Daglan]]. Her servantship to a [[Vesperus|female Asteri]] was more that of a slave. At one point she started a rebellion with her secret lover, [[Fionn]], using the Asteri's own [[Dread Trove|weapons]] against them, which ended in their usurpation of power. Fionn then became High King and made Theia his Queen. Theia proceeded to claim the very island which had belonged to the Asteri she had served. The island was chosen for its natural defensive location and mists that kept it veiled from others. 
<!--Admin Note: Factcheking the following quote: Dusk, twilight was what the island was in its long buried heart, the island was said to have had a soul that blossomed under her care and nurtured by the court she built there. -->

After murdering her husband when he refused to pass his throne onto her before he died - naming his older daughter as next in line instead -, she set her sights on conquering other worlds like her former masters did. When the Asteri were defeated, they left behind some of their weapons, of which she used the [[Luna's Horn|Horn]] and the [[The Harp|Harp]] to make [[the Crossing]] with some of her people, thus entering [[Midgard]] from her island.

She was subsequently the first Starborn Fae in Midgard, with both of her two daughters, [[Helena]] and [[Silene]], later receiving the gift after a power transfer from their mother. Somewhere along the way, she and [[Aidas]] met, fell in love, and discovered that they were mates. She then fought alongside [[Hel]] against the Asteri to free Midgard, but was later killed by her daughter Helena's husband, Prince [[Pelias]], on the battlefield. 

## Personality
Theia was power hungry, ambitious, and ruthless, as seen when she killed her husband for the throne. She was driven by a desire to constantly have more power and rule more land.

## Physical Description
Theia was said to be golden-haired. She looked like a High Fae female. Her face was as cold as her Daglan mistress's. She was hard-faced and beautiful.

## Powers & Abilities
### Powers
* '''Fae Physiology''':  As a Fae, Theia had enhanced senses and physical abilities than [[humans]]. She also healed from injuries much faster than a human. Moments of emotional intensity temporarily heightened her powers and abilities.
*'''Starlight''': Theia was Starborn and had the ability to wield starlight. A light of a pure star - it presumably has no other power other than the light itself and to blind people.
**'''Starfire''': Theia had the ability to wield starfire, which acted like a laser.
*'''Telepathy''': Theia could speak to others mind-to-mind, also known as mind-speaking. However, she couldn't actually read other's thoughts.
*'''Immortality:''' Theia had a slowed and expanded lifespan and was able to survive injuries that not even her accelerated healing would be able to regenerate from.

### Abilities
*'''Combat''': As she fought against the Asteri, it can be assumed that Theia was a skilled warrior.

## Trivia
* Theia was the owner of the [[Starsword]] before it was stolen from her by Pelias after her death.
* She was referred to as "Aidas's great love" by [[Apollion]] in ''House of Sky and Breath''.
* [[Bryce Quinlan]] bears her Starborn light.
<!--Admin Note: The following points were theories, which have been proven to be correct. Because of this, they don't have to appear under Trivia anymore, but are kept here for the later usage of links.

*Based on evidence from {{HOSAB}} and ''[[w:c:acourtofthornsandroses:A Court of Silver Flames|A Court of Silver Flames]]'', it is likely that both Theia and Pelias originally hailed from [[w:c:acourtofthornsandroses:Prythian|Prythian]] and might have been the wife and general to High King Fionn. They might have come through one of the rifts into Midgard using [[Luna's Horn]], which could be the fourth Cauldron-made object. Theia's descendent, Bryce, went back through the rift to Prythian by using the Horn which is now tattooed on her back.
**Since the Starsword originates from Prythian, Theia was the wife to the first and only High King of Prythian, [[w:c:acourtofthornsandroses:Fionn|Fionn]], who was killed by his wife Queen Theia and her general Pelias. Fionn was the last known person to be in possession of Gwydion before the sword vanished for over a millennium. -->

'''Thorne''' was [[Danika Fendyr]]'s Omega and part of [[the Pack of Devils]].

## Biography
###  
Thorne, along with the rest of the Pack of Devils, is at [[Danika and Bryce's Apartment|Danika and Bryce's apartment]] after going to [[Ithan Holstrom]]'s [[sunball]] game for Pack Night.

[[Micah Domitus]] hatched a plan to get revenge on Danika and to try and find the [[Luna's Horn|Horn]]. He waited until [[Philip Briggs]], a [[human]] rebel, was released from prison so he could frame him for the pack's deaths. He went to Danika and Bryce's apartment where the Pack of Devils, including Thorne, were at the time and used his [[magic]] to disable them. He then injected Danika with [[Synth]], which caused Danika to rip her pack apart before turning on herself. Micah then summoned a [[Kristallos]] [[demon]] to cover his traces. Bryce comes home to the massacre and recognizes what remains of Thorne was near Danika's remains, clearly protecting her until the end.

Philip Briggs was then accused of their murders due to him being imprisoned by Danika previously, which led the authorities to believe he did it for revenge. However, Micah admitted to [[Bryce Quinlan|Bryce]] that he was the one to kill Nathalie and the pack.

## Personality
Thorne was the only member of the Pack of Devils who understood how meddlesome the pack could be, and who cared as much about Danika's moods and Sabine's ruthlessness as Bryce. He greatly enjoyed sports and closely followed his favorite teams. Thorne was generally the voice of reason in the pack.

## Physical Description
Thorne had blue eyes and was very tall with a muscular build. He was handsome. He had a smile aimed to disarm and calm tensions.

## Powers & Abilities
### Powers
* '''Shifter Physiology''': Thorne had enhanced speed, strength, and healing abilities because of his Vanir status. He had more acute senses and could heal from injuries much faster than a human. He could effortlessly change between his human and animal forms.
* '''Shifting''': Thorne possessed the ability to transform into a magically enhanced animal form. His animal form was that of a larger than average wolf. In this form he was stronger, faster, and had keener senses than in his human form.

### Abilities
* '''Combat Skills''': Thorne had been trained in various forms of combat and self-defense to an exceedingly high level as a member of the Aux.

## Relationships
### [[Danika Fendyr]]
It was a long shot for an Omega like Thorne to ever be noticed by an Alpha like Danika. There seemed to be a gravitational pull that happened whenever Danika and Thorne were in a room together, like they were two stars orbiting each other. Thorne panted after Danika, who never seemed to be bothered by it until right before she died.

## Trivia
* [[Connor Holstrom|Connor]] and Thorne were supposed to [[anchor]] Danika and [[Bryce Quinlan|Bryce]] during their [[Drop]].

Lord '''Tristan Flynn''', more commonly know as '''Flynn''', is best friends with [[Ruhn Danaan]] and [[Declan Emmet]].

## Biography
### Early Life
Tristan is the oldest child of [[Padraig Flynn]], better known as Lord Hawthorne, and his wife [[Lady Hawthorne]], and the older brother of [[Sathia Flynn]]. He grew up in [[the Hawthorne Villa]] in [[Five Roses]] in [[Lunathion]]. Tristan and Ruhn became friends because they both hate the hierarchy and stifling roles of the [[Fae]] nobility. Tristan and Declan have been inseparable since birth. Tristan will one day inherit his father's title of Lord Hawthorne. He has spent every possible moment of the last several decades trying to forget this. 

About fifty years ago, Tristan went through his [[Ordeal]] with Ruhn and Declan. Afterwards, Tristan made the [[Drop]] and then [[Anchor|Anchored]] Ruhn during his. Tristan works as a member of Lunathion's [[Auxiliary]].

###  
TBA

## Personality
Tristan appears to be nothing more than a rich playboy, but it is just an act. He pretends to be a partier and full of irreverence. He is incredibly charming and equally lethal. He normally has a carefree demeanor, but dreads the moment when he will succeed his father as Lord Hawthorne. He doesn't look forward to the centuries of responsibilities the title will involve. [[Bryce Quinlan]] claims Declan and Tristan are the biggest gossips in the city.

## Physical Description
Tristan has golden-brown skin, short chestnut brown hair, and brown eyes. He is said to be the spitting image of his father. Tristan has a charming, boyish smile, classic [[Fae]] beauty and considerable muscles.

## Powers and Abilities
### Powers
* '''Fae Physiology''': As one of the Fae, Flynn has enhanced senses and physical abilities, including a sense of smell much stronger than that of humans. He also heals from injuries much faster than a regular human.
* '''Earth Magic''': Flynn appears to have some form of earth magic that Ruhn states is "rare among the fire-prone Valbaran Fae." It may be specific to plants like manipulating vines to use as weapons.
* '''Immortality''': Since he has made the Drop, Tristan is immortal, which not only grants him a slowed and expanded lifespan but allows him to survive injuries that not even his accelerated healing would be able to regenerate from. 

### Abilities
* '''Combat''': Tristan is trained in various forms of combat and a skilled warrior. Since he has trained his entire life to be a lethal, efficient killer, he's as well-trained as any of the angels' elite fighters. 

## Relationships
### [[Ruhn Danaan]]
Tristan and Ruhn have been best friends almost their entire lives and lived together for fifty years and were often throwing parties, getting drunk and high. They work together at the Aux and went through their Ordeal together.

### [[Declan Emmet]]
Flynn and Declan have been best friends since childhood. Ruhn states have been inseparable since birth. He also mentions that anything Declan knows, he automatically shares with Flynn in a matter of seconds. They also went through their Ordeal together. 

### [[Bryce Quinlan]]
When Bryce was younger, she had a huge crush on Tristan and fantasied about marrying him. When Flynn found out Bryce was the bastard daughter of the Autumn King, he considered her his princess and treated her like a royal.

'''Urd''' is a goddess, although it is unknown whom she favors.

## Biography
Urd is a goddess and one of the five deities in [[Midgard]]. Urd guides all. She's the weaver of fate and destiny. Upon learning about Urd, [[Nesta Archeron|Nesta]] wonders if Urd is the Midgardian version of the Cauldron, as the [[Fae]] of Prythian say the Cauldron holds everyone's fates.

###  
[[Bryce Quinlan]] comes to believe that Urd has sent her to the [[w:c:acourtofthornsandroses:Prythian|Fae Homeworld]] to discover something and to acquire [[Truth-Teller]].

[[The Under-King]] mentions that in [[w:c:throneofglass:Erilea|his world]], Urd has a different name, even though she is not a goddess, but a force that governs worlds. He depicts her as a cauldron of life, brimming with the language of creation. In his old world, she is called ''Wyrd'', with Urd being a bastardized version of her true name.

## Trivia
* There is a [[Urd's Temple|temple]] dedicated to the goddess in [[Lunathion]]'s [[Moonwood]] quarter.
* Urðr (Old Norse "fate") was one of the three Norns in Norse mythology.
* A phrase in Midgard is "Holy fucking Urd." Another is "Urd spare me."
* A warning against bad luck is to not "question the hand Urd dealt you."

'''Vesperus''' was one of the [[Asteri]] who conquered the [[w:c:acourtofthornsandroses:Prythian|Fae Homeworld]].

## Biography
### Early Life
Vesperus was one of the Asteri that left their failing homeworld in search of another. They soon found one and inhabited it, starting to overpower the population and installing themselves as masters. Vesperus herself chose a small [[w:c:acourtofthornsandroses:The Prison|island]] to rule over. One of her subjects was [[Theia]], whom she raised from childhood on, who later stole the [[Dread Trove]] from the Asteri and helped rid her world of them. Vesperus wasn't killed during this rebellion. Instead she was tricked into a sarcophagus made of clear quartz in a chamber underneath her own palace, which would then become her prison. She was confined, yet lived on, not able to breathe, eat, or drink, but left in a half-slumber, starving.

###  
She remained in this state for fifteen thousand years, until she was freed by [[Bryce Quinlan]] who was accompanied by [[Azriel]] and [[Nesta Archeron]].

Vesperus explains to the trio that locking her away was a greater punishment than death, for she had been alive but confined for eons. Despite Bryce threatening Vesperus, the Asteri refused to turn against her people and reveal how they could be stopped and killed. Vesperus asks to be set free in the [https://acourtofthornsandroses.fandom.com/wiki/The_Middle Middle], where unwanted creatures live. Bryce says that's not her choice to make.

Vesperus explains that there are hidden pockets of power throughout the lands, hinting that they might be under [https://acourtofthornsandroses.fandom.com/wiki/Sacred_Mountain the Sacred Mountains]. She explains that the Asteri's light thrives in such environments, where the veil between worlds is thin and magic naturally abounds. Bryce quickly realizes that Vesperus had been talking to distract them while she absorbed the core of firstlight that existed under their feet and they quickly start fighting. Using [[Starsword|Gwydion]] and the [[Truth-Teller]], Bryce is able to weaken Vesperus enough that Nesta can behead her with [https://acourtofthornsandroses.fandom.com/wiki/Ataraxia Ataraxia].

With her, the last Asteri left in this particular world died.

## Personality
TBA

## Physical Description
Vesperus' last vessel had black hair, which fell onto the floor, and extremely pale skin that glowed like a full moon. Her lips were an unnatural vibrant red and her eyes were a crushing blue that glowed.

## Powers & Abilities
* '''Firstlight and Secondlight Manipulation''': Like all the Asteri, Vesperus could manipulate the power of [[firstlight]] and [[secondlight]] to achieve God-like feats.
* '''Energy Absorption''': Like all the Asteri, Vesperus consumed firstlight and secondlight to increase her power to that of a god. However, it was alluded to that this power was not infinite and without regular consumption, her powers would have weakened over time.
* '''Body Jumping''': Like all the Asteri, Vesperus could transfer her own essence into the body of others. But due to the nature of her power, the host body would eventually waste away from the toll.

## Trivia
* The meaning of the Latin word vesperus is "evening."
* When Bryce had unwittingly awakened Vesperus, she asked if she was related to [[Hesperus]] in some way, referring to Hesperus by her title as the Evening Star, and Vesperus reacted angerly and claimed that she was the Evening Star instead. This might hint to a possibly strained relationship with her fellow Asteri.

'''Viktoria Vargos''' is a [[Wraith]], who was formerly working in [[the 33rd Legion]] under [[Isaiah Tiberian]].

## Biography
### Early Life
Viktoria was one of the few non-[[Malakh|malakim]] who had rebelled with [[Shahar]]. She was given to [[Micah Domitus]] soon after the rebellion failed, and her punishment went beyond the "halo" tattoo and slave markings all the [[The Fallen|Fallen]] received. Micah's punishment for her rebellion was to trap her in her current body forever. She has never disclosed how she had acquired her current body or who it had once belonged to.

###  
Viktoria attempted to question [[Bryce Quinlan]] the night of [[Danika Fendyr]] and [[the Pack of Devils]]' murder, but was unsuccessful. Instead, she went through the alibi footage [[Hunt Athalar]] and Bryce got from [[the Viper Queen]], with Vik confirming the alibi.

Viktoria's essence was ripped from her body and shoved into a glass box after she was found to be conspiring with Hunt and [[Justinian Gelos]] against Micah. The glass box was then put at the base of Justinian's crucifix, her essence invisible. She was forced to endure Justinian's crucified body as his blood dripped on the lid of her container. The box containing Viktoria's soul was dumped into the bottom of the sea to go insane from the isolation and darkness.

## Personality
Viktoria possessed an unnatural elegance, which is the only sign of the ancient power that ripples beneath her calm surface. Hunt likes her more than nearly anyone else in Micah's inner circle of warriors. He's liked her since their early days in [[the 18th Legion]]. Vik never pushes Hunt when he doesn't want to be bothered.

## Physical Description
Viktoria is incredibly beautiful and stunning. She usually makes people of all Houses do a double take. She has alabaster skin, pale green eyes, and dark hair. Her legs are long, she is graceful and wears immaculately tailored suits to show a front, using it as a sort of armor. A thorn "halo" is tattooed on her brow that binds and limits her powers. She is graceful. As a Wraith, Viktoria has no body of her own. She says she likes her current body's build and movement. As she is trapped in her current body, thin lines have started carving themselves around her eyes. A crease is now etched in her forehead above the tattoo's twining band of thorns.

## Powers & Abilities
### Powers
* '''Body Jumping''': As a Wraith, Viktoria can transfer her own soul into the body of others. This provides a form of immortality and an expanded lifespan. Due to Micah's punishment, Viktoria is unable to switch into a new body.
* '''Immortality''': Having made the [[Drop]], Viktoria is immortal which not only grants her a slowed and expanded lifespan but allows her to survive injuries that not even her accelerated healing would be able to regenerate from.

### Abilities
* '''Combat Skills''': Viktoria has been trained in various forms of combat and self-defense to an exceedingly high level. This includes training in various types of weapons. Even with her power limited to a tenth of its full strength due to the "halo" tattoo, her skills as a warrior are formidable.

'''Avallen''', also known as "The Summerlands," is the sacred [[Fae]] island located off the shore of [[Valbara]] and [[Pangera]]. 

## History
Not much is known about Avallen, besides that it houses a High King, [[Cormac Donnall]]'s father. Concerning the Fae, the Avallen Fae and the Pangeran Fae are known to be rivals. Avallen is surrounded by mists that protect the island. Not even the [[Asteri]] could pierce them, thus Avallen has been left to mostly govern itself. Its ways are hidden from the modern world. One must be invited to cross the mists by the High King or Queen. The Avallen Fae are particularly patriarchal and misogynist. Females are generally valued only for the sons they can bear. The [[Avallen Archives]] are not open to females and they can't go past the lobby. The Avallen Fae are known for their shadow [[magic]]. It is the power to summon shadows or mist that can not only veil the physical world, but the mind as well. They can also have telepathy.

'''Bryce's Apartment''' is located in one of the apartment buildings for the wealthier citizens of [[Lunathion]] near [[the Old Square]]. 

## General Information
[[Danika Fendyr]] bought the apartment as an investment and leaves it to [[Bryce Quinlan]] when she dies. Everything is paid for by Danika's will.

The apartment building has doors made of glass and a glass-and-marble lobby. The building's entrance is arched glass that's structured like a fish's fin. The doorman is an ursine [[shifter]] named [[Marrin]]. There's a rear stairwell in the building that opens in a small alley. Bryce's apartment is one of the two penthouses on the fifth floor. A small cream-colored hallway connects the elevator to her apartment. A plush cobalt runner flows between her apartment and the one across the hall. A blackwood table is against the wall with a [[firstlight]] orb and a white orchid in a bowl on top. There's a key lock and a finger pad lock beside the doorknob. There are heavy locks and spells protecting the apartment.

The apartment has an open floor plan. [[Hunt Athalar|Hunt]] notes that it looks like it was decorated without sparing any expense. The apartment has pale wood floor, white plush furniture, matching white walls, all as pristine as the day Bryce moved in. There are massive floor-to-ceiling windows that make up with wall across the great room from the door. A light panel is beside the door. There is a white deep-cushioned couch in the right third of the great room, set before a reclaimed wood coffee table and the massive television atop a carved oak console. A fogged-glass dining table with white leather chairs takes up the left third of the space, and the center third of it goes to the kitchen. The kitchen has white cabinets, chrome appliances, and white marble counters. A kitchen island is in the middle. All of it is impeccably clean, soft, and welcoming. The apartment also has two bedrooms, each with their own en suites. A pale oak hallway leads to the bedrooms. Bryce also keeps her collection of the Starlight Fancy horses in her linen closet. She uses a diffuser that makes the apartment smell like lilac.

Hunt moves in to the apartment when he and Bryce are working to solve Danika and [[the Pack of Devils]]' murder.

## Trivia
* After being exiled from the Wolves, [[Ithan Holstrom]] temporarily moves in with Bryce and Hunt.
* Bryce keeps her Firebright 3500 scooter locked up in the alley out back.

The '''Cave of Princes''', also known as '''Helena's Cave''', is a cave system underneath [[Avallen]]. It is the resting place of [[Pelias]] and his descendants.

## History
The caves are nearly identical to the ones in [[Helena]]'s [[w:c:acourtofthornsandroses:Prythian|home-world]], which she grew up with. Since the location in [[Avallen]] is a thin place, it is ideal for traveling between or communicating with other worlds. Furthermore, the caves are made of black salt, which can be used for summoning [[demon]]s. She created the system as a means to communicate wth [[Hel]]. Since her communications had to be kept secret, she dedicated it to her husband, [[Pelias]], as a reason to keep coming there. She essentially built him a tomb which would last, and where after hi death the [[Starsword]] might lie forever until a worthy successor came along.

The [[Ordeal]]s of [[Ruhn Danaan]] - together with [[Declan Emmet]] and [[Tristan Flynn]] - as well as [[Cormac Donnall]] - with the help of [[Duncan Donnall]] and [[Seamus Donnall]] - took place in the Cave of Princes. Both Ruhn and Cormac were supposed to find and recover the Starsword, which Ruhn ended up doing.

The '''Central Business District''', also known as the '''CBD''', is one of the seven districts of [[Lunathion]]. It's the main territory of the [[Malakim]].

## Information
The CBD is located in the eastern part of Lunathion, bordered by the [[Asphodel Meadows]], [[the Meat Market]], and [[the Old Square]]. It's half a mile westward from the Old Square. The Business District is the center of commerce in Lunathion, and the most developed area. It's filled with skyscrapers and offices, as well as expensive high-rise apartments, exclusive restaurants, and clubs. The color associated with the CBD is opal, and its insignia is a pair of wings.

### Demographics
The CBD is home to the Malakim, represented by the Archangel [[Micah Domitus]] as both head of the district and Governor of Lunathion. Upon his death, [[Celestina]] is appointed Governor. The skies above the CBD are almost always filled with angels. Some other [[Vanir]] also make their home in the District. While the exact population of [[human]]s in the CBD is unknown, there are at least a few human-owned corporations in the area, such as [[Redner Industries]].

### The Comitium
At the heart of the Central Business District lies [[the Comitium]], a five-towered, sprawling complex of glass and steel that serves as the headquarters and stronghold for Micah and those who work for him. The second of the five buildings that make up the Comitium houses the barracks for [[the 33rd Legion]]. The Comitium also has luxurious living quarters for any guests of the Governor, including a penthouse ballroom for functions, all watched over by countless surveillance cameras and members of the 33rd. The central tower of the Comitium is known as the Governor's spire, and there are holding cells beneath the ground level.

'''Danika and Bryce's Apartment''' was the apartment that [[Danika Fendyr|Danika]] and [[Bryce Quinlan|Bryce]] shared together until Danika's death. It was located fifteen blocks from [[Griffin Antiquities]]. 

## General Information
The apartment was located in a walk-up apartment building in [[the Old Square]]. The [[Istros River]] was three blocks away and many restaurants line the apartment's lively block. Danika and Bryce's apartment was on the third level. The apartment building was old and the monitors in the hallways hadn't been repaired in decades. Danika could have afforded a better apartment for both her and Bryce. However, she knew Bryce well enough not to even suggest that she alone pay for one of the high-rise, glossy apartments by the river’s edge or in the [[Central Business District|CBD]]. So after graduation, they’d only looked at places Bryce could swing with her paycheck. The apartment they end up with was the least bad out of all of them. [[The Pack of Devils]] was always over at Danika and Bryce's apartment. Bryce and Danika even kept different beverages in the fridge for each pack member so that they were always accommodated for.

The iron front door was painted blue, and its warped edges would mean the door would often get stuck. The narrow kitchen had white plastic counters, teal cabinets, and two meager recessed orbs of [[firstlight]]. Bryce's room had a sagging green dresser and was connected to the bathroom. Her bedroom was cozy. Every wall was covered in posters of legendary performances at the [[Crescent City Ballet]]. A full-length mirror was propped against the wall.

[[Micah Domitus|Micah]] went to Danika and Bryce's apartment where the Pack of Devils were at the time and used his [[magic]] to disable them. He then injected Danika with [[Synth]], which caused Danika to rip her pack apart before turning on herself. Micah then summoned a [[kristallos]] [[Demons|demon]] to make it look like it killed the pack. He then blamed [[Philip Briggs]] for its summoning. Bryce moves out of the apartment after Danika is killed and moves into a better quality [[Bryce's Apartment|apartment]]. When Bryce goes back to her and Danika's shared apartment, she finds that someone has burned it down to destroy evidence.

A '''Drop Center''' describes the place where [[Vanir]] are able to make the [[Drop]].

## General Information
During the Drop, Vanir give off a flash of light, the so-called [[firstlight]]. This "donation" of firstlight by each citizen is a key element of the Drop ritual and part of why it is always done in a governmental Drop Center. A Drop Center is a sterile room where the firstlight from the person making the Drop is gobbled up during the transition into immortality and true power. It's all tracked by the Eleusian system, which is able to monitor every moment through vibrations in the world's [[magic]]. Family members sometimes watch the feeds in an adjacent room.

The '''Gates''' are seven gates throughout the city of [[Lunathion]], one of each positioned in the seven districts.

## General Information
The seven Gates are each set along a ley line running through Lunathion. They had been designed as a quick way for the guards in the districts to speak to each other centuries ago. People merely had to place a hand against the golden disk in the center of the pad and speak, the wielder's voice would travel to the other Gates, a gem lighting up with the district from which the voice originated. Today the Gates are used as a tourist attraction due to technology such as mobile phones. 

All the Gates are carved from enormous blocks of quartz hewn from the [[Laconian Mountains]] to the north of Lunathion. They were memorials for some conflict or war. They each have the same plaque that reads "The power shall always belong to those who give their lives to the city." Each Gate has a golden block that sticks out of one side. Jutting out of the quartz about four feet off the ground is a dial pad made of solid gold embedded with seven different gems, each for a different district of the city and the insignia of each district beneath it. 

## Known Gates
*[[The Angels' Gate]] — located in the [[Central Business District]]
*[[The Dead Gate]] — located in [[The Bone Quarter]]
*[[The Heart Gate]] — located in [[The Old Square]]
*[[The Merchants' Gate]] — located in [[The Meat Market]]
*[[The Mortal Gate]] — located in [[Asphodel Meadows]]
*[[The River Gate]] — located in [[Moonwood]]
*[[The Rose Gate]] — located in [[Five Roses]]

## Trivia

'''Griffin Antiquities''' was formerly located in [[the Old Square]], [[Lunathion]] and belonged to [[Jesiba Roga]].

## General Information
Griffin Antiquities dealt with various artifacts and also held [[the Great Library of Parthos]] with several irreplaceable books and personal collectibles from Jesiba herself. It had been located in the Old Square on a quiet street a block off the [[Istros River]], but within a day of the attack on the city, Jesiba had cleared out the gallery and the precious volumes that made up all that remained of the ancient Great Library of Parthos. Most of Jesiba's other pieces are now in a warehouse with many in crates. Jesiba moved the Parthos books, one of the few remnants of the [[human]] world before the [[Asteri]]'s arrival, to her office in the [[House of Flame and Shadow Headquarters]]. After [[Bryce Quinlan]] inherited them, she moved them to an unnamed place so that everyone might see them.

When Griffin Antiquities was located in the Old Square, it was in a slim building with unadorned, thick, sandstone walls that belied the latest tech and grade A spell work that kept it operational and preserved many of the books in the archives below it. No windows lined the sandstone façade of the slender two-story building. Only a bronze plaque to the right of the heavy iron door gave the name of the place. The words "Griffin Antiquities" were etched in archaic, bold lettering. The words were adorned with a set of glaring owl eyes. An intercom with a matching bronze button lay beneath. It had extensive surveillance equipment. A nearly invisible skintight enchantment coated the stone, mortar, and door. It was a cold, clever spell. 

Upon entering was a modest gallery showroom. A green carpeted staircase led down to the archives through a gaping archway with glass sconces lining the walls. [[Lehabah]] guarded the archives day and night. Plush pine-green carpets covered the floor from wall to wall in the two-story showroom. The walls were wood-paneled. Alcoves of soft-lit art displays dotted the edges of the room. There were chunks of ancient frescoes, paintings, and statues of strange and rare [[Vanir]]. In the center of the space was a large ironwood desk. On the desk was a phone, a computer, and a conical diffuser added by Bryce. There were small tables between the chairs in front of the desk. A brass button beneath a ledge on the desk lowered the wood panel in the wall behind the desk, revealing a large screen. A small wood door behind the desk led upstairs to Jesiba's office where a floor-to-ceiling internal window overlooked the showroom. The simple, but six-inch-thick iron, door to the right of the desk led down into another full level that is stocked with things no one is supposed to know about or find. The enchantments on these two doors were insanely intense. 

A tight, carpeted staircase leads up to Jesiba's office. The office is a small, plush space. One wall is a pane of glass that overlooks the gallery floor a level below, the material utterly soundproof. A rack of [[Firstlight|firstlights]] illuminate the dismantled [[Godslayer Rifle|gun]] hanging on the wall behind the wood desk. A safe is beside the painting on the right wall. 

In the archives were the library. There were tables and chairs throughout the space. The ceiling had been painted to look like the night sky. A giant gold chandelier hung down the center of the space as was fashioned after an exploding sun with all the other dangling lights in perfect alignment of the seven planets. [[Syrinx]] liked to sleep soon a green velvet couch. Lehabah's official perch was a glass dome. The library's walls were oak panels that looked old but were wired with the latest tech and best enchantments. There was also a killer sound system. Bookshelves lined every wall, interrupted only by a dozen or so small tanks and terrariums occupied by all manner of small common animals. The biggest tank was a massive one that occupied an entire wall. 

## Notable Locations
* Gallery Showroom
* [[Jesiba Roga]]'s Office
* [[The Great Library of Parthos|Library]] Archives

## Trivia
* For a short period of time a [[kelpie]] was owned by Jesiba and lived in the massive tank of the gallery's library until Bryce gave it to a client as a gift.

'''Hel''' is a planet on which the seven princes of Hel, as well as several other [[demons]], live.

## History
According to [[Aidas]], one of the princes of Hel, it is another planet. At one point, the [[Asteri]] tried to conquer the planet, but lost it in 17.001 [[A.E.]], only shortly before they found and conquered [[Midgard]] instead. The documentation of the Asteri describes Hel as a dark, cold world with mighty creatures of night. They saw through our lures. Once warring factions, the royal armies of Hel then united and marched against the Asteri. They were overwhelmed and abandoned the world, but Hel gave chase, as they had learned from the Asteri's captured lieutenants how to slip between the cracks in realms. They found the Asteri on Midgard a year later and tried to convince the [[humans]] of the Asteri's real plans - some fell to their charms. The [[First Wars|ensuring war]] lasted until nearly the end of 17.003 A.E., but in the end Hel was defeated and sent back. The Asteri determined them to be far too dangerous to allow them access to Midgard again, but assumed that they might try, as they had developed attachments to the [[Fae]] in particular.

There used to be an [[Northern Rift|open rift]] between Hel and Midgard which was closed, but [[Luna's Horn]] allows these rifts to open. There are seven levels of Hel, which are described as darker than the last: the [[Hollow]], the [[Trench]], the [[Canyon]], the [[Ravine]]. The fifth level is called the [[Chasm]]. The sixth level holds the Prince of the [[Abyss]], and the seventh level holds the Prince of the [[Pit]] or [[Apollion|the Star-Eater.]] The Prince of the Pit is so scary his name is not said by anyone on the Midgardian side of the rift between the two worlds.

While it is severed from Midgard by time and space, Hel is still accessible through the two rifts at the north and south poles of Midgard. Hel can also be accessed by summoning demons through salts of varying powers. The supposed benefit of using salt is that only one demon can be summoned at a time.

* The [[Ravine]] — governed by [[Thanatos]]
* The [[Chasm]] — governed by [[Aidas]]
* The [[Abyss]]
* The [[Pit]] — governed by [[Apollion]]

## Notable Residents
* [[Aidas]]
* [[Apollion]]
* [[Thanatos]]

## Trivia
* In Norse mythology, '''Hel''', the location, shares a name with Hel, a being who rules over the location. In late Icelandic sources, varying descriptions of Hel are given and various figures are described as being buried with items that will facilitate their journey to Hel after their death. In the Poetic Edda, Brynhildr's trip to Hel after her death is described and Odin, while alive, also visits Hel upon his horse Sleipnir. In Snorri Sturluson's Prose Edda, Baldr goes to Hel on his death and subsequently Hermóðr uses Sleipnir to attempt to retrieve him.
*As stated by Aidas, during the time of Hel’s second war on Midgard and against the Asteri, Four of the other Princes of Hel where fighting to free other planets.

page_title: House of Flame and Shadow Headquarters
content:
The '''House of Flame and Shadow Headquarters''' are the headquarters for all members of the [[House of Flame and Shadow]].

## General Information
The House of Flame and Shadow Headquarters is essentially a private club for the species of its House. It is located a block westward along the quay from the Black Dock in the [[The Bone Quarter|Bone Quarter]] of [[Lunathion]]. The entrance is a great black door covered in crude, ancient markings that sits in the middle of a matching black marble building that has been styled after an elaborate mausoleum. The headquarters are actually located below the building. No one guards the door, but no guard is needed. At the center of the entrance door is an etching of a horned, humanoid skull engulfed in flames made of metal. The door opens on its own at the command of the leadership of the House.

Through the entrance door is a dark hallway with a long, straight staircase. The walls are black and the air is tight and dry like a tomb. The stairwell ends at another door. Past the door is a subterranean hall of black stone lit by crackling fires in hearths shaped like roaring, fanged mouths. It's weirdly normal, like a private club. At the end of this long hall is a set of double doors of black wood carved with the horned skull sigil of the House. Through these doors is essentially a bar. There's a sleek, onyx glass bar lit with deep blue lightning with gilded stools before it. A jazz quartet plays on a small stage beneath an archway in the rear of the space. There are many high tables adorned with glass votives of blue light.

[[Jesiba Roga]]'s office is located in the headquarters. It is crammed full of crates and boxes. There's a massive black desk with a lamp and chair for guests. The desk chair is a tufted white velvet armchair. Built-in bookshelves are behind the desk. They contain three glass terrariums filled with various small creatures.

'''Luna's Temple''' is a building devoted to the moon goddess [[Luna]].

## General Information
The temple is also referred to as [[Lunathion]]'s temple as Luna is the city's patron goddess. It is located three blocks north of [[the Heart Gate]], the center of [[the Old Square]]. The temple was the first thing built upon the city's founding five hundred years ago. 

Luna's Temple is across the street from [[the Oracle's Park]]. It is made of white stone. It's one of the few places in the city without security cameras. A sprawling villa for the acolytes and priestesses lays behind the temple itself. Outside the entry doors is a courtyard lined with cypress trees, the courtyard itself is modeled after the one in the [[Eternal City]]. Entry pillars are on either side of the steps leading into the temple. The inner sanctum, accessible through towering bronze doors, is a long chamber with a lofty ceiling and marble pillars. [[Firstlight]] shimmers from a row of braziers. A small door leads to the chamber where the stag sacrifices are prepared to be burned in Luna's honor. 

The temple's statue of Luna was crafted from a single block of marble hewn from the famed [[Caliprian Quarries]] by the [[Melanthos River]] north of the city. The thirty-foot-high statue of Luna seated on a carved golden throne is located at the back of the inner sanctum. The goddess is lovingly rendered in shimmering moonstone. A silver tiara of a full moon held by two crescent moons graces her upswept curling hair. At her sandaled feet are twin wolves with a bowl of incense in between them. Across the back of throne is a bow of solid gold and a quiver full of silver arrows with diamond tips. They were donated by Archangel [[Micah Domitus]].

'''Lunathion''', otherwise known as '''Crescent City''', is a wild and dangerous city. It is located on [[Valbara]]'s southeastern coast, midway up [[the Hand]].

## History
Lunathion was built as a model of the ancient coastal city around the [[Rhagan Sea]], a near-exact replica that includes its sandstone walls, the arid climate, the olive groves, and little farms that line distant hills beyond the city borders to the north, and down to the great temple for a patron goddess in the very center. However, unlike those cities it's modeled after, Lunathion has been allowed to adapt.

[[Micah Domitus]], the Governor, was responsible for updating and modernizing the city. He saw Lunathion as a tribute to the old model but also as a place for the future to thrive. Streets lay in an orderly grid, not a tangle, and modern buildings jut up like lances, far surpassing the strict height codes of [[Pangera]].

For Lunathion's [[Luna's Temple|temple]], the city's patron goddess, [[Luna]], was crafted from a single block of marble hewn from the famed Caliprian Quarries by the Melanthos River up north. The temple was the first thing built upon the city's founding five hundred years ago. The location of the city was selected precisely because of the way the [[Istros River]] bends through the land in the shape of a crescent. This is why the city is sometimes referred to as Crescent City.

## General Information
The metropolis is ruled by seven Heads of the City. Each Head controls a specific part of the city. The governor, who is Head of the [[Central Business District]] is above the six others. The [[Fendyr Prime|Prime]] of the wolves is Head of [[Moonwood]], the [[Fae]] [[Einar Danaan|Autumn King]] is Head of [[Five Roses]], the [[The Under-King|Under-King]] is Head of the [[The Bone Quarter|Bone Quarter]], the [[The Viper Queen|Viper Queen]] is Head of the [[The Meat Market|Meat Market]], the [[The Oracle|Oracle]] is the Head of the [[The Old Square|Old Square]], and the [[The River Queen|River Queen]] represents the [[House of Many Waters]] and her [[The Blue Court|Blue Court]] of the Istros River. The [[humans]], who live in [[Asphodel Meadows]], have no Head and thus no voice in how the city is run. Security and law enforcement is carried out by the [[Auxiliary]] and the [[The 33rd Legion|33rd Legion]]. The city is covered in cameras, all with unparalleled visual and audio coverage. Even apartment buildings have mandatory monitors in the hallways.

There are seven [[Gates]] in the city, each set along a ley line running through Lunathion. They had been designed as a quick way for the guards in the districts to speak to each other centuries ago. People merely had to place a hand against the golden disk in the center of the pad and speak. The wielder's voice would travel to the other Gates, a gem lighting up with the district from which the voice originated. Today the Gates are used as a tourist attraction due to technology such as mobile phones.

'''Midgard''' is the planet on which the [[Crescent City series]] takes place.

## History
[[Midgard]] was originally only inhabited by [[humans]] and [[Mer]]. But on their travels to find a sustainable food source, the [[Asteri]] landed on Midgard in either in 17.001 [[A.E.]] or 17.002 A.E., and tried to conquer it. They invited various [[Vanir]], which came through the [[Northern Rift]], and allowed them to become inhabitants of Midgard. Unbeknownst to both Vanir and humans, Midgard is actually harboring stolen species from other worlds to feed the Asteri's hunger for [[magic]].

The Asteri then ended the previously used [[H.E.]], or Human Era, calendar after having conquered Midgard in 17.003 A.E. and started the [[V.E.]], or Vanir Era, calendar instead. This meant that the humans spent the next fifteen thousand years in a state of near-slavery as prey, food, and whores. However, in recent centuries, the [[Imperial Senate]] has granted humans more rights. There are currently ten [[Malakh|Archangels]] who rule the various territories of the Republic, all bearing the title of Governor and answering only to the Asteri. The entire planet is practically covered in cameras. The security cameras are so respelled and warded that they are bombproof.

There are various ruins across the world, such as at [[Morrah]] and the great palace at [[Altium]].

## Notable Locations
* [[Avallen]]: the sacred [[Fae]] island, located off the shore of Valbara and Pangera
* [[Pangera]]: the continent where the [[Eternal City]] is located
* [[Valbara]]: the continent where [[Lunathion]] is located

## Notable Inhabitants

* [[Ember Quinlan]]
* [[Hunt Athalar]]
* [[Micah Domitus]] †
* [[Ruhn Danaan]]
* [[The Autumn King]] †

## Trivia
* The other planets in Midgard's solar system are, from the outermost planet, Siph, Orestes, which means the warrior, Oden, Lakos, Thurr, Farya, with Sol, the Sun, in the middle. Midgard is located directly between Lakos and Thurr.
* It's common in Midgardian art to see the caption "''Et in Avallen ego''," which means "Even in Avallen, there I am."
* In Germanic cosmology, Midgard (an anglicized form of Old Norse Miðgarðr; Old English Middangeard, Old Saxon Middilgard, Old High German Mittilagart, and Gothic Midjun-gards; "middle yard") is the name for Earth (equivalent in meaning to the Greek term οἰκουμένη, "inhabited") inhabited by gods and known to humans in early Germanic cosmology. The Old Norse form plays a notable role in Norse cosmology.
* Despite the propaganda of the Asteri, Midgard also possess its own native magic, albeit, unlike in the Fae worlds, it was not as dominant. With it being said that there were already a few magical beings and species native to the world, but kept to themselves.
**The Ocean Queen has confirmed that the Mer and possibly the [[Elemental]], due to her and her sisters' [[River-Spirit]] heritage, were some of Midgard's original magical species, with the Mer living in the oceans prior to the Asteri and the Fae’s arrival and living relatively peacefully with the humans in the land.

'''Moonwood''' is an area located in the southeast of [[Lunathion]]. It's the main territory of the [[Shifter]]s and was formerly under the control of the [[Fendyr Prime|Prime]] of the Valbaran Wolves. 

## General Information
Moonwood is full of oak trees, sprawling parks, riverfront walkways, palatial villas, and complexes for [[Vanir]] and a few wealthy [[human]]s, peppered with the best schools and many of the fanciest restaurants in town. At its heart is [[the Wolves Den]]. The color associated with Moonwood is sapphire, and its insignia is a single oak tree. 

Moonwood is also home to a three-level shooting range. It caters to lethal and creative clientele. It occupies a converted warehouse that stretches four city blocks along the [[Istros River]]. It also has the only sniper-length gallery in the city.

The '''Northern Rift''' is the name of a portal on [[Midgard]].

## History
The Rift was first opened when [[Theia]] and her cohort arrived in Midgard from the [[w:c:acoutofthornsandroses:Prythian|Fae Homeworld]]. It is located in [[Nena]] and once was open continually, with a connection to [[Hel]]. During the final battle of the First Wars, [[Pelias]] and [[Apollion]] faced each other. The two of them fought for around three days until Apollion struck the fatal blow. But not before Pelias was able to summon all the [[Luna's Horn|Horn]]'s strength, and banished [[Thanatos]], his brethren, and their armies back to Hel. He sealed the Northern Rift forever — so only small cracks in it or summonings with salt could bring the [[Demon]]s over. There is now an entire wall circling the Northern Rift to prevent Hel's denizens from crossing into Midgard. It also keeps Midgardians from leaving. 

[[Bryce Quinlan]] used the Horn to open the Rift again, both to the Fae Homeworld and Hel to gain their help in the defeat of the [[Asteri]]. She then closed the Rift again.

'''Pangera''' is one of the continents of [[Midgard]] and is where the [[Eternal City]] is located.

## General Information
Pangera is a vast and sprawling territory across the [[Haldren Sea]] from [[Valbara]]. Besides the Eternal City, Pangera is the location of the [[Asteri]]'s death camps. Their most notorious one is [[Kavalla]]. The [[Bracchus]] is another death camp located in the north of Pangera where elders are sent to be executed and buried in mass graves. About twenty miles from Kavalla is the port city of [[Servast]]. [[Alcene]] is a small city two hours from Servast that serves as a vital depot along [[the Spine]]. The Pangeran Archangels have palaces and country villas where feasts turn into orgies that last for days while [[Human|humans]] and the [[Vanir]] poor suffer and starve mere feet away.

The landscape is mostly made up of dry hills with mountains near the shore. Olive groves are scattered throughout the land. Hilly groves turn into rocky mountains that ends at the glittering blue sea. The mountain's seaward side looks as if it has been shaved off. There's nothing but a straight, lethal plunge to the water five hundred feet below.

## History
The human [[Ophion|uprising]] started in Pangera forty years ago, but has remained confined to the northern reaches. In Valbara, it is referred to as "the Pangeran conflict." According to reports, the human resistance is prepared to wipe themselves out rather than submit to the Asteri and their "elected" [[Imperial Senate|Senate]]'s rule. For the last forty years, the war has raged Pangera, but the Asteri "won't let" the conflict spread to Valbara.
