'''Parthos''' was a [[human]] city on [[Midgard]]. It was destroyed during the [[First Wars]].

## General Information
Parthos was an enormous walled city built of white stone. A great human civilization once flourished within its walls. Midgard was originally populated by humans and only a handful of unusual creatures who mostly kept to themselves. Parthos was said to be more beautiful than any city currently on Midgard. It was adorned with elegant spires and columns, massive obelisks in the market squares, sparkling fountains, and complex networks of aqueducts. The humans living there were in relative peace.

[[The Great Library of Parthos]] was located at the edge of the city, overlooking the marshes to the north. It was a massive, columned complex of several buildings. It was not only a place for books, but housed several academies for various fields of study, including the arts, sciences, mathematics, and philosophy. It was a treasure trove of thousands of years' worth of learning. An amphitheater was in the center of the complex. It was where all the scholars and thinkers of the day holed up. 

Both the city and the library were destroyed during the First Wars, after the [[Asteri]] brought the various [[Vanir]] into the human world. Parthos was the human's final stronghold.

## Trivia
*[[Palania]], one of [[Hypaxia Enador]]'s undead tutors, taught mathematics and science at Parthos' academy.

'''Ruhn's House''' is located near [[the Old Square]].

## General Information
Ruhn shared his house with his best friends [[Declan Emmet]] and [[Tristan Flynn]]. They have been living in this house for twenty years. They often use it for massive parties, causing the inside of the house to be left in disarray. The house might not last another two years. After [[Ithan Holstrom]] was thrown out of his [[Black Rose Wolf Pack|pack]], he temporarily stayed at [[Bryce's Apartment|Bryce's apartment]], before accepting Ruhn's offer to come and live with him. The house itself is big enough to have four bedrooms, one for each of the inhabitants. After Ithan frees the fire [[sprite]]s enslaved to [[the Astronomer]], they stay in Ruhn's house as well.

The house is on one of the rougher streets in the Old Square. There's a front porch and lawn. The outside of the house is painted white. The front porch has white columns and the front door is painted green. The bedrooms are on the second floor. Ruhn's bedroom and Declan's bedroom are across the hall from each other, with Tristan's bedroom down the hall from both. Ruhn's bedroom has a large bed and a chipped wood dresser. His door is peppered with holes from his idle knife target practice. The dim upstairs hallway hides most of the various stains on the white paint. Connecting the first and second floors is a curving grand staircase. A crystal chandelier hangs from the ceiling in the foyer of the first floor. The first floor has worn oak flooring. A beer pong table occupies most of the foyer. Tristan painted an enormous Fae male head devouring an angel whole, only its frayed wings visible through the snapped-shut teeth on the top of the table. The living room is a long, cavernous space with a pool table in the rear of it and a worn gray carpet. A sectional couch is at the other end of the living room. A built-in bar is against the far wall. Through the living room is the kitchen, accessible through an archway.

'''The Autumn King's Villa''' is located in [[Five Roses]] and was the seat of [[Einar Danaan|The Autumn King]].

## General Information
The Autumn's King villa is a sprawling marble home. The hallways are made of white columns and soaring atriums with expanses of floor-to-ceiling windows. The walls have artfully concealed panels for tech. There's radiant flooring, automatic blinds, and air conditioning. The ground level contains a formal living room, the dining room, the Autumn King's study, the kitchen, a den, and a game room complete with a pool table and a shuffleboard table. The next level up contains ten guest rooms and the Autumn King's private suite which takes up a whole wing. [[Ruhn Danaan|Ruhn]]'s bedroom is through double doors at the end of the opposite wing. His room looks exactly how he left it upon moving out. Posters of rock bands hang on the walls, ticket stubs are taped to the mirror, and a desk is by the window. There's a massive four-poster bed with black silk sheets. The closet is full of black shirts, jeans, boots, knives, and swords. 

The kitchen is utilitarian and clearly built for a team of chefs. There's a massive wine cellar. The basement holds a gym, steam room, massage room, and a sauna in one wing while the other wing has an indoor lap pool, a screening room, and the security office. The guest room [[Bryce Quinlan|Bryce]] stays in, is on the second floor. It is an elegant but bland blend of marble and overstuffed furniture in shades of gray and white with floor-to-ceiling windows. 

The Autumn King's personal study is larger than most single-family homes. It's accessible through towering oak doors. Bookshelves rise two stories on every wall, crammed with tomes and artifacts, both old and new, magic and ordinary. A golden balcony bisects the rectangular space, accessible by either of the spiral staircases at the front and back, and heavy black velvet curtains can block the light from the tall windows on the left wall overlooking the interior courtyard of the villa, olive trees, and lavender beds. The study also contains four massive worktables and an orrery in the far back of the space: a working model of the seven planets, moons, and sun made from solid gold. There are red leather armchairs and a massive personal desk.

'''The Bone Quarter''', otherwise known as the '''Sleeping City''', is a district located in [[Lunathion]]. It was under control of [[The Under-King]] until his death.

## General Information
The Bone Quarter is where the spirits of the dead reside before going through [[the Dead Gate]]. It is one of the Quiet Realms. Black steps ring the foggy shore. It is surrounded by heavy mist that obscures its view from the rest of Lunathion. The mist shortly parts for [[Bryce Quinlan]], showing her a vision of the six remaining [[The Pack of Devils|Pack of Devils]] members standing in the Bone Quarter. The vision describes the land as a grassy shore with rolling serene hills beyond. It is said not to be a land of stone and gloom, but of light and green. However, Bryce discovers this to be a false description months later when she and [[Hunt Athalar|Hunt]] visit the Bone Quarter. The true description shows the Bone Quarter as having dry ground covered in mist, grayness, and silence. Marble and granite obelisks are rising like thick spears, many inscribed, but not with names, just strange symbols. The Black Dock ends at the [[Istros River]], which looks like a gray mirror. Even during rush hour, it's silent, empty, and wreathed in mist. Mourners visit here where they can sit on benches nearby. The color associated with the Bone Quarter is onyx, and its insignia is a set of skull and crossbones.

## General Information

She occasionally helped [[Ophion]] - as their own form of rebellion - and helped in picking up rebels to hide them.

'''The Great Library of Parthos''' was originally built by the [[humans]] several thousand years ago before it was destroyed around [[Timeline|15.000 years ago]]. Some of the books were rescued by a priestess and then located underneath [[Griffin Antiquities]].

## General Information
The Great Library was located at the edge of the city of [[Parthos]], overlooking the marshes to the north. It was a massive, columned complex of several buildings. It was not only a place for books, but housed several academies for various fields of study, including the arts, sciences, mathematics, and philosophy. It was a treasure trove of thousands of years' worth of learning. An amphitheater was in the center of the complex.

The Library of Parthos contains books on evolution, mathematics, and theories to disprove the superiority of the [[Vanir]] and [[Asteri]]. Some books are from philosophers who claimed there was existence on [[Midgard]] before the Asteri arrived. The Asteri banned the books in the library for people to read and own, so that the only existing copies should be in the [[Asteri Archives]]. Humans died for the books in the library to be kept safe from the Asteri. Nevertheless, the Asteri tortured the humans who defended the library. They were burned alive with the heretical works used as kindling. With all the knowledge of the ancient world, where there was no Vanir and no Asteri only humans. The Parthos books are "one of the few remnants of the human world before the Asteri's arrival." The books are [[Magic|magical]] and seem alive. A third of the books want to escape, kill someone, or eat them.

## History
Unknown to most, [[Jesiba Roga]] was a priestess at the original Library and when it was attacked by Vanir and Asteri, most of the Library was destroyed and the priestesses killed. Jesiba was able to rescue some of the books, boarded a ship, ''[[the Griffin]]'', to smuggle some of the books out. She slipped through the Vanir nets and sailed across the [[Haldren Sea]] and found safe harbor in [[Valbara]]. She was aided by the [[mer]] across the sea, at the behest of [[the Ocean Queen]]. But before she was able to reach the Sea, [[Apollion]] found the Griffin. He had heard about the doomed stand at Parthos, and the ships, and the priestesses burned with their books. He was curious about what might be so valuable to the humans that they were willing to die for it. He didn't understand when Jesiba told him it was no power beyond knowledge, no weapon beyond learning. He refused to believe her and instead cursed her for her impudence in denying him the truth. Her curse was to live, unchanging, until she showed him the true power of the books. 

Jesiba founded Griffin Antiquities and used it to store the Library underneath. However, the library has since had to be moved into a nondescript warehouse after [[Micah Domitus]] discovered its location.

## ==
After the death of Jesiba, former priestess and caretaker of the remaining collection during the Asteri's 15.000 year reign, [[Bryce Quinlan]] took over the library's protection. After defeating the Asteri, Bryce decided to bring the books of the Library forth and making them available to the world.

'''The Heart Gate''' is one of the seven [[Gates]] located throughout [[Lunathion]]. It is located in [[the Old Square]].

## General Information
Like all the Gates of the city, the Heart Gate is carved from an enormous block of quartz hewn from the Laconian Mountains to the north of Lunathion. The Gates were memorials for some conflict or war. They each have the same plaque that reads "The power shall always belong to those who give their lives to the city." Each Gate has a golden block that sticks out of one side. Jutting out of the quartz about four feet off the ground is a dial pad made of solid gold embedded with seven different gems, each for a different district of the city and the insignia of each district beneath it.

The Old Square Gate's gem is a ruby, and its insignia is a heart. Its quartz archway is as clear as a frozen pond. On Summer Solstice, when the sun lines up perfectly with the Gate, it fills the entire square with rainbows. It is a twenty-foot-high landmark. It is called the ''Heart Gate'' due to its location being the dead center of Lunathion, with the other six Gates located equidistant from it, each one opening onto a road out of the walled city. The dial pad that communicates with the other Gates is located on a golden block that sticks out of its southern side.

'''The High King's Castle''' was the former seat of power of [[Avallen]]. It is currently destroyed.

## General Information
The High King's castle lay a few miles inland of Avallen. The gray-stoned building perched on a cliff above a winding, also gray, river. It could be entered via the river, through a yawning cave at its base. In the throne room was a throne, seemingly crafted from a single set of antlers.  The outer walls were covered with small castle balconies from the rooms, overlooking the hilly countryside. Both the castle and its surroundings had an air of neglect.

## ==
When [[Bryce Quinlan]] and her entourage arrive in Avallen, they are brought straight to the castle on their [[Avallen Boats|boat]], where they are recieved by High King [[Morven Donnall]]. They also meet [[Duncan Donnall|Duncan]] and [[Seamus Donnall]], as well as [[Sathia Flynn]]. The latter was brought by her parents, who made it clear that she marry either of the Donnall princes. Instead, [[Tharion Ketos]] offers her his hand in marriage and, after agreeing, marries Sathia.

The group stays in the castle while both exploring the [[Cave of Princes]] and the [[Avallen Archives]], even though females are factually banned from visiting the latter. After the High King is killed and the magic of the land freed, the castle is destroyed. While the occupants and the town had been spared, vines and trees had burst through the High Kings's castle and turned it into rubble.

'''The Meat Market''' is a district located in the southwest of [[Lunathion|Crescent City]]. It is currently under the control of [[the Viper Queen]].

## History
The Meat Market is a labyrinth of secret, subterraneanly, interconnected warehouses that make up the blocks of the district. The Meat Market is linked to the Western Road which is one of the four main roads leading to and from Lunathion. The color associated with the Meat Market is tiger's-eye, and its insignia is a serpent.

The Meat Market has its own laws and methods of enforcement.  Even the Governor has no stationed legionaries in the district. It's an incredibly dangerous and lethal place. The Viper Queen has ruled the district for longer than anyone can remember. There are predators, both born and trained, who use the Meat Market as their personal hunting grounds. Half-[[human]] females scape a living in the Meat Market by selling their bodies. Occasional [[Vanir]] are enslaved and bound with so many enchantments there's no hope of escape. Only the wealthiest can afford to purchase a few hours with them. Most of these women do not live long enough to make the [[Drop]]. The Meat Market has the [[The Merchants' Gate|Merchants' Gate]] in it, which is one of the seven [[Gates]] in the city.

Entering the district is not known by any sign or marker, but by a shift in darkness. No lights are visible in the skies above the low brick buildings that are crammed together. The shadows are permanent, tucked into alleys. The streetlamp are mostly shattered and are never repaired. In the warrens below ground are three different levels just for flesh; mostly human, mostly living. Every fetish can be bought, and no taboo is too foul. The streets are made of cobblestones. The market is packed with ramshackle stalls, vendors, and food stands. Smoke drifts throughout, along with the tang of blood and spark of [[magic]]. Above it all against the far wall of the enormous space is a towering mosaic. The tiles were taken from an ancient temple in [[Pangera]] and restored and recreated. The mosaic depicts a cloaked and hooded figure of death, whose skeleton's face grins out from the cowl, a scythe in one hand and an hourglass in the other. Above its head are words crafted in the Republic's most ancient language, ''Memento Mori'', meaning "remember that you will die." A rickety set of stairs leads to the mezzanine flanking either side of the warehouse floor. Doors are stationed every few feet.

'''The Old Square''' is an area located in [[Lunathion]]. The Head of Old Square is [[the Oracle]].

## General Information
The Old Square is the area where [[Bryce Quinlan]] has chosen to live, as it was the most friendly towards half-breeds. That is partly because the Old Square is one of the poorer regions of the city, but it's also very touristic. [[Ruhn Danaan]] previously lived near Old Square, on one of the rougher streets, presumably to piss off his father. His unofficial office is above a dive bar. It's also where [[Jesiba Roga]]'s [[Griffin Antiquities|Gallery]] was located, on a quiet street a block off the [[Istros River]]. The Old Square Gate's insignia is a ruby with a heart inside it. Because of the Gate's location, it is often referred to as [[the Heart Gate]]. [[Crescent City University]] students are always out in the Old Square causing trouble on the weekends. It has narrow streets lined with iron lampposts. The [[Central Business District]] is half a mile westward.

[[The White Raven]], one of the most popular nightclubs in the city, lies in the center of the Old Square. Danika and Bryce's apartment was a fifteen-block walk from Griffin Antiquities, the Istros River was three blocks away. and the Pearl and Rose was a twenty-minute walk away. Archer Street is lined with dozens of clubs.

[[The Pack of Devils]] were responsible for patrolling Old Square, making sure tourists didn't get hurt or come across the wrong person. They made sure the bars, cafes, music hall, and shops stayed safe.

'''The Oracle's Temple''' is the seat of [[Lunathion]]'s [[The Oracle|Oracle]].

## General Information
The temple lies in the heart of [[the Oracle's Park]] in [[the Old Square]]. The temple itself is a black-stoned, domed, onyx building. The temple has twenty-foot doors. The inside is a black chamber, which reeks of sulfur and roasted meat, the former from the natural gases rising from the hole in the center of the space, the latter from the pile of bull bones smoldering atop the altar against the far wall, an offering to [[Ogenas]]. In the middle of the room is a hole in the ground, behind which the Oracle awaits her visitors. Braziers are on either side of the space.

'''The Viper Queen's Den''' is located in [[the Meat Market]] of [[Lunathion]] and is the seat of [[the Viper Queen]].

## General Information
The Viper Queen's office is a mess, barely better than the stockroom of a dive bar. A dented metal desk occupies most of the cramped space, a scratched purple chair behind it with tufts of stuffing poking out of the upper corner. A mess of papers are strewn atop the desk as well as a computer that's about twenty years out of date. A little statue of [[Luna]] is poised atop it. The pale green paint peels off the wall in half a dozen spots. A water stain is on the ceiling that's made worse by the thrumming fluorescent [[Firstlight|firstlights]]. Against one wall is an open shelving unit filled with everything from files to crates of liquor to discarded guns. On the opposite wall are stacked cardboard boxes taller than a [[human]]. Cheap aluminum chairs are provided for guests across from the desk. Sounds from the fighting pit rumble through the concrete floor.

###  
[[Bryce Quinlan]] and [[Hunt Athalar]] are brought to the Viper Queen's Den during their investigation of [[Danika Fendyr]]'s death. [[Maximus Tertian]] had also been murdered in the same manner as Danika on the outskirts of the Meat Market.

'''The White Raven''' is one of the most popular nightclubs in [[Lunathion]] and owned by [[Riso Sergatto]].

## General Information
### History
The White Raven is located in the heart of the [[The Old Square|Old Square]]. It was once a temple of pleasure. It has a white marble façade. It has been renovated so a building now encases the ruins, but the dance floor remains the original, ancient stones of some long-forgotten god's temple. The carved stone pillars throughout the temples also still stand from that time. To dance inside in the White Raven is to worship that nameless god that was once worshiped at the temple, who is hinted at in the age-worn carvings of [[Satyr|satyrs]] and [[Faun|fauns]] drinking and dancing and having sex amid grapevines. All show what it had once been when it was a temple. A few steps lead into the glass-enclosed courtyard, which is flanked on two sides by the temple's original stone walls. The entrance is a pair of enormous iron doors. Half-wolf, half-[[daemonaki]] work as bouncers. The White Raven has a VIP bar on the mezzanine, accessible by an illuminated golden staircase. The VIP bar has a glowing solid golden bar with twenty glass stools flanking it. The White Raven serves as a neutral space. Riso employs prostitutes, calling doing so "sacred." Riso claims that since the club lays on the ruins of a temple to pleasure, it is his duty to continue its traditions.

###  
The White Raven was the target for a bombing by [[Philip Briggs]] and his [[Keres|human rebels]]. Before they could execute their plan, they were apprehended by [[Danika Fendyr]] and [[the Pack of Devils]]. After breaking up with [[Reid Redner|Reid]], [[Bryce Quinlan|Bryce]] spends the night dancing, drinking, and doing drugs with [[Juniper Andromeda|Juniper]] and [[Fury Axtar|Fury]]. Bryce meets [[Maximus Tertian]] at the VIP bar to complete a business transaction for [[Jesiba Roga]]. Later, it was blown up by [[Micah Domitus|Micah]] while Bryce, [[Hunt Athalar|Hunt]], Juniper, and [[Ruhn Danaan|Ruhn]] were inside.

'''Valbara''' is the continent on [[Midgard]] where [[Lunathion]], also known as Crescent City, is located on.

## General Information
Valbara is located across the [[Haldren Sea]] from [[Pangera]] and is a key territory full of vital resources to the [[Asteri]].

Valbara has four great rivers, each ruled by a different court. There is the [[Istros River]], which is ruled by [[the Blue Court]], the [[Melanthos River]], which is ruled by [[the Black Court]], the [[Niveus River]], which is ruled by [[the White Court]], and the [[Rubellus River]], which is ruled by [[the Red Court]]. All the courts are ruled by sister queens and have long been rivals. They are each mighty and gifted with magic but also vain, ancient, and bored.

The '''Auxiliary''', known as the ''' Aux''' for short, is the law enforcement group in [[Lunathion]]. The current Commander of the Crescent City Auxiliary is [[Ruhn Danaan]].

## Information
The Auxiliary takes their orders from the City Heads. The Auxiliary is divided into units based on district and species and grows murkier from there. Predator-born [[Shifter|shifters]], such as the Wolves, do not have a choice in joining the Auxiliary. They are destined for certain Aux packs before they are even born, and they serve for their lifetime. The packs have mandatory nights off once a week. The wolves claim the shifter packs are the commanders of the Aux, but the [[Fae]] insist that they are in charge. It makes dividing and claiming responsibilities difficult.

The '''Black Rose Wolf Pack''' is a pack consisting of several wolf [[Shifter]]s under the Alpha [[Amelie Ravenscroft]].

## History
Prior to the murders of the entire [[The Pack of Devils|Pack of Devils]], the Black Rose Pack was largely unknown and eclipsed by the more popular, and effective, pack led by [[Danika Fendyr]]. In the years following the murders, however, the Black Rose Pack moved to occupy the position held by the Pack of Devils, taking on more responsibility to patrol the city and guard [[the Wolves Den]] in [[Moonwood]]. The '''Dreadwolves''' are a certain fraction in the [[Asteri]]'s guards.

## General Information
There is little information about the dreadwolves. It's likely that all of them are Wolf [[Shifter]]s. There are various operational areas for the dreadwolves. The most famous ones being the elite squadron of interrogators under the command of [[Mordoc]], who follows [[Lidia Cervos]]' orders. Still, there are also dreadwolves acting as guards in [[Kavalla]] or on normal patrol duty in the citites. They tend to wear pristine gray uniforms, with a black-and-white patch of a wolf's skull and crossbones adorning the uniform's left arm and the seven golden stars of the Asteri shining on a red patch above their hearts.

### Elite Squadron
The aforementioned elite squad under Lidia's orders, sometimes referred to as ''The Hind's private guard'', were the only ones who wore silver darts on their collars. They received one dart for every rebel spy hunted down and broken.

The '''Human Conscription Service''' describes the military service of [[Human|humans]] in [[Midgard]].

## General Information
The Human Conscription Service was started by the [[Asteri]] in response to the human [[Ophion|rebellion]]. Every human is a member of the [[peregrini]] class, as opposed to the [[Vanir]], who are full citizens and are members of the [[civitas]]. Every peregrini is required to serve in the [[Imperial Military]] for three years. The Asteri send them right to the rebel front, forcing them to kill the humans fighting for their freedom.

To preserve the brightest human minds in the Asteri's view, the Asteri offer a test to place out of the draft. Those who score the highest are deemed valuable enough not to serve.

## Trivia
* In order to get [[Bryce Quinlan]] exempt from serving, her mother [[Ember Quinlan]] made an arrangement with her father [[Einar Danaan|the Autumn King]] to get Bryce appointed to full civitas status.
* Ember took the test when she was sixteen and aced it, allowing her to skip the service. [[Randall Silago]] missed the cut off by a single point and was shipped to the front two weeks later.

The '''Imperial Military''' are the armed forces of [[Midgard]].

## General Information
Part of the Imperial Military are the [[human]] soldiers, who have to serve for three years after being drafted through the [[Human Conscription Service]]. [[Vanir]] also serve in the military, though presumably voluntarily as opposed to drafted. The [[Malakh|malakim]] at least serve in their respective legions both on land and in the air. Military service is mandatory for any non-[[Lower|Lowers]] in the [[peregrini]] class.

The military has a fleet consisting at least of several [[Omega Ship]]s, equipped with [[brimstone]] torpedoes and radars, that are programmed to pick up traces of [[firstlight]]. Furthermore, there are the [[Mech-Suits]], which formerly had to be manned by humans, but are now equpiied for Vanir pilots. The military also has various helicopters and vehicles.

## Trivia
* [[Declan Emmet]] once hacked into an imperial military database and saw the uncensored footage from the battlefields and camps.

'''Ophion''' is a rebel organization.

## General Information
The definite extent of the group operating in [[Midgard]] is unknown. It consists of several members in Command and possesses a special unit named [[Lightfall]], which was led by [[Pippa Spetsos]]. One of its offshoot groups is [[Keres]]. Their insignia is that of a golden sun bracketed by a gun and a blade. For the last forty years, the human rebellion has been engaged in resistance in [[Pangera]]. According to reports, the human resistance is prepared to wipe themselves out rather than submit to the [[Asteri]] and their "elected" [[Imperial Senate|Senate]]'s rule.

###  
[[Philip Briggs]], a leader of Keres, has found a loophole and is released from prison. He is blamed for [[Danika Fendyr]] and the [[The Pack of Devils|Pack of Devils]]' murder.

'''Redner Industries''' is a [[human]]-run company.

## Information
Redner Industries, owned by the Redner family, is a massive [[Magic|magi]]-tech company based in a towering skyscraper in the [[Central Business District]].

Their tech five decades ago allowed for a new era of linking the world, and occurred right in Redner Industries’ famed tech lab, and vaulted the company into unprecedented fortune. In the current area everyone wants a piece of Redner Industries. Even [[Micah Domitus]] was a major investor in its cutting-edge experiments.

Redner Industries created [[Synth]] in one of the their labs. The got investment to create the drug by telling investors it was a healing drug. The earliest trials were a success: with it, humans could heal faster than with any [[Witch|medwitch]] or [[Fae]] power for an hour. But later trials did not go according to plan. [[Vanir]] and humans go out of their minds when given it in high dosages. High dosages of Synth would make the person taking the it kill everyone around them and then tear themselves apart.

## Trivia

* [[Danika Fendyr]] did part-time security work for them.

'''The 33rd Legion''', or the '''33rd Imperial Legion''', is the personal army, or [[Triarii]], of the Governor of [[Lunathion]].

## General Information
The 33rd Legion are owned by whomever is the current Governor of Lunathion. They serve as the personal Guard for the Governor as well as fulfilling the wish of the Governor to keep peace in Lunathion. The 33rd Legion is a [[Malakh|Malakim]] unit of 3,000 legionaries.

The job of the 33rd Legion can range from patrolling to assassinating people that the Governor wants removed from the city. Publicly, they are supposed to protect tourists and provide safety. [[Bryce Quinlan]] says that they actually do more glowering and threatening than helping. The 33rd Legion's holding center is a sterile, subterranean processing center a few blocks from [[the Comitium]]. Most of the slaves in Crescent City are the warrior-angels in the 33rd Legion. They are identifiable by the SPQM slave tattoo on their wrists. Many of the 33rd fulfill their roles in the legion in the hopes of being granted their freedom.

'''The Pack of Devils''' was a pack consisting of several wolf [[Shifter]]s under the Alpha [[Danika Fendyr]]. The whole pack got annihilated in an attack.

## History
The Pack of Devils was one of the most elite shifter units in [[Lunathion]]'s [[Auxiliary]]. They patrolled [[the Old Square]], making sure tourists didn't get hurt or come across the wrong person. They also made sure the bars, cafes, music halls, and shops stayed safe. They claimed [[Bryce Quinlan]] as one of their own due to being Danika's best friend and roommate. They were the most feared and lethal of all the city's Auxiliary units.

The whole pack was killed off in an attack that was orchestrated by [[Micah Domitus]]. He wanted [[Luna's Horn]] so that he could summon an army to decimate the [[human]] [[Ophion|rebels]]. Micah blackmailed Danika into stealing the Horn. However, Danika did not trust Micah so did not give him the Horn after stealing it. 

Micah hatched a plan. He waited until [[Philip Briggs]], a human rebel, was released for prison so he could frame him for the pack's deaths. He went to [[Danika and Bryce's Apartment|Danika and Bryce's apartment]] where the Pack of Devils were at the time and used his [[magic]] to disable them. He then injected Danika with [[Synth]]. The Synth made Danika rip her pack apart before turning on herself. Micah then summoned a [[Kristallos]] [[demon]] to make it look like it killed the pack. Philip Briggs was then accused of their murders due to him being imprisoned by Danika previously which led the authorities to believe he did it for revenge. However, Micah admitted to Bryce that he was the one to kill Danika and the pack.
